26. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show 86ee64901 -- lib/transfer.c | head -100",
  "description": "Show what the transfer commit changed"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit 86ee64901f9a8e78d56b50f83e27d20691809764\nAuthor: Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\nDate:   Wed Feb 5 02:26:01 2025 +0100\n\n    transfer: replace `false` with `FALSE`\n    \n    Follow-up to c9afcecee94284e754d82354c79e4e37f09bff61 #16170\n    \n    Closes #16177\n\ndiff --git a/lib/transfer.c b/lib/transfer.c\nindex ba4616138..c4b23a8f0 100644\n--- a/lib/transfer.c\n+++ b/lib/transfer.c\n@@ -176,9 +176,9 @@ static bool xfer_recv_shutdown_started(struct Curl_easy *data)\n   int sockindex;\n \n   if(!data || !data->conn)\n-    return false;\n+    return FALSE;\n   if(data->conn->sockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\n-    return false;\n+    return FALSE;\n   sockindex = (data->conn->sockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\n   return Curl_shutdown_started(data, sockindex);\n }"
}
27. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show 03cf1c7b8 -- lib/base64.c | head -100",
  "description": "Show what the base64 commit changed"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit 03cf1c7b8c6617801e00e1d4851d0cdca1c1a4e7\nAuthor: Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\nDate:   Wed Apr 24 19:34:12 2024 +0200\n\n    lib: silence `-Wsign-conversion` in base64, strcase, mprintf\n    \n    Closes #13467\n\ndiff --git a/lib/base64.c b/lib/base64.c\nindex 919eb6235..8373115d2 100644\n--- a/lib/base64.c\n+++ b/lib/base64.c\n@@ -243,7 +243,7 @@ static CURLcode base64_encode(const char *table64,\n   *outptr = base64data;\n \n   /* Return the length of the new data */\n-  *outlen = output - base64data;\n+  *outlen = (size_t)(output - base64data);\n \n   return CURLE_OK;\n }"
}

Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace describe --tags 2>/dev/null || git -C /workspace log --oneline -3",
  "description": "Get repo version info"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "curl-8_12_0"
}
29. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace log --all --oneline -30",
  "description": "Check all git commits"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "4497dbd9a clang-tidy: fixes and improvements\n5fa5cb382 build: fix `-Wunused-macros` warnings, and related tidy-ups\n633ec719d curl_ntlm_core: merge two `#if` blocks\ndc08922a6 openssl: disable local keylog feature if built-in upstream\nf1c9d5e48 easy: reset errorbuf on eyeballing success\n36c2c7626 libcurl-security.md: Fix typos and add a point about URLs\n970e59a82 GHA/windows: mark 3023/3024 flaky\n8fb87f5a4 lib: delete unused local includes\ndaa6b27b4 include: avoid recursive macros\n1eec8b8d0 mprintf: rename internal enum to avoid collision with AmigaOS symbol\ndead29362 GHA/windows: mark test 3000 flaky too\na55f160b1 src: simplify declaring `curl_ca_embed`\n298f73f95 cmake: enable binutils ld workaround for all toolchains at build-time (revert)\n9e1e5ea67 curl_setup.h: update/expand `OPENSSL_SUPPRESS_DEPRECATED` comment\n849401219 examples/usercertinmem: use modern OpenSSL API, drop mentions of RSA\nd445f2d93 lib: skip compiling code with features disabled\n0649eb082 GHA/windows: bump cygwin/cygwin-install-action to v6.1\ncf9e445e0 cmake: normalize uppercase hex winver (for display)\n20ae14a32 cmake: warn for invalid `CURL_TARGET_WINDOWS_VERSION` values\nfb5541c28 GHA/windows: replace Cygwin distro server\n64d9f3543 fixup free the send token on errors\nfe276ff15 fixup merge two lines\ncd7cd5462 fixup white space\n45f2ed955 fixup return code\n207ab758f ws/tftp: include header file even when protocol disabled\n36dc50b7e smb: include arpa/inet.h for NonStop\n614abe111 fixup 'etbuf_size' may be used uninitialized\n57d5935f6 fixup oopses\n38a5098f8 socks_sspi: split Curl_SOCKS5_gssapi_negotiate into sub functions\n10bb489b2 schannel: refactor: reduce variable scopes, fix comment, fix indent"
}
30. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 200,
  "path": "lib/cookie.c",
  "repo": "github.com/curl/curl",
  "revision": "curl-8_12_0",
  "startLine": 1
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /***************************************************************************\\n2:  *                                  _   _ ____  _\\n3:  *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\\\| |\\n4:  *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\\n5:  *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\\n6:  *                             \\\\___|\\\\___/|_| \\\\_\\\\_____|\\n7:  *\\n8:  * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\\n9:  *\\n10:  * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\\n11:  * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\\n12:  * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\\n13:  *\\n14:  * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\\n15:  * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\\n16:  * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\\n17:  *\\n18:  * This software is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\\n19:  * KIND, either express or implied.\\n20:  *\\n21:  * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\\n22:  *\\n23:  ***************************************************************************/\\n24: \\n25: /***\\n26: \\n27: \\n28: RECEIVING COOKIE INFORMATION\\n29: ============================\\n30: \\n31: Curl_cookie_init()\\n32: \\n33:         Inits a cookie struct to store data in a local file. This is always\\n34:         called before any cookies are set.\\n35: \\n36: Curl_cookie_add()\\n37: \\n38:         Adds a cookie to the in-memory cookie jar.\\n39: \\n40: \\n41: SENDING COOKIE INFORMATION\\n42: ==========================\\n43: \\n44: Curl_cookie_getlist()\\n45: \\n46:         For a given host and path, return a linked list of cookies that\\n47:         the client should send to the server if used now. The secure\\n48:         boolean informs the cookie if a secure connection is achieved or\\n49:         not.\\n50: \\n51:         It shall only return cookies that have not expired.\\n52: \\n53: Example set of cookies:\\n54: \\n55:     Set-cookie: PRODUCTINFO=webxpress; domain=.fidelity.com; path=/; secure\\n56:     Set-cookie: PERSONALIZE=none;expires=Monday, 13-Jun-1988 03:04:55 GMT;\\n57:     domain=.fidelity.com; path=/ftgw; secure\\n58:     Set-cookie: FidHist=none;expires=Monday, 13-Jun-1988 03:04:55 GMT;\\n59:     domain=.fidelity.com; path=/; secure\\n60:     Set-cookie: FidOrder=none;expires=Monday, 13-Jun-1988 03:04:55 GMT;\\n61:     domain=.fidelity.com; path=/; secure\\n62:     Set-cookie: DisPend=none;expires=Monday, 13-Jun-1988 03:04:55 GMT;\\n63:     domain=.fidelity.com; path=/; secure\\n64:     Set-cookie: FidDis=none;expires=Monday, 13-Jun-1988 03:04:55 GMT;\\n65:     domain=.fidelity.com; path=/; secure\\n66:     Set-cookie:\\n67:     Session_Key@6791a9e0-901a-11d0-a1c8-9b012c88aa77=none;expires=Monday,\\n68:     13-Jun-1988 03:04:55 GMT; domain=.fidelity.com; path=/; secure\\n69: ****/\\n70: \\n71: \\n72: #include \\\"curl_setup.h\\\"\\n73: \\n74: #if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES)\\n75: \\n76: #include \\\"urldata.h\\\"\\n77: #include \\\"cookie.h\\\"\\n78: #include \\\"psl.h\\\"\\n79: #include \\\"strtok.h\\\"\\n80: #include \\\"sendf.h\\\"\\n81: #include \\\"slist.h\\\"\\n82: #include \\\"share.h\\\"\\n83: #include \\\"strtoofft.h\\\"\\n84: #include \\\"strcase.h\\\"\\n85: #include \\\"curl_get_line.h\\\"\\n86: #include \\\"curl_memrchr.h\\\"\\n87: #include \\\"parsedate.h\\\"\\n88: #include \\\"rename.h\\\"\\n89: #include \\\"fopen.h\\\"\\n90: #include \\\"strdup.h\\\"\\n91: #include \\\"llist.h\\\"\\n92: \\n93: /* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */\\n94: #include \\\"curl_printf.h\\\"\\n95: #include \\\"curl_memory.h\\\"\\n96: #include \\\"memdebug.h\\\"\\n97: \\n98: static void strstore(char **str, const char *newstr, size_t len);\\n99: \\n100: /* number of seconds in 400 days */\\n101: #define COOKIES_MAXAGE (400*24*3600)\\n102: \\n103: /* Make sure cookies never expire further away in time than 400 days into the\\n104:    future. (from RFC6265bis draft-19)\\n105: \\n106:    For the sake of easier testing, align the capped time to an even 60 second\\n107:    boundary.\\n108: */\\n109: static void cap_expires(time_t now, struct Cookie *co)\\n110: {\\n111:   if((TIME_T_MAX - COOKIES_MAXAGE - 30) > now) {\\n112:     timediff_t cap = now + COOKIES_MAXAGE;\\n113:     if(co->expires > cap) {\\n114:       cap += 30;\\n115:       co->expires = (cap/60)*60;\\n116:     }\\n117:   }\\n118: }\\n119: \\n120: static void freecookie(struct Cookie *co)\\n121: {\\n122:   free(co->domain);\\n123:   free(co->path);\\n124:   free(co->spath);\\n125:   free(co->name);\\n126:   free(co->value);\\n127:   free(co);\\n128: }\\n129: \\n130: static bool cookie_tailmatch(const char *cookie_domain,\\n131:                              size_t cookie_domain_len,\\n132:                              const char *hostname)\\n133: {\\n134:   size_t hostname_len = strlen(hostname);\\n135: \\n136:   if(hostname_len < cookie_domain_len)\\n137:     return FALSE;\\n138: \\n139:   if(!strncasecompare(cookie_domain,\\n140:                       hostname + hostname_len-cookie_domain_len,\\n141:                       cookie_domain_len))\\n142:     return FALSE;\\n143: \\n144:   /*\\n145:    * A lead char of cookie_domain is not '.'.\\n146:    * RFC6265 4.1.2.3. The Domain Attribute says:\\n147:    * For example, if the value of the Domain attribute is\\n148:    * \\\"example.com\\\", the user agent will include the cookie in the Cookie\\n149:    * header when making HTTP requests to example.com, www.example.com, and\\n150:    * www.corp.example.com.\\n151:    */\\n152:   if(hostname_len == cookie_domain_len)\\n153:     return TRUE;\\n154:   if('.' == *(hostname + hostname_len - cookie_domain_len - 1))\\n155:     return TRUE;\\n156:   return FALSE;\\n157: }\\n158: \\n159: /*\\n160:  * matching cookie path and URL path\\n161:  * RFC6265 5.1.4 Paths and Path-Match\\n162:  */\\n163: static bool pathmatch(const char *cookie_path, const char *request_uri)\\n164: {\\n165:   size_t cookie_path_len;\\n166:   size_t uri_path_len;\\n167:   char *uri_path = NULL;\\n168:   char *pos;\\n169:   bool ret = FALSE;\\n170: \\n171:   /* cookie_path must not have last '/' separator. ex: /sample */\\n172:   cookie_path_len = strlen(cookie_path);\\n173:   if(1 == cookie_path_len) {\\n174:     /* cookie_path must be '/' */\\n175:     return TRUE;\\n176:   }\\n177: \\n178:   uri_path = strdup(request_uri);\\n179:   if(!uri_path)\\n180:     return FALSE;\\n181:   pos = strchr(uri_path, '?');\\n182:   if(pos)\\n183:     *pos = 0x0;\\n184: \\n185:   /* #-fragments are already cut off! */\\n186:   if(0 == strlen(uri_path) || uri_path[0] != '/') {\\n187:     strstore(&uri_path, \\\"/\\\", 1);\\n188:     if(!uri_path)\\n189:       return FALSE;\\n190:   }\\n191: \\n192:   /*\\n193:    * here, RFC6265 5.1.4 says\\n194:    *  4. Output the characters of the uri-path from the first character up\\n195:    *     to, but not including, the right-most %x2F (\\\"/\\\").\\n196:    *  but URL path /hoge?fuga=xxx means /hoge/index.cgi?fuga=xxx in some site\\n197:    *  without redirect.\\n198:    *  Ignore this algorithm because /hoge is uri path for this case\\n199:    *  (uri path is not /).\\n200:    */\\n201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl@34cf9d54a46598c44938aa7598820484d7af7133/-/blob/lib/cookie.c%3FL1-200\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /***************************************************************************\n2:  *                                  _   _ ____  _\n3:  *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n4:  *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n5:  *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n6:  *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n7:  *\n8:  * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n9:  *\n10:  * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n11:  * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n12:  * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n13:  *\n14:  * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n15:  * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n16:  * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n17:  *\n18:  * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n19:  * KIND, either express or implied.\n20:  *\n21:  * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n22:  *\n23:  ***************************************************************************/\n24: \n25: /***\n26: \n27: \n28: RECEIVING COOKIE INFORMATION\n29: ============================\n30: \n31: Curl_cookie_init()\n32: \n33:         Inits a cookie struct to store data in a local file. This is always\n34:         called before any cookies are set.\n35: \n36: Curl_cookie_add()\n37: \n38:         Adds a cookie to the in-memory cookie jar.\n39: \n40: \n41: SENDING COOKIE INFORMATION\n42: ==========================\n43: \n44: Curl_cookie_getlist()\n45: \n46:         For a given host and path, return a linked list of cookies that\n47:         the client should send to the server if used now. The secure\n48:         boolean informs the cookie if a secure connection is achieved or\n49:         not.\n50: \n51:         It shall only return cookies that have not expired.\n52: \n53: Example set of cookies:\n54: \n55:     Set-cookie: PRODUCTINFO=webxpress; domain=.fidelity.com; path=/; secure\n56:     Set-cookie: PERSONALIZE=none;expires=Monday, 13-Jun-1988 03:04:55 GMT;\n57:     domain=.fidelity.com; path=/ftgw; secure\n58:     Set-cookie: FidHist=none;expires=Monday, 13-Jun-1988 03:04:55 GMT;\n59:     domain=.fidelity.com; path=/; secure\n60:     Set-cookie: FidOrder=none;expires=Monday, 13-Jun-1988 03:04:55 GMT;\n61:     domain=.fidelity.com; path=/; secure\n62:     Set-cookie: DisPend=none;expires=Monday, 13-Jun-1988 03:04:55 GMT;\n63:     domain=.fidelity.com; path=/; secure\n64:     Set-cookie: FidDis=none;expires=Monday, 13-Jun-1988 03:04:55 GMT;\n65:     domain=.fidelity.com; path=/; secure\n66:     Set-cookie:\n67:     Session_Key@6791a9e0-901a-11d0-a1c8-9b012c88aa77=none;expires=Monday,\n68:     13-Jun-1988 03:04:55 GMT; domain=.fidelity.com; path=/; secure\n69: ****/\n70: \n71: \n72: #include \"curl_setup.h\"\n73: \n74: #if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES)\n75: \n76: #include \"urldata.h\"\n77: #include \"cookie.h\"\n78: #include \"psl.h\"\n79: #include \"strtok.h\"\n80: #include \"sendf.h\"\n81: #include \"slist.h\"\n82: #include \"share.h\"\n83: #include \"strtoofft.h\"\n84: #include \"strcase.h\"\n85: #include \"curl_get_line.h\"\n86: #include \"curl_memrchr.h\"\n87: #include \"parsedate.h\"\n88: #include \"rename.h\"\n89: #include \"fopen.h\"\n90: #include \"strdup.h\"\n91: #include \"llist.h\"\n92: \n93: /* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */\n94: #include \"curl_printf.h\"\n95: #include \"curl_memory.h\"\n96: #include \"memdebug.h\"\n97: \n98: static void strstore(char **str, const char *newstr, size_t len);\n99: \n100: /* number of seconds in 400 days */\n101: #define COOKIES_MAXAGE (400*24*3600)\n102: \n103: /* Make sure cookies never expire further away in time than 400 days into the\n104:    future. (from RFC6265bis draft-19)\n105: \n106:    For the sake of easier testing, align the capped time to an even 60 second\n107:    boundary.\n108: */\n109: static void cap_expires(time_t now, struct Cookie *co)\n110: {\n111:   if((TIME_T_MAX - COOKIES_MAXAGE - 30) > now) {\n112:     timediff_t cap = now + COOKIES_MAXAGE;\n113:     if(co->expires > cap) {\n114:       cap += 30;\n115:       co->expires = (cap/60)*60;\n116:     }\n117:   }\n118: }\n119: \n120: static void freecookie(struct Cookie *co)\n121: {\n122:   free(co->domain);\n123:   free(co->path);\n124:   free(co->spath);\n125:   free(co->name);\n126:   free(co->value);\n127:   free(co);\n128: }\n129: \n130: static bool cookie_tailmatch(const char *cookie_domain,\n131:                              size_t cookie_domain_len,\n132:                              const char *hostname)\n133: {\n134:   size_t hostname_len = strlen(hostname);\n135: \n136:   if(hostname_len < cookie_domain_len)\n137:     return FALSE;\n138: \n139:   if(!strncasecompare(cookie_domain,\n140:                       hostname + hostname_len-cookie_domain_len,\n141:                       cookie_domain_len))\n142:     return FALSE;\n143: \n144:   /*\n145:    * A lead char of cookie_domain is not '.'.\n146:    * RFC6265 4.1.2.3. The Domain Attribute says:\n147:    * For example, if the value of the Domain attribute is\n148:    * \"example.com\", the user agent will include the cookie in the Cookie\n149:    * header when making HTTP requests to example.com, www.example.com, and\n150:    * www.corp.example.com.\n151:    */\n152:   if(hostname_len == cookie_domain_len)\n153:     return TRUE;\n154:   if('.' == *(hostname + hostname_len - cookie_domain_len - 1))\n155:     return TRUE;\n156:   return FALSE;\n157: }\n158: \n159: /*\n160:  * matching cookie path and URL path\n161:  * RFC6265 5.1.4 Paths and Path-Match\n162:  */\n163: static bool pathmatch(const char *cookie_path, const char *request_uri)\n164: {\n165:   size_t cookie_path_len;\n166:   size_t uri_path_len;\n167:   char *uri_path = NULL;\n168:   char *pos;\n169:   bool ret = FALSE;\n170: \n171:   /* cookie_path must not have last '/' separator. ex: /sample */\n172:   cookie_path_len = strlen(cookie_path);\n173:   if(1 == cookie_path_len) {\n174:     /* cookie_path must be '/' */\n175:     return TRUE;\n176:   }\n177: \n178:   uri_path = strdup(request_uri);\n179:   if(!uri_path)\n180:     return FALSE;\n181:   pos = strchr(uri_path, '?');\n182:   if(pos)\n183:     *pos = 0x0;\n184: \n185:   /* #-fragments are already cut off! */\n186:   if(0 == strlen(uri_path) || uri_path[0] != '/') {\n187:     strstore(&uri_path, \"/\", 1);\n188:     if(!uri_path)\n189:       return FALSE;\n190:   }\n191: \n192:   /*\n193:    * here, RFC6265 5.1.4 says\n194:    *  4. Output the characters of the uri-path from the first character up\n195:    *     to, but not including, the right-most %x2F (\"/\").\n196:    *  but URL path /hoge?fuga=xxx means /hoge/index.cgi?fuga=xxx in some site\n197:    *  without redirect.\n198:    *  Ignore this algorithm because /hoge is uri path for this case\n199:    *  (uri path is not /).\n200:    */\n201: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl@34cf9d54a46598c44938aa7598820484d7af7133/-/blob/lib/cookie.c%3FL1-200"
  }
}

WARRANTY OF ANY\\n19:  * KIND, either express or implied.\\n20:  *\\n21:  * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\\n22:  *\\n23: #include <netinet/in.h>\\n30: #endif\\n31: #ifdef HAVE_NETDB_H\\n32: #include <netdb.h>\\n33: #endif\\n34: #ifdef HAVE_ARPA_INET_H\\n35: #include <arpa/inet.h>\\n36: #endif\\n37: #ifdef HAVE_NET_IF_H\\n38: #include <net/if.h>\\n39: #endif\\n40: #ifdef HAVE_SYS_IOCTL_H\\n41: #include <sys/ioctl.h>\\n42: #endif\\n43: #include <signal.h>\\n44: \\n45: #ifdef HAVE_SYS_PARAM_H\\n46: #include <sys/param.h>\\n47: #endif\\n48: \\n49: #ifdef HAVE_SYS_SELECT_H\\n50: #include <sys/select.h>\\n51: #elif defined(HAVE_UNISTD_H)\\n52: #include <unistd.h>\\n53: #endif\\n54: \\n55: #ifndef HAVE_SOCKET\\n56: #error \\\"We cannot compile without socket() support!\\\"\\n57: #endif\\n58: \\n59: #include \\\"urldata.h\\\"\\n60: #include <curl/curl.h>\\n61: #include \\\"netrc.h\\\"\\n62: \\n63: #include \\\"content_encoding.h\\\"\\n64: #include \\\"hostip.h\\\"\\n65: #include \\\"cfilters.h\\\"\\n66: #include \\\"cw-out.h\\\"\\n67: #include \\\"transfer.h\\\"\\n68: #include \\\"sendf.h\\\"\\n69: #include \\\"speedcheck.h\\\"\\n70: #include \\\"progress.h\\\"\\n71: #include \\\"http.h\\\"\\n72: #include \\\"url.h\\\"\\n73: #include \\\"getinfo.h\\\"\\n74: #include \\\"vtls/vtls.h\\\"\\n75: #include \\\"vquic/vquic.h\\\"\\n76: #include \\\"select.h\\\"\\n77: #include \\\"multiif.h\\\"\\n78: #include \\\"connect.h\\\"\\n79: #include \\\"http2.h\\\"\\n80: #include \\\"mime.h\\\"\\n81: #include \\\"strcase.h\\\"\\n82: #include \\\"hsts.h\\\"\\n83: #include \\\"setopt.h\\\"\\n84: #include \\\"headers.h\\\"\\n85: \\n86: /* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */\\n87: #include \\\"curl_printf.h\\\"\\n88: #include \\\"curl_memory.h\\\"\\n89: #include \\\"memdebug.h\\\"\\n90: \\n91: #if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_SMTP) || \\\\\\n92:     !defined(CURL_DISABLE_IMAP)\\n93: /*\\n94:  * checkheaders() checks the linked list of custom headers for a\\n95:  * particular header (prefix). Provide the prefix without colon!\\n96:  *\\n97:  * Returns a pointer to the first matching header or NULL if none matched.\\n98:  */\\n99: char *Curl_checkheaders(const struct Curl_easy *data,\\n100:                         const char *thisheader,\\n101:                         const size_t thislen)\\n102: {\\n103:   struct curl_slist *head;\\n104:   DEBUGASSERT(thislen);\\n105:   DEBUGASSERT(thisheader[thislen-1] != ':');\\n106: \\n107:   for(head = data->set.headers; head; head = head->next) {\\n108:     if(strncasecompare(head->data, thisheader, thislen) &&\\n109:        Curl_headersep(head->data[thislen]) )\\n110:       return head->data;\\n111:   }\\n112: \\n113:   return NULL;\\n114: }\\n115: #endif\\n116: \\n117: static int data_pending(struct Curl_easy *data)\\n118: {\\n119:   struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\\n120: \\n121:   if(conn->handler->protocol&PROTO_FAMILY_FTP)\\n122:     return Curl_conn_data_pending(data, SECONDARYSOCKET);\\n123: \\n124:   /* in the case of libssh2, we can never be really sure that we have emptied\\n125:      its internal buffers so we MUST always try until we get EAGAIN back */\\n126:   return conn->handler->protocol&(CURLPROTO_SCP|CURLPROTO_SFTP) ||\\n127:     Curl_conn_data_pending(data, FIRSTSOCKET);\\n128: }\\n129: \\n130: /*\\n131:  * Check to see if CURLOPT_TIMECONDITION was met by comparing the time of the\\n132:  * remote document with the time provided by CURLOPT_TIMEVAL\\n133:  */\\n134: bool Curl_meets_timecondition(struct Curl_easy *data, time_t timeofdoc)\\n135: {\\n136:   if((timeofdoc == 0) || (data->set.timevalue == 0))\\n137:     return TRUE;\\n138: \\n139:   switch(data->set.timecondition) {\\n140:   case CURL_TIMECOND_IFMODSINCE:\\n141:   default:\\n142:     if(timeofdoc <= data->set.timevalue) {\\n143:       infof(data,\\n144:             \\\"The requested document is not new enough\\\");\\n145:       data->info.timecond = TRUE;\\n146:       return FALSE;\\n147:     }\\n148:     break;\\n149:   case CURL_TIMECOND_IFUNMODSINCE:\\n150:     if(timeofdoc >= data->set.timevalue) {\\n151:       infof(data,\\n152:             \\\"The requested document is not old enough\\\");\\n153:       data->info.timecond = TRUE;\\n154:       return FALSE;\\n155:     }\\n156:     break;\\n157:   }\\n158: \\n159:   return TRUE;\\n160: }\\n161: \\n162: static CURLcode xfer_recv_shutdown(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done)\\n163: {\\n164:   int sockindex;\\n165: \\n166:   if(!data || !data->conn)\\n167:     return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\\n168:   if(data->conn->sockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\\n169:     return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\\n170:   sockindex = (data->conn->sockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\\n171:   return Curl_conn_shutdown(data, sockindex, done);\\n172: }\\n173: \\n174: static bool xfer_recv_shutdown_started(struct Curl_easy *data)\\n175: {\\n176:   int sockindex;\\n177: \\n178:   if(!data || !data->conn)\\n179:     return FALSE;\\n180:   if(data->conn->sockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\\n181:     return FALSE;\\n182:   sockindex = (data->conn->sockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\\n183:   return Curl_shutdown_started(data, sockindex);\\n184: }\\n185: \\n186: CURLcode Curl_xfer_send_shutdown(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done)\\n187: {\\n188:   int sockindex;\\n189: \\n190:   if(!data || !data->conn)\\n191:     return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\\n192:   if(data->conn->writesockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\\n193:     return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\\n194:   sockindex = (data->conn->writesockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\\n195:   return Curl_conn_shutdown(data, sockindex, done);\\n196: }\\n197: \\n198: /**\\n199:  * Receive raw response data for the transfer.\\n200:  * @param data         the transfer\\n201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl@34cf9d54a46598c44938aa7598820484d7af7133/-/blob/lib/transfer.c%3FL1-200\"}",

    "content": "1: /***************************************************************************\n2:  *                                  _   _ ____  _\n3:  *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n4:  *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n5:  *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n6:  *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n7:  *\n8:  * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n9:  *\n10:  * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n11:  * you should have received as part of this distribution. #include \"curl_setup.h\"\n26: #include \"strtoofft.h\"\n27: \n28: #ifdef HAVE_NETINET_IN_H\n29: #include <netinet/in.h>\n30: #endif\n31: #ifdef HAVE_NETDB_H\n32: #include <netdb.h>\n33: #endif\n34: #ifdef HAVE_ARPA_INET_H\n35: #include <arpa/inet.h>\n36: #endif\n37: #ifdef HAVE_NET_IF_H\n38: #include <net/if.h>\n39: #endif\n40: #ifdef HAVE_SYS_IOCTL_H\n41: #include <sys/ioctl.h>\n42: #endif\n43: #include <signal.h>\n44: \n45: #ifdef HAVE_SYS_PARAM_H\n46: #include <sys/param.h>\n47: #endif\n48: \n49: #ifdef HAVE_SYS_SELECT_H\n50: #include <sys/select.h>\n51: #elif defined(HAVE_UNISTD_H)\n52: #include <unistd.h>\n53: #endif\n54: \n55: #ifndef HAVE_SOCKET\n56: #error \"We cannot compile without socket() support!\"\n57: #endif\n58: \n59: #include \"urldata.h\"\n60: #include <curl/curl.h>\n61: #include \"netrc.h\"\n62: \n63: #include \"content_encoding.h\"\n64: #include \"hostip.h\"\n65: #include \"cfilters.h\"\n66: #include \"cw-out.h\"\n67: #include \"transfer.h\"\n68: #include \"sendf.h\"\n69: #include \"speedcheck.h\"\n70: #include \"progress.h\"\n71: #include \"http.h\"\n72: #include \"url.h\"\n73: #include \"getinfo.h\"\n74: #include \"vtls/vtls.h\"\n75: #include \"vquic/vquic.h\"\n76: #include \"select.h\"\n77: #include \"multiif.h\"\n78: #include \"connect.h\"\n79: #include \"http2.h\"\n80: #include \"mime.h\"\n81: #include \"strcase.h\"\n82: #include \"hsts.h\"\n83: #include \"setopt.h\"\n84: #include \"headers.h\"\n85: \n86: /* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */\n87: #include \"curl_printf.h\"\n88: #include \"curl_memory.h\"\n89: #include \"memdebug.h\"\n90: \n91: #if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_SMTP) || \\\n92:     !defined(CURL_DISABLE_IMAP)\n93: /*\n94:  * checkheaders() checks the linked list of custom headers for a\n95:  * particular header (prefix). Provide the prefix without colon!\n96:  *\n97:  * Returns a pointer to the first matching header or NULL if none matched.\n98:  */\n99: char *Curl_checkheaders(const struct Curl_easy *data,\n100:                         const char *thisheader,\n101:                         const size_t thislen)\n102: {\n103:   struct curl_slist *head;\n104:   DEBUGASSERT(thislen);\n105:   DEBUGASSERT(thisheader[thislen-1] != ':');\n106: \n107:   for(head = data->set.headers; head; head = head->next) {\n108:     if(strncasecompare(head->data, thisheader, thislen) &&\n109:        Curl_headersep(head->data[thislen]) )\n110:       return head->data;\n111:   }\n112: \n113:   return NULL;\n114: }\n115: #endif\n116: \n117: static int data_pending(struct Curl_easy *data)\n118: {\n119:   struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n120: \n121:   if(conn->handler->protocol&PROTO_FAMILY_FTP)\n122:     return Curl_conn_data_pending(data, SECONDARYSOCKET);\n123: \n124:   /* in the case of libssh2, we can never be really sure that we have emptied\n125:      its internal buffers so we MUST always try until we get EAGAIN back */\n126:   return conn->handler->protocol&(CURLPROTO_SCP|CURLPROTO_SFTP) ||\n127:     Curl_conn_data_pending(data, FIRSTSOCKET);\n128: }\n129: \n130: /*\n131:  * Check to see if CURLOPT_TIMECONDITION was met by comparing the time of the\n132:  * remote document with the time provided by CURLOPT_TIMEVAL\n133:  */\n134: bool Curl_meets_timecondition(struct Curl_easy *data, time_t timeofdoc)\n135: {\n136:   if((timeofdoc == 0) || (data->set.timevalue == 0))\n137:     return TRUE;\n138: \n139:   switch(data->set.timecondition) {\n140:   case CURL_TIMECOND_IFMODSINCE:\n141:   default:\n142:     if(timeofdoc <= data->set.timevalue) {\n143:       infof(data,\n144:             \"The requested document is not new enough\");\n145:       data->info.timecond = TRUE;\n146:       return FALSE;\n147:     }\n148:     break;\n149:   case CURL_TIMECOND_IFUNMODSINCE:\n150:     if(timeofdoc >= data->set.timevalue) {\n151:       infof(data,\n152:             \"The requested document is not old enough\");\n153:       data->info.timecond = TRUE;\n154:       return FALSE;\n155:     }\n156:     break;\n157:   }\n158: \n159:   return TRUE;\n160: }\n161: \n162: static CURLcode xfer_recv_shutdown(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done)\n163: {\n164:   int sockindex;\n165: \n166:   if(!data || !data->conn)\n167:     return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n168:   if(data->conn->sockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\n169:     return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n170:   sockindex = (data->conn->sockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\n171:   return Curl_conn_shutdown(data, sockindex, done);\n172: }\n173: \n174: static bool xfer_recv_shutdown_started(struct Curl_easy *data)\n175: {\n176:   int sockindex;\n177: \n178:   if(!data || !data->conn)\n179:     return FALSE;\n180:   if(data->conn->sockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\n181:     return FALSE;\n182:   sockindex = (data->conn->sockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\n183:   return Curl_shutdown_started(data, sockindex);\n184: }\n185: \n186: CURLcode Curl_xfer_send_shutdown(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done)\n187: {\n188:   int sockindex;\n189: \n190:   if(!data || !data->conn)\n191:     return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n192:   if(data->conn->writesockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\n193:     return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n194:   sockindex = (data->conn->writesockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\n195:   return Curl_conn_shutdown(data, sockindex, done);\n196: }\n197: \n198: /**\n199:  * Receive raw response data for the transfer.\n200:  * @param data         the transfer\n201: ",

  "description": "Find fixup commits"
}

  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "64d9f3543 fixup free the send token on errors\nfe276ff15 fixup merge two lines\ncd7cd5462 fixup white space\n45f2ed955 fixup return code\n614abe111 fixup 'etbuf_size' may be used uninitialized\n57d5935f6 fixup oopses\ndbe06f38a DEPRECATE.md: move OpenSSL to past removals (fixup)\n7ba58cf89 autotools: drop headers from src mk-unity rules (fixup)\n682800969 windows: fixup `fopen()` in `CURLDEBUG` builds\n596da988c GHA/windows: fixup MSYS2 downgrade step\n1d0e19a64 tests: fixup tunit tests for cmake\n04c78c897 curl_multibyte: fixup low-level calls, include in unity builds\n5ce5c6266 GHA: fixup CM/AM order within Apple steps, move `name:` first, quote them\n554d4aee4 appveyor: fixup job name\n72edb22b8 tool_getpass: restore UWP `getpass_r()`, fixup CI builds, fix UWP `-Wnull-dereference`\n8ea120f61 GHA/linux: fixup pip for Ubuntu 24.04\n01a815799 cmake: tidy-ups and rebase fixups\naa092012b tests: fixup `checkcmd` `PATH` on non-unixy platforms\n28d1d71ff lib: memdebug comment fixup [ci skip]\n81a086146 cmake: fixup linking libgsasl when detected via CMake-native\n4beb23647 cmake: fixup variable reference in FindZstd\n62b13ecfe configure: fixup copy-paste mistake\n7b1c0ab75 Makefile.mk: fixup enabling libidn2\n4f198c852 tests: fixup `tests/data/Makefile.am` references\n5a488251f curldown: fixups\na3e613a84 GHA/badwords.yml: fixup indent for yamllint [ci skip]\n08fa47317 CURLOPT_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD/USERNAME.md: language fixups\nb02d587e7 fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! GHA: Make ngtcp2 cache efficient\n98c5778eb fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! GHA: Make ngtcp2 cache efficient\n50d91c8de fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! GHA: Make ngtcp2 cache efficient\nbc511e004 fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! GHA: Make ngtcp2 cache efficient\nbb21ad0b6 fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! GHA: Make ngtcp2 cache efficient\n3d26904d3 fixup! fixup! fixup! fixup! GHA: Make ngtcp2 cache efficient\n2aa10d9af fixup! fixup! fixup! GHA: Make ngtcp2 cache efficient\n51121c676 fixup! fixup! GHA: Make ngtcp2 cache efficient\nbe60eeb81 fixup! GHA: Make ngtcp2 cache efficient\nf709a45c5 appveyor: fixup job name [ci skip]\ndad126b84 cmake: update ECH code and minor fixups\n0469c68f4 ci: add CMake build variation, fixup libssh detection in `linux-old`\n4f15443d2 quic: fixup duplicate static function name (for cmake unity)\n2cac6a49b cmake: fixup `DEPENDS` filename\na7f8d04ee curl-functions.m4: fixup recent bad edits\nf402c181e lib: build fixups when built with most things disabled\nacca40c9b lib: build fixups when built with most things disabled\n4a67b23f3 cmake: fixup H2 duplicate symbols for unity builds\ncbf571761 tests: HTTP server fixups\n9325ab2cf windows: autotools .rc warnings fixup\n22e34da09 tests/libtest/Makefile.inc: fixup merge conflict mistake\nc6631e827 configure: fixup bsdsocket detection code for AmigaOS 4.x\n0239a5fbf tests/libtest/lib670.c: fixup the copyright year range\nc65262192 winbuild/README.md: fixup dead link\n439aa5021 test1156: fixup the stdout check for Windows\n697970c1d mailmap: fixup Micha\u0142 Antoniak\n124198631 tests: fixup several tests\neafe1eaff mailmap: fixups of some contributors\n33d60d09f tool_paramhlp: fixup C89 mistake\nd973bdf88 mailmap: fixup a few author names/fields\n7ef4e2e90 mailmap: fixup Massimiliano Fantuzzi\n0428852c2 DEPRECATE: fixup versions and spelling\n4da5794d8 wolfssl: fixup for SNI use\n89165c1a9 THANKS: fixups and a dedupe\ne1360a1c9 (lib)curl.rc: fixup for minor bugs\naa18b573c mailmap: a Richard Alcock fixup\n462d8378e mailmap: add a monnerat fixup [ci skip]\nb2afc36da THANKS + mailmap: remove duplicates, fixup full names\n06a0a26fb mailmap: fixup two old git Author \"aliases\"\na69a4d222 ftp: UBsan fixup 'pointer index expression overflowed'\n328ce03d5 fixup data/test1135\nc4eb10e2f SSH: three state machine fixups\n013d5c18c debug: added new connection cache output, plus fixups"
}

\"memdebug.h\"\n    90\t\n    91\t#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_SMTP) || \\\n    92\t    !defined(CURL_DISABLE_IMAP)\n    93\t/*\n    94\t * checkheaders() checks the linked list of custom headers for a\n    95\t * particular header (prefix). Provide the prefix without colon!\n    96\t *\n    97\t * Returns a pointer to the first matching header or NULL if none matched.\n    98\t */\n    99\tchar *Curl_checkheaders(const struct Curl_easy *data,\n   100\t                        const char *thisheader,\n   101\t                        const size_t thislen)\n   102\t{\n   103\t  struct curl_slist *head;\n   104\t  DEBUGASSERT(thislen);\n   105\t  DEBUGASSERT(thisheader[thislen-1] != ':');\n   106\t\n   107\t  for(head = data->set.headers; head; head = head->next) {\n   108\t    if(strncasecompare(head->data, thisheader, thislen) &&\n   109\t       Curl_headersep(head->data[thislen]) )\n   110\t      return head->data;\n   111\t  }\n   112\t\n   113\t  return NULL;\n   114\t}\n   115\t#endif\n   116\t\n   117\tstatic int data_pending(struct Curl_easy *data)\n   118\t{\n   119\t  struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n   120\t\n   121\t  if(conn->handler->protocol&PROTO_FAMILY_FTP)\n   122\t    return Curl_conn_data_pending(data, SECONDARYSOCKET);\n   123\t\n   124\t  /* in the case of libssh2, we can never be really sure that we have emptied\n   125\t     its internal buffers so we MUST always try until we get EAGAIN back */\n   126\t  return conn->handler->protocol&(CURLPROTO_SCP|CURLPROTO_SFTP) ||\n   127\t    Curl_conn_data_pending(data, FIRSTSOCKET);\n   128\t}\n   129\t\n   130\t/*\n   131\t * Check to see if CURLOPT_TIMECONDITION was met by comparing the time of the\n   132\t * remote document with the time provided by CURLOPT_TIMEVAL\n   133\t */\n   134\tbool Curl_meets_timecondition(struct Curl_easy *data, time_t timeofdoc)\n   135\t{\n   136\t  if((timeofdoc == 0) || (data->set.timevalue == 0))\n   137\t    return TRUE;\n   138\t\n   139\t  switch(data->set.timecondition) {\n   140\t  case CURL_TIMECOND_IFMODSINCE:\n   141\t  default:\n   142\t    if(timeofdoc <= data->set.timevalue) {\n   143\t      infof(data,\n   144\t            \"The requested document is not new enough\");\n   145\t      data->info.timecond = TRUE;\n   146\t      return FALSE;\n   147\t    }\n   148\t    break;\n   149\t  case CURL_TIMECOND_IFUNMODSINCE:\n   150\t    if(timeofdoc >= data->set.timevalue) {\n   151\t      infof(data,\n   152\t            \"The requested document is not old enough\");\n   153\t      data->info.timecond = TRUE;\n   154\t      return FALSE;\n   155\t    }\n   156\t    break;\n   157\t  }\n   158\t\n   159\t  return TRUE;\n   160\t}\n   161\t\n   162\tstatic CURLcode xfer_recv_shutdown(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done)\n   163\t{\n   164\t  int sockindex;\n   165\t\n   166\t  if(!data || !data->conn)\n   167\t    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n   168\t  if(data->conn->sockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\n   169\t    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n   170\t  sockindex = (data->conn->sockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\n   171\t  return Curl_conn_shutdown(data, sockindex, done);\n   172\t}\n   173\t\n   174\tstatic bool xfer_recv_shutdown_started(struct Curl_easy *data)\n   175\t{\n   176\t  int sockindex;\n   177\t\n   178\t  if(!data || !data->conn)\n   179\t    return FALSE;\n   180\t  if(data->conn->sockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\n   181\t    return FALSE;\n   182\t  sockindex = (data->conn->sockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\n   183\t  return Curl_shutdown_started(data, sockindex);\n   184\t}\n CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n   192\t  if(data->conn->writesockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\n   193\t    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n   194\t  sockindex = (data->conn->writesockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\n   195\t  return Curl_conn_shutdown(data, sockindex, done);\n   196\t}\n   197\t\n   198\t/**\n   199\t * Receive raw response data for the transfer.\n   200\t * @param data         the transfer"
}
35. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat -n /workspace/lib/transfer.c | sed -n '200,400p'",
  "description": "Read transfer.c lines 200-400"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "   200\t * @param data         the transfer\n   201\t * @param buf          buffer to keep response data received\n   202\t * @param blen         length of `buf`\n   203\t * @param eos_reliable if EOS detection in underlying connection is reliable\n   204\t * @param err error    code in case of -1 return\n   205\t * @return number of bytes read or -1 for error\n   206\t */\n   207\tstatic ssize_t xfer_recv_resp(struct Curl_easy *data,\n   208\t                              char *buf, size_t blen,\n   209\t                              bool eos_reliable,\n   210\t                              CURLcode *err)\n   211\t{\n   212\t  ssize_t nread;\n   213\t\n   214\t  DEBUGASSERT(blen > 0);\n   215\t  /* If we are reading BODY data and the connection does NOT handle EOF\n   216\t   * and we know the size of the BODY data, limit the read amount */\n   217\t  if(!eos_reliable && !data->req.header && data->req.size != -1) {\n   218\t    curl_off_t totalleft = data->req.size - data->req.bytecount;\n   219\t    if(totalleft <= 0)\n   220\t      blen = 0;\n   221\t    else if(totalleft < (curl_off_t)blen)\n   222\t      blen = (size_t)totalleft;\n   223\t  }\n   224\t  else if(xfer_recv_shutdown_started(data)) {\n   225\t    /* we already received everything. Do not try more. */\n   226\t    blen = 0;\n   227\t  }\n   228\t\n   229\t  if(!blen) {\n   230\t    /* want nothing more */\n   231\t    *err = CURLE_OK;\n   232\t    nread = 0;\n   233\t  }\n   234\t  else {\n   235\t    *err = Curl_xfer_recv(data, buf, blen, &nread);\n   236\t  }\n   237\t\n   238\t  if(*err)\n   239\t    return -1;\n   240\t  if(nread == 0) {\n   241\t    if(data->req.shutdown) {\n   242\t      bool done;\n   243\t      *err = xfer_recv_shutdown(data, &done);\n   244\t      if(*err)\n   245\t        return -1;\n   246\t      if(!done) {\n   247\t        *err = CURLE_AGAIN;\n   248\t        return -1;\n   249\t      }\n   250\t    }\n   251\t    DEBUGF(infof(data, \"sendrecv_dl: we are done\"));\n   252\t  }\n   253\t  DEBUGASSERT(nread >= 0);\n   254\t  return nread;\n   255\t}\n   256\t\n   257\t/*\n   258\t * Go ahead and do a read if we have a readable socket or if\n   259\t * the stream was rewound (in which case we have data in a\n   260\t * buffer)\n   261\t */\n   262\tstatic CURLcode sendrecv_dl(struct Curl_easy *data,\n   263\t                            struct SingleRequest *k,\n   264\t                            int *didwhat)\n   265\t{\n   266\t  struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n   267\t  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n   268\t  char *buf, *xfer_buf;\n   269\t  size_t blen, xfer_blen;\n   270\t  int maxloops = 10;\n   271\t  curl_off_t total_received = 0;\n   272\t  bool is_multiplex = FALSE;\n   273\t\n   274\t  result = Curl_multi_xfer_buf_borrow(data, &xfer_buf, &xfer_blen);\n   275\t  if(result)\n   276\t    goto out;\n   277\t\n   278\t  /* This is where we loop until we have read everything there is to\n   279\t     read or we get a CURLE_AGAIN */\n   280\t  do {\n   281\t    bool is_eos = FALSE;\n   282\t    size_t bytestoread;\n   283\t    ssize_t nread;\n   284\t\n   285\t    if(!is_multiplex) {\n   286\t      /* Multiplexed connection have inherent handling of EOF and we do not\n   287\t       * have to carefully restrict the amount we try to read.\n   288\t       * Multiplexed changes only in one direction. */\n   289\t      is_multiplex = Curl_conn_is_multiplex(conn, FIRSTSOCKET);\n   290\t    }\n   291\t\n   292\t    buf = xfer_buf;\n   293\t    bytestoread = xfer_blen;\n   294\t\n   295\t    if(bytestoread && data->set.max_recv_speed > 0) {\n   296\t      /* In case of speed limit on receiving: if this loop already got\n   297\t       * data, break out. If not, limit the amount of bytes to receive.\n   298\t       * The overall, timed, speed limiting is done in multi.c */\n   299\t      if(total_received)\n   300\t        break;\n   301\t      if(data->set.max_recv_speed < (curl_off_t)bytestoread)\n   302\t        bytestoread = (size_t)data->set.max_recv_speed;\n   303\t    }\n   304\t\n   305\t    nread = xfer_recv_resp(data, buf, bytestoread, is_multiplex, &result);\n   306\t    if(nread < 0) {\n   307\t      if(CURLE_AGAIN != result)\n   308\t        goto out; /* real error */\n   309\t      result = CURLE_OK;\n   310\t      if(data->req.download_done && data->req.no_body &&\n   311\t         !data->req.resp_trailer) {\n   312\t        DEBUGF(infof(data, \"EAGAIN, download done, no trailer announced, \"\n   313\t               \"not waiting for EOS\"));\n   314\t        nread = 0;\n   315\t        /* continue as if we read the EOS */\n   316\t      }\n   317\t      else\n   318\t        break; /* get out of loop */\n   319\t    }\n   320\t\n   321\t    /* We only get a 0-length read on EndOfStream */\n   322\t    blen = (size_t)nread;\n   323\t    is_eos = (blen == 0);\n   324\t    *didwhat |= KEEP_RECV;\n   325\t\n   326\t    if(!blen) {\n   327\t      /* if we receive 0 or less here, either the data transfer is done or the\n   328\t         server closed the connection and we bail out from this! */\n   329\t      if(is_multiplex)\n   330\t        DEBUGF(infof(data, \"nread == 0, stream closed, bailing\"));\n   331\t      else\n   332\t        DEBUGF(infof(data, \"nread <= 0, server closed connection, bailing\"));\n   333\t      result = Curl_req_stop_send_recv(data);\n   334\t      if(result)\n   335\t        goto out;\n   336\t      if(k->eos_written) /* already did write this to client, leave */\n   337\t        break;\n   338\t    }\n   339\t    total_received += blen;\n   340\t\n   341\t    result = Curl_xfer_write_resp(data, buf, blen, is_eos);\n   342\t    if(result || data->req.done)\n   343\t      goto out;\n   344\t\n   345\t    /* if we are done, we stop receiving. On multiplexed connections,\n   346\t     * we should read the EOS. Which may arrive as meta data after\n   347\t     * the bytes. Not taking it in might lead to RST of streams. */\n   348\t    if((!is_multiplex && data->req.download_done) || is_eos) {\n   349\t      data->req.keepon &= ~KEEP_RECV;\n   350\t    }\n   351\t    /* if we are PAUSEd or stopped receiving, leave the loop */\n   352\t    if((k->keepon & KEEP_RECV_PAUSE) || !(k->keepon & KEEP_RECV))\n   353\t      break;\n   354\t\n   355\t  } while(maxloops--);\n   356\t\n   357\t  if((maxloops <= 0) || data_pending(data)) {\n   358\t    /* did not read until EAGAIN or there is still pending data, mark as\n   359\t       read-again-please */\n   360\t    data->state.select_bits = CURL_CSELECT_IN;\n   361\t    if((k->keepon & KEEP_SENDBITS) == KEEP_SEND)\n   362\t      data->state.select_bits |= CURL_CSELECT_OUT;\n   363\t  }\n   364\t\n   365\t  if(((k->keepon & (KEEP_RECV|KEEP_SEND)) == KEEP_SEND) &&\n   366\t     (conn->bits.close || is_multiplex)) {\n   367\t    /* When we have read the entire thing and the close bit is set, the server\n   368\t       may now close the connection. If there is now any kind of sending going\n   369\t       on from our side, we need to stop that immediately. */\n   370\t    infof(data, \"we are done reading and this is set to close, stop send\");\n   371\t    Curl_req_abort_sending(data);\n   372\t  }\n   373\t\n   374\tout:\n   375\t  Curl_multi_xfer_buf_release(data, xfer_buf);\n   376\t  if(result)\n   377\t    DEBUGF(infof(data, \"sendrecv_dl() -> %d\", result));\n   378\t  return result;\n   379\t}\n   380\t\n   381\t/*\n   382\t * Send data to upload to the server, when the socket is writable.\n   383\t */\n   384\tstatic CURLcode sendrecv_ul(struct Curl_easy *data, int *didwhat)\n   385\t{\n   386\t  /* We should not get here when the sending is already done. It\n   387\t   * probably means that someone set `data-req.keepon |= KEEP_SEND`\n   388\t   * when it should not. */\n   389\t  DEBUGASSERT(!Curl_req_done_sending(data));\n   390\t\n   391\t  if(!Curl_req_done_sending(data)) {\n   392\t    *didwhat |= KEEP_SEND;\n   393\t    return Curl_req_send_more(data);\n   394\t  }\n   395\t  return CURLE_OK;\n   396\t}\n   397\t\n   398\tstatic int select_bits_paused(struct Curl_easy *data, int select_bits)\n   399\t{\n   400\t  /* See issue #11982: we really need to be careful not to progress"
}
36. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat -n /workspace/lib/transfer.c | sed -n '400,600p'",
  "description": "Read transfer.c lines 400-600"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "   400\t  /* See issue #11982: we really need to be careful not to progress\n   401\t   * a transfer direction when that direction is paused. Not all parts\n   402\t   * of our state machine are handling PAUSED transfers correctly. So, we\n   403\t   * do not want to go there.\n   404\t   * NOTE: we are only interested in PAUSE, not HOLD. */\n   405\t\n   406\t  /* if there is data in a direction not paused, return false */\n   407\t  if(((select_bits & CURL_CSELECT_IN) &&\n   408\t      !(data->req.keepon & KEEP_RECV_PAUSE)) ||\n   409\t     ((select_bits & CURL_CSELECT_OUT) &&\n   410\t      !(data->req.keepon & KEEP_SEND_PAUSE)))\n   411\t    return FALSE;\n   412\t\n   413\t  return (data->req.keepon & (KEEP_RECV_PAUSE|KEEP_SEND_PAUSE));\n   414\t}\n   415\t\n   416\t/*\n   417\t * Curl_sendrecv() is the low-level function to be called when data is to\n   418\t * be read and written to/from the connection.\n   419\t */\n   420\tCURLcode Curl_sendrecv(struct Curl_easy *data, struct curltime *nowp)\n   421\t{\n   422\t  struct SingleRequest *k = &data->req;\n   423\t  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n   424\t  int didwhat = 0;\n   425\t\n   426\t  DEBUGASSERT(nowp);\n   427\t  if(data->state.select_bits) {\n   428\t    if(select_bits_paused(data, data->state.select_bits)) {\n   429\t      /* leave the bits unchanged, so they'll tell us what to do when\n   430\t       * this transfer gets unpaused. */\n   431\t      result = CURLE_OK;\n   432\t      goto out;\n   433\t    }\n   434\t    data->state.select_bits = 0;\n   435\t  }\n   436\t\n   437\t  /* We go ahead and do a read if we have a readable socket or if the stream\n   438\t     was rewound (in which case we have data in a buffer) */\n   439\t  if(k->keepon & KEEP_RECV) {\n   440\t    result = sendrecv_dl(data, k, &didwhat);\n   441\t    if(result || data->req.done)\n   442\t      goto out;\n   443\t  }\n   444\t\n   445\t  /* If we still have writing to do, we check if we have a writable socket. */\n   446\t  if(Curl_req_want_send(data) || (data->req.keepon & KEEP_SEND_TIMED)) {\n   447\t    result = sendrecv_ul(data, &didwhat);\n   448\t    if(result)\n   449\t      goto out;\n   450\t  }\n   451\t\n   452\t  if(!didwhat) {\n   453\t    /* Transfer wanted to send/recv, but nothing was possible. */\n   454\t    result = Curl_conn_ev_data_idle(data);\n   455\t    if(result)\n   456\t      goto out;\n   457\t  }\n   458\t\n   459\t  if(Curl_pgrsUpdate(data))\n   460\t    result = CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK;\n   461\t  else\n   462\t    result = Curl_speedcheck(data, *nowp);\n   463\t  if(result)\n   464\t    goto out;\n   465\t\n   466\t  if(k->keepon) {\n   467\t    if(0 > Curl_timeleft(data, nowp, FALSE)) {\n   468\t      if(k->size != -1) {\n   469\t        failf(data, \"Operation timed out after %\" FMT_TIMEDIFF_T\n   470\t              \" milliseconds with %\" FMT_OFF_T \" out of %\"\n   471\t              FMT_OFF_T \" bytes received\",\n   472\t              Curl_timediff(*nowp, data->progress.t_startsingle),\n   473\t              k->bytecount, k->size);\n   474\t      }\n   475\t      else {\n   476\t        failf(data, \"Operation timed out after %\" FMT_TIMEDIFF_T\n   477\t              \" milliseconds with %\" FMT_OFF_T \" bytes received\",\n   478\t              Curl_timediff(*nowp, data->progress.t_startsingle),\n   479\t              k->bytecount);\n   480\t      }\n   481\t      result = CURLE_OPERATION_TIMEDOUT;\n   482\t      goto out;\n   483\t    }\n   484\t  }\n   485\t  else {\n   486\t    /*\n   487\t     * The transfer has been performed. Just make some general checks before\n   488\t     * returning.\n   489\t     */\n   490\t    if(!(data->req.no_body) && (k->size != -1) &&\n   491\t       (k->bytecount != k->size) && !k->newurl) {\n   492\t      failf(data, \"transfer closed with %\" FMT_OFF_T\n   493\t            \" bytes remaining to read\", k->size - k->bytecount);\n   494\t      result = CURLE_PARTIAL_FILE;\n   495\t      goto out;\n   496\t    }\n   497\t    if(Curl_pgrsUpdate(data)) {\n   498\t      result = CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK;\n   499\t      goto out;\n   500\t    }\n   501\t  }\n   502\t\n   503\t  /* If there is nothing more to send/recv, the request is done */\n   504\t  if(0 == (k->keepon&(KEEP_RECVBITS|KEEP_SENDBITS)))\n   505\t    data->req.done = TRUE;\n   506\t\n   507\tout:\n   508\t  if(result)\n   509\t    DEBUGF(infof(data, \"Curl_sendrecv() -> %d\", result));\n   510\t  return result;\n   511\t}\n   512\t\n   513\t/* Curl_init_CONNECT() gets called each time the handle switches to CONNECT\n   514\t   which means this gets called once for each subsequent redirect etc */\n   515\tvoid Curl_init_CONNECT(struct Curl_easy *data)\n   516\t{\n   517\t  data->state.fread_func = data->set.fread_func_set;\n   518\t  data->state.in = data->set.in_set;\n   519\t  data->state.upload = (data->state.httpreq == HTTPREQ_PUT);\n   520\t}\n   521\t\n   522\t/*\n   523\t * Curl_pretransfer() is called immediately before a transfer starts, and only\n   524\t * once for one transfer no matter if it has redirects or do multi-pass\n   525\t * authentication etc.\n   526\t */\n   527\tCURLcode Curl_pretransfer(struct Curl_easy *data)\n   528\t{\n   529\t  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n   530\t\n   531\t  if(!data->set.str[STRING_SET_URL] && !data->set.uh) {\n   532\t    /* we cannot do anything without URL */\n   533\t    failf(data, \"No URL set\");\n   534\t    return CURLE_URL_MALFORMAT;\n   535\t  }\n   536\t\n   537\t  /* CURLOPT_CURLU overrides CURLOPT_URL and the contents of the CURLU handle\n   538\t     is allowed to be changed by the user between transfers */\n   539\t  if(data->set.uh) {\n   540\t    CURLUcode uc;\n   541\t    free(data->set.str[STRING_SET_URL]);\n   542\t    uc = curl_url_get(data->set.uh,\n   543\t                      CURLUPART_URL, &data->set.str[STRING_SET_URL], 0);\n   544\t    if(uc) {\n   545\t      failf(data, \"No URL set\");\n   546\t      return CURLE_URL_MALFORMAT;\n   547\t    }\n   548\t  }\n   549\t\n   550\t  /* since the URL may have been redirected in a previous use of this handle */\n   551\t  if(data->state.url_alloc) {\n   552\t    Curl_safefree(data->state.url);\n   553\t    data->state.url_alloc = FALSE;\n   554\t  }\n   555\t\n   556\t  data->state.url = data->set.str[STRING_SET_URL];\n   557\t\n   558\t  if(data->set.postfields && data->set.set_resume_from) {\n   559\t    /* we cannot */\n   560\t    failf(data, \"cannot mix POSTFIELDS with RESUME_FROM\");\n   561\t    return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n   562\t  }\n   563\t\n   564\t  data->state.prefer_ascii = data->set.prefer_ascii;\n   565\t#ifdef CURL_LIST_ONLY_PROTOCOL\n   566\t  data->state.list_only = data->set.list_only;\n   567\t#endif\n   568\t  data->state.httpreq = data->set.method;\n   569\t\n   570\t#ifdef USE_SSL\n   571\t  if(!data->state.ssl_scache)\n   572\t    /* There was no ssl session cache set via a share, use the multi one */\n   573\t    data->state.ssl_scache = data->multi->ssl_scache;\n   574\t#endif\n   575\t\n   576\t  data->state.requests = 0;\n   577\t  data->state.followlocation = 0; /* reset the location-follow counter */\n   578\t  data->state.this_is_a_follow = FALSE; /* reset this */\n   579\t  data->state.errorbuf = FALSE; /* no error has occurred */\n   580\t  data->state.httpwant = data->set.httpwant;\n   581\t  data->state.httpversion = 0;\n   582\t  data->state.authproblem = FALSE;\n   583\t  data->state.authhost.want = data->set.httpauth;\n   584\t  data->state.authproxy.want = data->set.proxyauth;\n   585\t  Curl_safefree(data->info.wouldredirect);\n   586\t  Curl_data_priority_clear_state(data);\n   587\t\n   588\t  if(data->state.httpreq == HTTPREQ_PUT)\n   589\t    data->state.infilesize = data->set.filesize;\n   590\t  else if((data->state.httpreq != HTTPREQ_GET) &&\n   591\t          (data->state.httpreq != HTTPREQ_HEAD)) {\n   592\t    data->state.infilesize = data->set.postfieldsize;\n   593\t    if(data->set.postfields && (data->state.infilesize == -1))\n   594\t      data->state.infilesize = (curl_off_t)strlen(data->set.postfields);\n   595\t  }\n   596\t  else\n   597\t    data->state.infilesize = 0;\n   598\t\n   599\t  /* If there is a list of cookie files to read, do it now! */\n   600\t  Curl_cookie_loadfiles(data);"
}
37. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat -n /workspace/lib/transfer.c | sed -n '600,800p'",
  "description": "Read transfer.c lines 600-800"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "   600\t  Curl_cookie_loadfiles(data);\n   601\t\n   602\t  /* If there is a list of host pairs to deal with */\n   603\t  if(data->state.resolve)\n   604\t    result = Curl_loadhostpairs(data);\n   605\t\n   606\t  /* If there is a list of hsts files to read */\n   607\t  Curl_hsts_loadfiles(data);\n   608\t\n   609\t  if(!result) {\n   610\t    /* Allow data->set.use_port to set which port to use. This needs to be\n   611\t     * disabled for example when we follow Location: headers to URLs using\n   612\t     * different ports! */\n   613\t    data->state.allow_port = TRUE;\n   614\t\n   615\t#if defined(HAVE_SIGNAL) && defined(SIGPIPE) && !defined(HAVE_MSG_NOSIGNAL)\n   616\t    /*************************************************************\n   617\t     * Tell signal handler to ignore SIGPIPE\n   618\t     *************************************************************/\n   619\t    if(!data->set.no_signal)\n   620\t      data->state.prev_signal = signal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN);\n   621\t#endif\n   622\t\n   623\t    Curl_initinfo(data); /* reset session-specific information \"variables\" */\n   624\t    Curl_pgrsResetTransferSizes(data);\n   625\t    Curl_pgrsStartNow(data);\n   626\t\n   627\t    /* In case the handle is reused and an authentication method was picked\n   628\t       in the session we need to make sure we only use the one(s) we now\n   629\t       consider to be fine */\n   630\t    data->state.authhost.picked &= data->state.authhost.want;\n   631\t    data->state.authproxy.picked &= data->state.authproxy.want;\n   632\t\n   633\t#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n   634\t    data->state.wildcardmatch = data->set.wildcard_enabled;\n   635\t    if(data->state.wildcardmatch) {\n   636\t      struct WildcardData *wc;\n   637\t      if(!data->wildcard) {\n   638\t        data->wildcard = calloc(1, sizeof(struct WildcardData));\n   639\t        if(!data->wildcard)\n   640\t          return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n   641\t      }\n   642\t      wc = data->wildcard;\n   643\t      if(wc->state < CURLWC_INIT) {\n   644\t        if(wc->ftpwc)\n   645\t          wc->dtor(wc->ftpwc);\n   646\t        Curl_safefree(wc->pattern);\n   647\t        Curl_safefree(wc->path);\n   648\t        result = Curl_wildcard_init(wc); /* init wildcard structures */\n   649\t        if(result)\n   650\t          return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n   651\t      }\n   652\t    }\n   653\t#endif\n   654\t    result = Curl_hsts_loadcb(data, data->hsts);\n   655\t  }\n   656\t\n   657\t  /*\n   658\t   * Set user-agent. Used for HTTP, but since we can attempt to tunnel\n   659\t   * basically anything through an HTTP proxy we cannot limit this based on\n   660\t   * protocol.\n   661\t   */\n   662\t  if(data->set.str[STRING_USERAGENT]) {\n   663\t    Curl_safefree(data->state.aptr.uagent);\n   664\t    data->state.aptr.uagent =\n   665\t      aprintf(\"User-Agent: %s\\r\\n\", data->set.str[STRING_USERAGENT]);\n   666\t    if(!data->state.aptr.uagent)\n   667\t      return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n   668\t  }\n   669\t\n   670\t  if(data->set.str[STRING_USERNAME] ||\n   671\t     data->set.str[STRING_PASSWORD])\n   672\t    data->state.creds_from = CREDS_OPTION;\n   673\t  if(!result)\n   674\t    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->state.aptr.user,\n   675\t                            data->set.str[STRING_USERNAME]);\n   676\t  if(!result)\n   677\t    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->state.aptr.passwd,\n   678\t                            data->set.str[STRING_PASSWORD]);\n   679\t#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n   680\t  if(!result)\n   681\t    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->state.aptr.proxyuser,\n   682\t                            data->set.str[STRING_PROXYUSERNAME]);\n   683\t  if(!result)\n   684\t    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->state.aptr.proxypasswd,\n   685\t                            data->set.str[STRING_PROXYPASSWORD]);\n   686\t#endif\n   687\t\n   688\t  data->req.headerbytecount = 0;\n   689\t  Curl_headers_cleanup(data);\n   690\t  return result;\n   691\t}\n   692\t\n   693\t/* Returns CURLE_OK *and* sets '*url' if a request retry is wanted.\n   694\t\n   695\t   NOTE: that the *url is malloc()ed. */\n   696\tCURLcode Curl_retry_request(struct Curl_easy *data, char **url)\n   697\t{\n   698\t  struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n   699\t  bool retry = FALSE;\n   700\t  *url = NULL;\n   701\t\n   702\t  /* if we are talking upload, we cannot do the checks below, unless the\n   703\t     protocol is HTTP as when uploading over HTTP we will still get a\n   704\t     response */\n   705\t  if(data->state.upload &&\n   706\t     !(conn->handler->protocol&(PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP|CURLPROTO_RTSP)))\n   707\t    return CURLE_OK;\n   708\t\n   709\t  if((data->req.bytecount + data->req.headerbytecount == 0) &&\n   710\t     conn->bits.reuse &&\n   711\t     (!data->req.no_body || (conn->handler->protocol & PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP))\n   712\t#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n   713\t     && (data->set.rtspreq != RTSPREQ_RECEIVE)\n   714\t#endif\n   715\t    )\n   716\t    /* We got no data, we attempted to reuse a connection. For HTTP this\n   717\t       can be a retry so we try again regardless if we expected a body.\n   718\t       For other protocols we only try again only if we expected a body.\n   719\t\n   720\t       This might happen if the connection was left alive when we were\n   721\t       done using it before, but that was closed when we wanted to read from\n   722\t       it again. Bad luck. Retry the same request on a fresh connect! */\n   723\t    retry = TRUE;\n   724\t  else if(data->state.refused_stream &&\n   725\t          (data->req.bytecount + data->req.headerbytecount == 0) ) {\n   726\t    /* This was sent on a refused stream, safe to rerun. A refused stream\n   727\t       error can typically only happen on HTTP/2 level if the stream is safe\n   728\t       to issue again, but the nghttp2 API can deliver the message to other\n   729\t       streams as well, which is why this adds the check the data counters\n   730\t       too. */\n   731\t    infof(data, \"REFUSED_STREAM, retrying a fresh connect\");\n   732\t    data->state.refused_stream = FALSE; /* clear again */\n   733\t    retry = TRUE;\n   734\t  }\n   735\t  if(retry) {\n   736\t#define CONN_MAX_RETRIES 5\n   737\t    if(data->state.retrycount++ >= CONN_MAX_RETRIES) {\n   738\t      failf(data, \"Connection died, tried %d times before giving up\",\n   739\t            CONN_MAX_RETRIES);\n   740\t      data->state.retrycount = 0;\n   741\t      return CURLE_SEND_ERROR;\n   742\t    }\n   743\t    infof(data, \"Connection died, retrying a fresh connect (retry count: %d)\",\n   744\t          data->state.retrycount);\n   745\t    *url = strdup(data->state.url);\n   746\t    if(!*url)\n   747\t      return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n   748\t\n   749\t    connclose(conn, \"retry\"); /* close this connection */\n   750\t    conn->bits.retry = TRUE; /* mark this as a connection we are about\n   751\t                                to retry. Marking it this way should\n   752\t                                prevent i.e HTTP transfers to return\n   753\t                                error just because nothing has been\n   754\t                                transferred! */\n   755\t    Curl_creader_set_rewind(data, TRUE);\n   756\t  }\n   757\t  return CURLE_OK;\n   758\t}\n   759\t\n   760\t/*\n   761\t * xfer_setup() is called to setup basic properties for the transfer.\n   762\t */\n   763\tstatic void xfer_setup(\n   764\t  struct Curl_easy *data,   /* transfer */\n   765\t  int sockindex,            /* socket index to read from or -1 */\n   766\t  curl_off_t size,          /* -1 if unknown at this point */\n   767\t  bool getheader,           /* TRUE if header parsing is wanted */\n   768\t  int writesockindex,       /* socket index to write to, it may be the same we\n   769\t                               read from. -1 disables */\n   770\t  bool shutdown,            /* shutdown connection at transfer end. Only\n   771\t                             * supported when sending OR receiving. */\n   772\t  bool shutdown_err_ignore  /* errors during shutdown do not fail the\n   773\t                             * transfer */\n   774\t  )\n   775\t{\n   776\t  struct SingleRequest *k = &data->req;\n   777\t  struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n   778\t  bool want_send = Curl_req_want_send(data);\n   779\t\n   780\t  DEBUGASSERT(conn != NULL);\n   781\t  DEBUGASSERT((sockindex <= 1) && (sockindex >= -1));\n   782\t  DEBUGASSERT((writesockindex <= 1) && (writesockindex >= -1));\n   783\t  DEBUGASSERT(!shutdown || (sockindex == -1) || (writesockindex == -1));\n   784\t\n   785\t  if(Curl_conn_is_multiplex(conn, FIRSTSOCKET) || want_send) {\n   786\t    /* when multiplexing, the read/write sockets need to be the same! */\n   787\t    conn->sockfd = sockindex == -1 ?\n   788\t      ((writesockindex == -1 ? CURL_SOCKET_BAD : conn->sock[writesockindex])) :\n   789\t      conn->sock[sockindex];\n   790\t    conn->writesockfd = conn->sockfd;\n   791\t    if(want_send)\n   792\t      /* special and HTTP-specific */\n   793\t      writesockindex = FIRSTSOCKET;\n   794\t  }\n   795\t  else {\n   796\t    conn->sockfd = sockindex == -1 ?\n   797\t      CURL_SOCKET_BAD : conn->sock[sockindex];\n   798\t    conn->writesockfd = writesockindex == -1 ?\n   799\t      CURL_SOCKET_BAD : conn->sock[writesockindex];\n   800\t  }"
}

40. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "sed -n '2270,2400p' /workspace/lib/vtls/openssl.c",
  "description": "Read ossl_verifyhost function in openssl.c"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "   In some cases, the URI is specified as an IP address rather than a\n   hostname. In this case, the iPAddress subjectAltName must be present\n   in the certificate and must exactly match the IP in the URI.\n\n   This function is now used from ngtcp2 (QUIC) as well.\n*/\nstatic CURLcode ossl_verifyhost(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                struct connectdata *conn,\n                                struct ssl_peer *peer, X509 *server_cert)\n{\n  bool matched = FALSE;\n  int target; /* target type, GEN_DNS or GEN_IPADD */\n  size_t addrlen = 0;\n  STACK_OF(GENERAL_NAME) *altnames;\n#ifdef USE_IPV6\n  struct in6_addr addr;\n#else\n  struct in_addr addr;\n#endif\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  bool dNSName = FALSE; /* if a dNSName field exists in the cert */\n  bool iPAddress = FALSE; /* if an iPAddress field exists in the cert */\n  size_t hostlen;\n\n  (void)conn;\n  hostlen = strlen(peer->hostname);\n  switch(peer->type) {\n  case CURL_SSL_PEER_IPV4:\n    if(!Curl_inet_pton(AF_INET, peer->hostname, &addr))\n      return CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION;\n    target = GEN_IPADD;\n    addrlen = sizeof(struct in_addr);\n    break;\n#ifdef USE_IPV6\n  case CURL_SSL_PEER_IPV6:\n    if(!Curl_inet_pton(AF_INET6, peer->hostname, &addr))\n      return CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION;\n    target = GEN_IPADD;\n    addrlen = sizeof(struct in6_addr);\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURL_SSL_PEER_DNS:\n    target = GEN_DNS;\n    break;\n  default:\n    DEBUGASSERT(0);\n    failf(data, \"unexpected ssl peer type: %d\", peer->type);\n    return CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION;\n  }\n\n  /* get a \"list\" of alternative names */\n  altnames = X509_get_ext_d2i(server_cert, NID_subject_alt_name, NULL, NULL);\n\n  if(altnames) {\n#if defined(OPENSSL_IS_BORINGSSL) || defined(OPENSSL_IS_AWSLC)\n    size_t numalts;\n    size_t i;\n#else\n    int numalts;\n    int i;\n#endif\n    bool dnsmatched = FALSE;\n    bool ipmatched = FALSE;\n\n    /* get amount of alternatives, RFC2459 claims there MUST be at least\n       one, but we do not depend on it... */\n    numalts = sk_GENERAL_NAME_num(altnames);\n\n    /* loop through all alternatives - until a dnsmatch */\n    for(i = 0; (i < numalts) && !dnsmatched; i++) {\n      /* get a handle to alternative name number i */\n      const GENERAL_NAME *check = sk_GENERAL_NAME_value(altnames, i);\n\n      if(check->type == GEN_DNS)\n        dNSName = TRUE;\n      else if(check->type == GEN_IPADD)\n        iPAddress = TRUE;\n\n      /* only check alternatives of the same type the target is */\n      if(check->type == target) {\n        /* get data and length */\n        const char *altptr = (char *)ASN1_STRING_get0_data(check->d.ia5);\n        size_t altlen = (size_t) ASN1_STRING_length(check->d.ia5);\n\n        switch(target) {\n        case GEN_DNS: /* name/pattern comparison */\n          /* The OpenSSL manpage explicitly says: \"In general it cannot be\n             assumed that the data returned by ASN1_STRING_data() is null\n             terminated or does not contain embedded nulls.\" But also that\n             \"The actual format of the data will depend on the actual string\n             type itself: for example for an IA5String the data will be ASCII\"\n\n             It has been however verified that in 0.9.6 and 0.9.7, IA5String\n             is always null-terminated.\n          */\n          if((altlen == strlen(altptr)) &&\n             /* if this is not true, there was an embedded zero in the name\n                string and we cannot match it. */\n             subj_alt_hostcheck(data, altptr, altlen,\n                                peer->hostname, hostlen,\n                                peer->dispname)) {\n            dnsmatched = TRUE;\n          }\n          break;\n\n        case GEN_IPADD: /* IP address comparison */\n          /* compare alternative IP address if the data chunk is the same size\n             our server IP address is */\n          if((altlen == addrlen) && !memcmp(altptr, &addr, altlen)) {\n            ipmatched = TRUE;\n            infof(data,\n                  \" subjectAltName: host \\\"%s\\\" matched cert's IP address!\",\n                  peer->dispname);\n          }\n          break;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    GENERAL_NAMES_free(altnames);\n\n    if(dnsmatched || ipmatched)\n      matched = TRUE;\n  }\n\n  if(matched)\n    /* an alternative name matched */\n    ;\n  else if(dNSName || iPAddress) {\n    const char *tname = (peer->type == CURL_SSL_PEER_DNS) ? \"hostname\" :\n                        (peer->type == CURL_SSL_PEER_IPV4) ?"
}
41. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "sed -n '2390,2500p' /workspace/lib/vtls/openssl.c",
  "description": "Read ossl_verifyhost continuation"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "    if(dnsmatched || ipmatched)\n      matched = TRUE;\n  }\n\n  if(matched)\n    /* an alternative name matched */\n    ;\n  else if(dNSName || iPAddress) {\n    const char *tname = (peer->type == CURL_SSL_PEER_DNS) ? \"hostname\" :\n                        (peer->type == CURL_SSL_PEER_IPV4) ?\n                        \"ipv4 address\" : \"ipv6 address\";\n    infof(data, \" subjectAltName does not match %s %s\", tname, peer->dispname);\n    failf(data, \"SSL: no alternative certificate subject name matches \"\n          \"target %s '%s'\", tname, peer->dispname);\n    result = CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION;\n  }\n  else {\n    /* we have to look to the last occurrence of a commonName in the\n       distinguished one to get the most significant one. */\n    int i = -1;\n    unsigned char *cn = NULL;\n    int cnlen = 0;\n    bool free_cn = FALSE;\n\n    /* The following is done because of a bug in 0.9.6b */\n    X509_NAME *name = X509_get_subject_name(server_cert);\n    if(name) {\n      int j;\n      while((j = X509_NAME_get_index_by_NID(name, NID_commonName, i)) >= 0)\n        i = j;\n    }\n\n    /* we have the name entry and we will now convert this to a string\n       that we can use for comparison. Doing this we support BMPstring,\n       UTF8, etc. */\n\n    if(i >= 0) {\n      ASN1_STRING *tmp =\n        X509_NAME_ENTRY_get_data(X509_NAME_get_entry(name, i));\n\n      /* In OpenSSL 0.9.7d and earlier, ASN1_STRING_to_UTF8 fails if the input\n         is already UTF-8 encoded. We check for this case and copy the raw\n         string manually to avoid the problem. This code can be made\n         conditional in the future when OpenSSL has been fixed. */\n      if(tmp) {\n        if(ASN1_STRING_type(tmp) == V_ASN1_UTF8STRING) {\n          cnlen = ASN1_STRING_length(tmp);\n          cn = (unsigned char *) ASN1_STRING_get0_data(tmp);\n        }\n        else { /* not a UTF8 name */\n          cnlen = ASN1_STRING_to_UTF8(&cn, tmp);\n          free_cn = TRUE;\n        }\n\n        if((cnlen <= 0) || !cn)\n          result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n        else if((size_t)cnlen != strlen((char *)cn)) {\n          /* there was a terminating zero before the end of string, this\n             cannot match and we return failure! */\n          failf(data, \"SSL: illegal cert name field\");\n          result = CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    if(result)\n      /* error already detected, pass through */\n      ;\n    else if(!cn) {\n      failf(data,\n            \"SSL: unable to obtain common name from peer certificate\");\n      result = CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION;\n    }\n    else if(!Curl_cert_hostcheck((const char *)cn, cnlen,\n                                 peer->hostname, hostlen)) {\n      failf(data, \"SSL: certificate subject name '%s' does not match \"\n            \"target hostname '%s'\", cn, peer->dispname);\n      result = CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION;\n    }\n    else {\n      infof(data, \" common name: %s (matched)\", cn);\n    }\n    if(free_cn)\n      OPENSSL_free(cn);\n  }\n\n  return result;\n}\n\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x0090808fL) && !defined(OPENSSL_NO_TLSEXT) && \\\n  !defined(OPENSSL_NO_OCSP)\nstatic CURLcode verifystatus(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                             struct Curl_easy *data,\n                             struct ossl_ctx *octx)\n{\n  int i, ocsp_status;\n#if defined(OPENSSL_IS_AWSLC)\n  const uint8_t *status;\n#else\n  unsigned char *status;\n#endif\n  const unsigned char *p;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  OCSP_RESPONSE *rsp = NULL;\n  OCSP_BASICRESP *br = NULL;\n  X509_STORE     *st = NULL;\n  STACK_OF(X509) *ch = NULL;\n  X509 *cert;\n  OCSP_CERTID *id = NULL;\n  int cert_status, crl_reason;"
}

44. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "sed -n '987,1010p' /workspace/lib/vtls/openssl.c",
  "description": "Read passwd_callback function in openssl.c"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "static int passwd_callback(char *buf, int num, int encrypting,\n                           void *global_passwd)\n{\n  DEBUGASSERT(0 == encrypting);\n\n  if(!encrypting && num >= 0) {\n    int klen = curlx_uztosi(strlen((char *)global_passwd));\n    if(num > klen) {\n      memcpy(buf, global_passwd, klen + 1);\n      return klen;\n    }\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * rand_enough() returns TRUE if we have seeded the random engine properly.\n */\nstatic bool rand_enough(void)\n{\n  return 0 != RAND_status();\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode ossl_seed(struct Curl_easy *data)"
}

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"73: static bool cookie_tailmatch(const char *cookie_domain,\\n74:                              size_t cookie_domain_len,\\n75:                              const char *hostname)\\n76: {\\n77:   size_t hostname_len = strlen(hostname);\\n78: \\n79:   if(hostname_len < cookie_domain_len)\\n80:     return FALSE;\\n81: \\n82:   if(!curl_strnequal(cookie_domain,\\n83:                      hostname + hostname_len - cookie_domain_len,\\n84:                      cookie_domain_len))\\n85:     return FALSE;\\n86: \\n87:   /*\\n88:    * A lead char of cookie_domain is not '.'.\\n89:    * RFC6265 4.1.2.3. The Domain Attribute says:\\n90:    * For example, if the value of the Domain attribute is\\n91:    * \\\"example.com\\\", the user agent will include the cookie in the Cookie\\n92:    * header when making HTTP requests to example.com, www.example.com, and\\n93:    * www.corp.example.com.\\n94:    */\\n95:   if(hostname_len == cookie_domain_len)\\n96:     return TRUE;\\n97:   if('.' == *(hostname + hostname_len - cookie_domain_len - 1))\\n98:     return TRUE;\\n99:   return FALSE;\\n100: }\\n101: \\n102: /*\\n103:  * matching cookie path and URL path\\n104:  * RFC6265 5.1.4 Paths and Path-Match\\n105:  */\\n106: static bool pathmatch(const char *cookie_path, const char *uri_path)\\n107: {\\n108:   size_t cookie_path_len;\\n109:   size_t uri_path_len;\\n110:   bool ret = FALSE;\\n111: \\n112:   /* cookie_path must not have last '/' separator. ex: /sample */\\n113:   cookie_path_len = strlen(cookie_path);\\n114:   if(cookie_path_len == 1) {\\n115:     /* cookie_path must be '/' */\\n116: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl@4497dbd9ac4a31b0056a9179d500676ebe091de5/-/blob/lib/cookie.c%3FL73-115\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "73: static bool cookie_tailmatch(const char *cookie_domain,\n74:                              size_t cookie_domain_len,\n75:                              const char *hostname)\n76: {\n77:   size_t hostname_len = strlen(hostname);\n78: \n79:   if(hostname_len < cookie_domain_len)\n80:     return FALSE;\n81: \n82:   if(!curl_strnequal(cookie_domain,\n83:                      hostname + hostname_len - cookie_domain_len,\n84:                      cookie_domain_len))\n85:     return FALSE;\n86: \n87:   /*\n88:    * A lead char of cookie_domain is not '.'.\n89:    * RFC6265 4.1.2.3. The Domain Attribute says:\n90:    * For example, if the value of the Domain attribute is\n91:    * \"example.com\", the user agent will include the cookie in the Cookie\n92:    * header when making HTTP requests to example.com, www.example.com, and\n93:    * www.corp.example.com.\n94:    */\n95:   if(hostname_len == cookie_domain_len)\n96:     return TRUE;\n97:   if('.' == *(hostname + hostname_len - cookie_domain_len - 1))\n98:     return TRUE;\n99:   return FALSE;\n100: }\n101: \n102: /*\n103:  * matching cookie path and URL path\n104:  * RFC6265 5.1.4 Paths and Path-Match\n105:  */\n106: static bool pathmatch(const char *cookie_path, const char *uri_path)\n107: {\n108:   size_t cookie_path_len;\n109:   size_t uri_path_len;\n110:   bool ret = FALSE;\n111: \n112:   /* cookie_path must not have last '/' separator. ex: /sample */\n113:   cookie_path_len = strlen(cookie_path);\n114:   if(cookie_path_len == 1) {\n115:     /* cookie_path must be '/' */\n116: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl@4497dbd9ac4a31b0056a9179d500676ebe091de5/-/blob/lib/cookie.c%3FL73-115"
