    "github.com/curl/curl"
  ]
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"commits\":[{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"a78a07d\",\"date\":\"2025-12-07\",\"message\":\"cookie: cleanups and improvements\\n\\n- Stricter cookie validation with earlier rejection of empty/invalid\\n  cookie names\\n\\n- secure and httponly attributes no longer accept = with empty values\\n  (only bare keywords)\\n\\n- Validation checks (length, TAB, prefixes) moved into the first\\n  name/value pair block for better code organization\\n\\n- Deferred time(NULL) calls for better performance when expires/max-age\\n  aren't used\\n\\n- Simplified loop control flow by removing done flag\\n\\n- The cookie size restriction now only applies to name + value, not other\\n  parts of the header line.\\n\\n- Fixed a gcc 4.8.1 qui... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: cleanups and improvements\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a78a07d3a9dc808a51f464d0dd297939e544e2a4\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"193cb00\",\"date\":\"2025-10-08\",\"message\":\"build: stop overriding standard memory allocation functions\\n\\nBefore this patch curl used the C preprocessor to override standard\\nmemory allocation symbols: malloc, calloc, strdup, realloc, free.\\nThe goal of these is to replace them with curl's debug wrappers in\\n`CURLDEBUG` builds, another was to replace them with the wrappers\\ncalling user-defined allocators in libcurl. This solution needed a bunch\\nof workarounds to avoid breaking external headers: it relied on include\\norder to do the overriding last. For \\\"unity\\\" builds it needed to reset\\noverrides before external includes. Also in test apps, w... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: stop overriding standard memory allocation functions\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/193cb00ce9b47e75d42157c650cc3de3fd96d35d\"},{\"author\":\"Jay Satiro <raysatiro@yahoo.com>\",\"commit\":\"39e2179\",\"date\":\"2024-12-24\",\"message\":\"cookie: fix crash in netscape cookie parsing\\n\\n- Parse the input string without modifying it.\\n\\nPrior to this change a segfault could occur if the input string was\\nconst because the tokenizer modified the input string. For example if\\nthe user set CURLOPT_COOKIELIST to a const string then libcurl would\\nlikely cause a crash when modifying that string. Even if the string was\\nnot const or a crash did not occur there was still the incorrect and\\nunexpected modification of the user's input string.\\n\\nThis issue was caused by 30da1f59 (precedes 8.11.0) which refactored\\nsome options parsing and eliminated ... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: fix crash in netscape cookie parsing\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/39e21794a78dc111b3bee7ad916a4dc34c40d32f\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"18af4e4\",\"date\":\"2025-12-25\",\"message\":\"cookie: flush better\\n\\nThe cookie flushing (saving to a cookie jar) should only be done if a\\ntransfer has been started. This is now done by checking the\\ncookies->running field, which is not reset in curl_easy_reset() so the\\nsaving works correctly even after a call to that.\\n\\nFollow-up to fd6eb8d6e77d95e71c\\n\\nVerified by test 1920\\n\\nReported-by: Alexander Batischev\\nFixes #20090\\nCloses #20094\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: flush better\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/18af4e4e109f3825d1891c32aacb064fdd7e9dc4\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"784a8ec\",\"date\":\"2025-02-09\",\"message\":\"tidy-up: delete, comment or scope C macros reported unused\\n\\nTo reduce the number `-Wunused-macro` compiler warnings:\\n- delete unused macros.\\n- comment out unused macro that are part of a set.\\n- move macros into the scope they are used.\\n\\nThis may be useful to enable by default, but there are tricky cases that\\nI didn't manage to fix and paused the effort. E.g. internal features\\nchecks in `openssl.c`. There is more, once those are fixed.\\n\\nCloses #16279\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tidy-up: delete, comment or scope C macros reported unused\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/784a8ec2c1a3cc4bd676077a28a0d5f6ee7786a5\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"2dc71ba\",\"date\":\"2025-11-14\",\"message\":\"badwords: check indented lines in source code, fix fallouts\\n\\n- badwords.pl: add `-a` option to check all lines in source code files.\\n  Before this patch indented lines were skipped (to avoid Markdown code\\n  fences.)\\n- GHA/checksrc: use `-a` when verifying the source code.\\n- GHA/checksrc: disable `So` and `But` rules for source code.\\n- GHA/checksrc: add docs/examples to the verified sources.\\n- badwords.txt: delete 4 duplicates.\\n- badwords.txt: group and sort contractions.\\n- badwords.txt: allow ` url = `, `DIR`, `<file name`.\\n\\nCloses #19536\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"badwords: check indented lines in source code, fix fallouts\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/2dc71ba8bf70fa8d7e345cb0baeb4a4ea804994e\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"6d0ee7b\",\"date\":\"2025-12-20\",\"message\":\"curlx: add `curlx_rename()`, fix to support long filenames on Windows\\n\\nMove existing `Curl_rename()` `rename()` wrapper from lib to\\ncurlx/fopen, and make it a curlx macro/function. To allow using\\nthe local worker function to fixup long filenames on Windows.\\n\\nThen fix the Windows-specific rename implementation to support long\\nfilenames. This operation may happen when using a cookie jar, HSTS cache\\nor alt-svc cache, via libcurl or the curl tool.\\n\\nBefore this patch, when passing a long filename to the above options,\\na `<random>.tmp` file was left on the disk without renaming it to the\\nfilename pa... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"curlx: add `curlx_rename()`, fix to support long filenames on Windows\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/6d0ee7b17b0c37c034a78314eda4148104187689\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"1e1ec7f\",\"date\":\"2025-11-17\",\"message\":\"badwords: add more contractions, fix fallouts\\n\\nAlso fix hits in autotools scripts (not to enforce).\\n\\nCloses #19576\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"badwords: add more contractions, fix fallouts\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/1e1ec7f6c2864abef038822077e5e6a7f4e47cbb\"},{\"author\":\"Terence Eden <edent@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"a042c67\",\"date\":\"2025-02-08\",\"message\":\"docs: use valid example domain names\\n\\nReplace .site domains and domain.com with valid example domains.\\n\\nFixes #16269\\nCloses #16270\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"docs: use valid example domain names\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a042c67df3845b55817ee03635e558768868aad4\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"b12da22\",\"date\":\"2025-10-04\",\"message\":\"lib: stop overriding system printf symbols\\n\\nAfter this patch, the codebase no longer overrides system printf\\nfunctions. Instead it explicitly calls either the curl printf functions\\n`curl_m*printf()` or the system ones using their original names.\\n\\nAlso:\\n- drop unused `curl_printf.h` includes.\\n- checksrc: ban system printf functions, allow where necessary.\\n\\nFollow-up to db98daab05aec251bcb6615d2d38dfebec291736 #18844\\nFollow-up to 4deea9396bc7dd25c6362fa746a57bf309c74ada #18814\\n\\nCloses #18866\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: stop overriding system printf symbols\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/b12da22db1f11da51082977dc21a7edee7858911\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"fd6eb8d\",\"date\":\"2025-09-19\",\"message\":\"cookie: avoid saving a cookie file if no transfer was done\\n\\nBecause parts of the cookie loading happens on transfer start the\\nin-memory cookie jar risks being incomplete and then a save might\\nwrongly truncate the target file.\\n\\nAdded test 1902 to verify.\\n\\nReported-by: divinity76 on github\\nFixes #18621\\nCloses #18622\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: avoid saving a cookie file if no transfer was done\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/fd6eb8d6e77d95e71c0c55678b46173b21edd1e9\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"86b3464\",\"date\":\"2025-11-27\",\"message\":\"lib: fix formatting nits (part 2)\\n\\nFrom `lib/curl*` to `lib/g*`. With fixes to part 1.\\n\\npart 1: 47a1ab2ebecb21485c0e955316d90511e80a3c43 #19764\\n\\nCloses #19800\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: fix formatting nits (part 2)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/86b346443b68cde7ef33e1ab770e6c8ab641d2b1\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"386f570\",\"date\":\"2025-01-08\",\"message\":\"cookie: cap expire times to 400 days\\n\\nThe pending cookie RFC update (currently known as 6265bis draft-19) says\\n\\n  Let cookie-age-limit be the maximum age of the cookie (which name of\\n  Max-Age and an attribute-value of expiry-time. SHOULD be 400 days or\\n  less.\\n\\nThis change makes received cookies over the wire get capped to 400 days.\\n\\nIt does not cap the expiry date of cookies loaded from file.\\n\\nIt does this by rounding the expire time to a even minute. This, to\\nallow the test suite to do the same and have a chance to get the same\\nnumber for stable testing without requiring a debug build.\\n\\nThe... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: cap expire times to 400 days\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/386f570df60f58fe9e90dddbda2fdd822a318f04\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"31a4f41\",\"date\":\"2026-02-02\",\"message\":\"build: tidy up and dedupe `strdup` functions\\n\\n- de-dupe lib/src strdup/memdup functions into curlx.\\n- introduce `CURLX_STRDUP_LOW()` for mapping `strdup()`, and to do it at\\n  one place within the code, in `curl_setup.h`.\\n- tests/server: use `curlx_strdup()`. (Also to fix building without\\n  a system `strdup()`.)\\n- curlx/curlx.h: shorten and tidy up.\\n- adjust Windows build path to not need `HAVE_STRDUP`.\\n- build: stop detecting `HAVE_STRDUP` on Windows.\\n\\nCloses #20497\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: tidy up and dedupe `strdup` functions\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/31a4f415af112351ad54e59b2d6b03df4f4cb187\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"9308ea2\",\"date\":\"2025-10-28\",\"message\":\"cookie: remove the temporary file on (all) errors\\n\\nFixes #19267\\nReported-by: Harry Sintonen\\nCloses #19268\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: remove the temporary file on (all) errors\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/9308ea22e8299fb79f4acd0398829cbe37a3fb1c\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"5a26b90\",\"date\":\"2025-03-13\",\"message\":\"cookie: accept \\\"high byte\\\" cookie content\\n\\nRegression from 597ee915c4c6da132dbd1735345 (not shipped in a release)\\nReported-by: Carlos Henrique Lima Melara\\nAssisted-by: Scott Talbert\\n\\nAdded such a cookie to test 31.\\n\\nFixes #16692\\nCloses #16703\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: accept \\\"high byte\\\" cookie content\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/5a26b901d47f81915381b5ea188d87f173415a58\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"c6e5dfa\",\"date\":\"2025-12-07\",\"message\":\"cookie: allocate the main struct once cookie is fine\\n\\nThis delays the allocating of the cookie struct until after all the\\nchecks have been done, as many cookies are received and discarded\\ninstead of accepted and this then saves one allocation for every\\ndiscarded cookie.\\n\\nCloses #19864\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: allocate the main struct once cookie is fine\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c6e5dfa2c67570f98225959148a6e78c1e2180f2\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"f4e2395\",\"date\":\"2025-01-31\",\"message\":\"build: enable `-Wcast-qual`, fix or silence compiler warnings\\n\\nThe issues found fell into these categories, with the applied fixes:\\n\\n- const was accidentally stripped.\\n  Adjust code to not cast or cast with const.\\n\\n- const/volatile missing from arguments, local variables.\\n  Constify arguments or variables, adjust/delete casts. Small code\\n  changes in a few places.\\n\\n- const must be stripped because an API dependency requires it.\\n  Strip `const` with `CURL_UNCONST()` macro to silence the warning out\\n  of our control. These happen at API boundaries. Sometimes they depend\\n  on dependency version, ... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: enable `-Wcast-qual`, fix or silence compiler warnings\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/f4e23950c7b1c389cf0dde8b91353d85b8361b64\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"3003c32\",\"date\":\"2026-01-23\",\"message\":\"tidy-up: miscellaneous\\n\\n- INSTALL-CMAKE.md: add missing periods, text fixes.\\n- md4, md5: sync variables names.\\n- curl_trc: sync an argument type.\\n- docs/examples: sync debug/trace function copies, constify, tidy-ups.\\n- replace commented code with `#if 0`.\\n- drop redundant parenthesis (macro values, `return`, around single\\n  variables, function calls).\\n- fix indentation, apply clang-format in places.\\n\\nCloses #20481\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tidy-up: miscellaneous\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/3003c32cb29347478b8e2917ca5b3e2b37b4ae33\"},{\"author\":\"Patrick Stoeckle <patrick.stoeckle@siemens.com>\",\"commit\":\"86f43af\",\"date\":\"2025-07-11\",\"message\":\"misc: fix typos\\n\\nJust fixing some typos using: https://github.com/crate-ci/typos\\n\\nCloses #17904\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"misc: fix typos\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/86f43af95122f49b1fb95e0ad42955af431cae5b\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"5b5e2f7\",\"date\":\"2025-02-26\",\"message\":\"cookie: do prefix matching case-sensitively\\n\\nAccording to section 4.1.3.1 and 4.1.3.2 of\\ndraft-ietf-httpbis-rfc6265bis-19\\n\\nRef: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-httpbis-rfc6265bis-19#section-4.1.3.1\\n\\nCloses #16494\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: do prefix matching case-sensitively\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/5b5e2f7318bff3244def7c5865db47657dcf877b\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"255aac5\",\"date\":\"2025-05-07\",\"message\":\"curlx: move into to curlx/\\n\\nMove curlx_ functions into its own subdir.\\n\\nThe idea is to use the curlx_ prefix proper on these functions, and use\\nthese same function names both in tool, lib and test suite source code.\\nStop the previous special #define setup for curlx_ names.\\n\\nThe printf defines are now done for the library alone. Tests no longer\\nuse the printf defines. The tool code sets its own defines. The printf\\nfunctions are not curlx, they are publicly available.\\n\\nThe strcase defines are not curlx_ functions and should not be used by\\ntool or server code.\\n\\ndynbuf, warnless, base64, strparse,... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"curlx: move into to curlx/\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/255aac56f9773d7c138816bfe0390293f7da33a5\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"7032982\",\"date\":\"2025-12-20\",\"message\":\"tidy-up: miscellaneous\\n\\n- asyn-thrdd.c: scope an include.\\n- apply more clang-format suggestions.\\n- tidy-up PP guard comments.\\n- delete empty line from the top of headers.\\n- add empty line after `curl_setup.h` include where missing.\\n- fix indent.\\n- CODE_STYLE.md: add `strcpy`.\\n  Follow-up to 8636ad55dfbdbcc2809a61e65c3511baf5e7b0e8 #20088\\n- lib1901.c: drop unnecessary line.\\n  Follow-up to 436e67f65b9dee1e80aa063c39450f0d1df6ac72 #20076\\n\\nCloses #20070\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tidy-up: miscellaneous\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/70329828961a7de194c4c258bd5825afb1075888\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"0064708\",\"date\":\"2025-02-17\",\"message\":\"cookie: minor parser simplification\\n\\n- parse whitespace before the value is handled\\n- remove superflous checks from some ISBLANK() loops\\n\\nCloses #16362\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: minor parser simplification\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/0064708f3f1879e0982f5486ebae25e28bee27d8\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"a8c852b\",\"date\":\"2024-12-10\",\"message\":\"cookie: parse only the exact expire date\\n\\nThe date parser function is very forgiving and skips most \\\"irrelevant\\\"\\ncharacters in its hunt for a date to figure out. Therefore it is\\nimportant to make sure the date string is properly null terminated so\\nthat it does not accidentally parse a piece of whatever text follows\\nafter the date.\\n\\nAdd test483: test (overly) long expire dates in cookies\\n\\nCloses #15709\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: parse only the exact expire date\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a8c852b9a5e4f92e13e0dc23a13ac3520d822c69\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"47a1ab2\",\"date\":\"2025-11-29\",\"message\":\"lib: fix formatting nits (part 1)\\n\\nFrom `lib/a*` to `lib/cs*`.\\n\\nCloses #19764\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: fix formatting nits (part 1)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/47a1ab2ebecb21485c0e955316d90511e80a3c43\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"9919149\",\"date\":\"2024-11-06\",\"message\":\"cookie: treat cookie name case sensitively\\n\\nExtend test 31 to verify\\n\\nReported-by: delogicsreal on github\\nFixes #15492\\nCloses #15493\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: treat cookie name case sensitively\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/9919149aef67014150e2a1c75a7aa2c79204e30d\"}],\"limitHit\":false,\"query\":\"type:commit (message:fix OR message:crash OR message:overflow OR message:null OR message:check) file:lib/cookie.c repo:github.com/curl/curl after:2024-10-01 count:100\",\"totalCount\":27}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "commits": [
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "a78a07d",
        "date": "2025-12-07",
        "message": "cookie: cleanups and improvements\n\n- Stricter cookie validation with earlier rejection of empty/invalid\n  cookie names\n\n- secure and httponly attributes no longer accept = with empty values\n  (only bare keywords)\n\n- Validation checks (length, TAB, prefixes) moved into the first\n  name/value pair block for better code organization\n\n- Deferred time(NULL) calls for better performance when expires/max-age\n  aren't used\n\n- Simplified loop control flow by removing done flag\n\n- The cookie size restriction now only applies to name + value, not other\n  parts of the header line.\n\n- Fixed a gcc 4.8.1 qui... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: cleanups and improvements",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a78a07d3a9dc808a51f464d0dd297939e544e2a4"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "193cb00",
        "date": "2025-10-08",
        "message": "build: stop overriding standard memory allocation functions\n\nBefore this patch curl used the C preprocessor to override standard\nmemory allocation symbols: malloc, calloc, strdup, realloc, free.\nThe goal of these is to replace them with curl's debug wrappers in\n`CURLDEBUG` builds, another was to replace them with the wrappers\ncalling user-defined allocators in libcurl. This solution needed a bunch\nof workarounds to avoid breaking external headers: it relied on include\norder to do the overriding last. For \"unity\" builds it needed to reset\noverrides before external includes. Also in test apps, w... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: stop overriding standard memory allocation functions",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/193cb00ce9b47e75d42157c650cc3de3fd96d35d"
      },
      {
        "author": "Jay Satiro <raysatiro@yahoo.com>",
        "commit": "39e2179",
        "date": "2024-12-24",
        "message": "cookie: fix crash in netscape cookie parsing\n\n- Parse the input string without modifying it.\n\nPrior to this change a segfault could occur if the input string was\nconst because the tokenizer modified the input string. For example if\nthe user set CURLOPT_COOKIELIST to a const string then libcurl would\nlikely cause a crash when modifying that string. Even if the string was\nnot const or a crash did not occur there was still the incorrect and\nunexpected modification of the user's input string.\n\nThis issue was caused by 30da1f59 (precedes 8.11.0) which refactored\nsome options parsing and eliminated ... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: fix crash in netscape cookie parsing",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/39e21794a78dc111b3bee7ad916a4dc34c40d32f"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "18af4e4",
        "date": "2025-12-25",
        "message": "cookie: flush better\n\nThe cookie flushing (saving to a cookie jar) should only be done if a\ntransfer has been started. This is now done by checking the\ncookies->running field, which is not reset in curl_easy_reset() so the\nsaving works correctly even after a call to that.\n\nFollow-up to fd6eb8d6e77d95e71c\n\nVerified by test 1920\n\nReported-by: Alexander Batischev\nFixes #20090\nCloses #20094",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: flush better",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/18af4e4e109f3825d1891c32aacb064fdd7e9dc4"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "784a8ec",
        "date": "2025-02-09",
        "message": "tidy-up: delete, comment or scope C macros reported unused\n\nTo reduce the number `-Wunused-macro` compiler warnings:\n- delete unused macros.\n- comment out unused macro that are part of a set.\n- move macros into the scope they are used.\n\nThis may be useful to enable by default, but there are tricky cases that\nI didn't manage to fix and paused the effort. E.g. internal features\nchecks in `openssl.c`. There is more, once those are fixed.\n\nCloses #16279",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tidy-up: delete, comment or scope C macros reported unused",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/784a8ec2c1a3cc4bd676077a28a0d5f6ee7786a5"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "2dc71ba",
        "date": "2025-11-14",
        "message": "badwords: check indented lines in source code, fix fallouts\n\n- badwords.pl: add `-a` option to check all lines in source code files.\n  Before this patch indented lines were skipped (to avoid Markdown code\n  fences.)\n- GHA/checksrc: use `-a` when verifying the source code.\n- GHA/checksrc: disable `So` and `But` rules for source code.\n- GHA/checksrc: add docs/examples to the verified sources.\n- badwords.txt: delete 4 duplicates.\n- badwords.txt: group and sort contractions.\n- badwords.txt: allow ` url = `, `DIR`, `<file name`.\n\nCloses #19536",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "badwords: check indented lines in source code, fix fallouts",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/2dc71ba8bf70fa8d7e345cb0baeb4a4ea804994e"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "6d0ee7b",
        "date": "2025-12-20",
        "message": "curlx: add `curlx_rename()`, fix to support long filenames on Windows\n\nMove existing `Curl_rename()` `rename()` wrapper from lib to\ncurlx/fopen, and make it a curlx macro/function. To allow using\nthe local worker function to fixup long filenames on Windows.\n\nThen fix the Windows-specific rename implementation to support long\nfilenames. This operation may happen when using a cookie jar, HSTS cache\nor alt-svc cache, via libcurl or the curl tool.\n\nBefore this patch, when passing a long filename to the above options,\na `<random>.tmp` file was left on the disk without renaming it to the\nfilename pa... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "curlx: add `curlx_rename()`, fix to support long filenames on Windows",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/6d0ee7b17b0c37c034a78314eda4148104187689"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "1e1ec7f",
        "date": "2025-11-17",
        "message": "badwords: add more contractions, fix fallouts\n\nAlso fix hits in autotools scripts (not to enforce).\n\nCloses #19576",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "badwords: add more contractions, fix fallouts",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/1e1ec7f6c2864abef038822077e5e6a7f4e47cbb"
      },
      {
        "author": "Terence Eden <edent@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "a042c67",
        "date": "2025-02-08",
        "message": "docs: use valid example domain names\n\nReplace .site domains and domain.com with valid example domains.\n\nFixes #16269\nCloses #16270",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "docs: use valid example domain names",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a042c67df3845b55817ee03635e558768868aad4"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "b12da22",
        "date": "2025-10-04",
        "message": "lib: stop overriding system printf symbols\n\nAfter this patch, the codebase no longer overrides system printf\nfunctions. Instead it explicitly calls either the curl printf functions\n`curl_m*printf()` or the system ones using their original names.\n\nAlso:\n- drop unused `curl_printf.h` includes.\n- checksrc: ban system printf functions, allow where necessary.\n\nFollow-up to db98daab05aec251bcb6615d2d38dfebec291736 #18844\nFollow-up to 4deea9396bc7dd25c6362fa746a57bf309c74ada #18814\n\nCloses #18866",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: stop overriding system printf symbols",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/b12da22db1f11da51082977dc21a7edee7858911"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "fd6eb8d",
        "date": "2025-09-19",
        "message": "cookie: avoid saving a cookie file if no transfer was done\n\nBecause parts of the cookie loading happens on transfer start the\nin-memory cookie jar risks being incomplete and then a save might\nwrongly truncate the target file.\n\nAdded test 1902 to verify.\n\nReported-by: divinity76 on github\nFixes #18621\nCloses #18622",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: avoid saving a cookie file if no transfer was done",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/fd6eb8d6e77d95e71c0c55678b46173b21edd1e9"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "86b3464",
        "date": "2025-11-27",
        "message": "lib: fix formatting nits (part 2)\n\nFrom `lib/curl*` to `lib/g*`. With fixes to part 1.\n\npart 1: 47a1ab2ebecb21485c0e955316d90511e80a3c43 #19764\n\nCloses #19800",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: fix formatting nits (part 2)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/86b346443b68cde7ef33e1ab770e6c8ab641d2b1"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "386f570",
        "date": "2025-01-08",
        "message": "cookie: cap expire times to 400 days\n\nThe pending cookie RFC update (currently known as 6265bis draft-19) says\n\n  Let cookie-age-limit be the maximum age of the cookie (which name of\n  Max-Age and an attribute-value of expiry-time. SHOULD be 400 days or\n  less.\n\nThis change makes received cookies over the wire get capped to 400 days.\n\nIt does not cap the expiry date of cookies loaded from file.\n\nIt does this by rounding the expire time to a even minute. This, to\nallow the test suite to do the same and have a chance to get the same\nnumber for stable testing without requiring a debug build.\n\nThe... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: cap expire times to 400 days",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/386f570df60f58fe9e90dddbda2fdd822a318f04"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "31a4f41",
        "date": "2026-02-02",
        "message": "build: tidy up and dedupe `strdup` functions\n\n- de-dupe lib/src strdup/memdup functions into curlx.\n- introduce `CURLX_STRDUP_LOW()` for mapping `strdup()`, and to do it at\n  one place within the code, in `curl_setup.h`.\n- tests/server: use `curlx_strdup()`. (Also to fix building without\n  a system `strdup()`.)\n- curlx/curlx.h: shorten and tidy up.\n- adjust Windows build path to not need `HAVE_STRDUP`.\n- build: stop detecting `HAVE_STRDUP` on Windows.\n\nCloses #20497",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: tidy up and dedupe `strdup` functions",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/31a4f415af112351ad54e59b2d6b03df4f4cb187"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "9308ea2",
        "date": "2025-10-28",
        "message": "cookie: remove the temporary file on (all) errors\n\nFixes #19267\nReported-by: Harry Sintonen\nCloses #19268",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: remove the temporary file on (all) errors",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/9308ea22e8299fb79f4acd0398829cbe37a3fb1c"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "5a26b90",
        "date": "2025-03-13",
        "message": "cookie: accept \"high byte\" cookie content\n\nRegression from 597ee915c4c6da132dbd1735345 (not shipped in a release)\nReported-by: Carlos Henrique Lima Melara\nAssisted-by: Scott Talbert\n\nAdded such a cookie to test 31.\n\nFixes #16692\nCloses #16703",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: accept \"high byte\" cookie content",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/5a26b901d47f81915381b5ea188d87f173415a58"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "c6e5dfa",
        "date": "2025-12-07",
        "message": "cookie: allocate the main struct once cookie is fine\n\nThis delays the allocating of the cookie struct until after all the\nchecks have been done, as many cookies are received and discarded\ninstead of accepted and this then saves one allocation for every\ndiscarded cookie.\n\nCloses #19864",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: allocate the main struct once cookie is fine",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c6e5dfa2c67570f98225959148a6e78c1e2180f2"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "f4e2395",
        "date": "2025-01-31",
        "message": "build: enable `-Wcast-qual`, fix or silence compiler warnings\n\nThe issues found fell into these categories, with the applied fixes:\n\n- const was accidentally stripped.\n  Adjust code to not cast or cast with const.\n\n- const/volatile missing from arguments, local variables.\n  Constify arguments or variables, adjust/delete casts. Small code\n  changes in a few places.\n\n- const must be stripped because an API dependency requires it.\n  Strip `const` with `CURL_UNCONST()` macro to silence the warning out\n  of our control. These happen at API boundaries. Sometimes they depend\n  on dependency version, ... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: enable `-Wcast-qual`, fix or silence compiler warnings",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/f4e23950c7b1c389cf0dde8b91353d85b8361b64"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "3003c32",
        "date": "2026-01-23",
        "message": "tidy-up: miscellaneous\n\n- INSTALL-CMAKE.md: add missing periods, text fixes.\n- md4, md5: sync variables names.\n- curl_trc: sync an argument type.\n- docs/examples: sync debug/trace function copies, constify, tidy-ups.\n- replace commented code with `#if 0`.\n- drop redundant parenthesis (macro values, `return`, around single\n  variables, function calls).\n- fix indentation, apply clang-format in places.\n\nCloses #20481",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tidy-up: miscellaneous",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/3003c32cb29347478b8e2917ca5b3e2b37b4ae33"
      },
      {
        "author": "Patrick Stoeckle <patrick.stoeckle@siemens.com>",
        "commit": "86f43af",
        "date": "2025-07-11",
        "message": "misc: fix typos\n\nJust fixing some typos using: https://github.com/crate-ci/typos\n\nCloses #17904",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "misc: fix typos",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/86f43af95122f49b1fb95e0ad42955af431cae5b"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "5b5e2f7",
        "date": "2025-02-26",
        "message": "cookie: do prefix matching case-sensitively\n\nAccording to section 4.1.3.1 and 4.1.3.2 of\ndraft-ietf-httpbis-rfc6265bis-19\n\nRef: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-httpbis-rfc6265bis-19#section-4.1.3.1\n\nCloses #16494",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: do prefix matching case-sensitively",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/5b5e2f7318bff3244def7c5865db47657dcf877b"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "255aac5",
        "date": "2025-05-07",
        "message": "curlx: move into to curlx/\n\nMove curlx_ functions into its own subdir.\n\nThe idea is to use the curlx_ prefix proper on these functions, and use\nthese same function names both in tool, lib and test suite source code.\nStop the previous special #define setup for curlx_ names.\n\nThe printf defines are now done for the library alone. Tests no longer\nuse the printf defines. The tool code sets its own defines. The printf\nfunctions are not curlx, they are publicly available.\n\nThe strcase defines are not curlx_ functions and should not be used by\ntool or server code.\n\ndynbuf, warnless, base64, strparse,... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "curlx: move into to curlx/",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/255aac56f9773d7c138816bfe0390293f7da33a5"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "7032982",
        "date": "2025-12-20",
        "message": "tidy-up: miscellaneous\n\n- asyn-thrdd.c: scope an include.\n- apply more clang-format suggestions.\n- tidy-up PP guard comments.\n- delete empty line from the top of headers.\n- add empty line after `curl_setup.h` include where missing.\n- fix indent.\n- CODE_STYLE.md: add `strcpy`.\n  Follow-up to 8636ad55dfbdbcc2809a61e65c3511baf5e7b0e8 #20088\n- lib1901.c: drop unnecessary line.\n  Follow-up to 436e67f65b9dee1e80aa063c39450f0d1df6ac72 #20076\n\nCloses #20070",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tidy-up: miscellaneous",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/70329828961a7de194c4c258bd5825afb1075888"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "0064708",
        "date": "2025-02-17",
        "message": "cookie: minor parser simplification\n\n- parse whitespace before the value is handled\n- remove superflous checks from some ISBLANK() loops\n\nCloses #16362",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: minor parser simplification",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/0064708f3f1879e0982f5486ebae25e28bee27d8"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "a8c852b",
        "date": "2024-12-10",
        "message": "cookie: parse only the exact expire date\n\nThe date parser function is very forgiving and skips most \"irrelevant\"\ncharacters in its hunt for a date to figure out. Therefore it is\nimportant to make sure the date string is properly null terminated so\nthat it does not accidentally parse a piece of whatever text follows\nafter the date.\n\nAdd test483: test (overly) long expire dates in cookies\n\nCloses #15709",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: parse only the exact expire date",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a8c852b9a5e4f92e13e0dc23a13ac3520d822c69"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "47a1ab2",
        "date": "2025-11-29",
        "message": "lib: fix formatting nits (part 1)\n\nFrom `lib/a*` to `lib/cs*`.\n\nCloses #19764",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: fix formatting nits (part 1)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/47a1ab2ebecb21485c0e955316d90511e80a3c43"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "9919149",
        "date": "2024-11-06",
        "message": "cookie: treat cookie name case sensitively\n\nExtend test 31 to verify\n\nReported-by: delogicsreal on github\nFixes #15492\nCloses #15493",

72. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "sed -n '300,500p' /workspace/lib/cookie.c",
  "description": "Read cookie.c lines 300-500"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": " * cookie path sanitize\n */\nstatic char *sanitize_cookie_path(const char *cookie_path)\n{\n  size_t len;\n  char *new_path = strdup(cookie_path);\n  if(!new_path)\n    return NULL;\n\n  /* some stupid site sends path attribute with '\"'. */\n  len = strlen(new_path);\n  if(new_path[0] == '\\\"') {\n    memmove(new_path, new_path + 1, len);\n    len--;\n  }\n  if(len && (new_path[len - 1] == '\\\"')) {\n    new_path[--len] = 0x0;\n  }\n\n  /* RFC6265 5.2.4 The Path Attribute */\n  if(new_path[0] != '/') {\n    /* Let cookie-path be the default-path. */\n    strstore(&new_path, \"/\", 1);\n    return new_path;\n  }\n\n  /* convert /hoge/ to /hoge */\n  if(len && new_path[len - 1] == '/') {\n    new_path[len - 1] = 0x0;\n  }\n\n  return new_path;\n}\n\n/*\n * Load cookies from all given cookie files (CURLOPT_COOKIEFILE).\n *\n * NOTE: OOM or cookie parsing failures are ignored.\n */\nvoid Curl_cookie_loadfiles(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct curl_slist *list = data->state.cookielist;\n  if(list) {\n    Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n    while(list) {\n      struct CookieInfo *ci =\n        Curl_cookie_init(data, list->data, data->cookies,\n                         data->set.cookiesession);\n      if(!ci)\n        /*\n         * Failure may be due to OOM or a bad cookie; both are ignored\n         * but only the first should be\n         */\n        infof(data, \"ignoring failed cookie_init for %s\", list->data);\n      else\n        data->cookies = ci;\n      list = list->next;\n    }\n    Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE);\n  }\n}\n\n/*\n * strstore\n *\n * A thin wrapper around strdup which ensures that any memory allocated at\n * *str will be freed before the string allocated by strdup is stored there.\n * The intended usecase is repeated assignments to the same variable during\n * parsing in a last-wins scenario. The caller is responsible for checking\n * for OOM errors.\n */\nstatic void strstore(char **str, const char *newstr, size_t len)\n{\n  DEBUGASSERT(newstr);\n  DEBUGASSERT(str);\n  free(*str);\n  *str = Curl_memdup0(newstr, len);\n}\n\n/*\n * remove_expired\n *\n * Remove expired cookies from the hash by inspecting the expires timestamp on\n * each cookie in the hash, freeing and deleting any where the timestamp is in\n * the past. If the cookiejar has recorded the next timestamp at which one or\n * more cookies expire, then processing will exit early in case this timestamp\n * is in the future.\n */\nstatic void remove_expired(struct CookieInfo *ci)\n{\n  struct Cookie *co;\n  curl_off_t now = (curl_off_t)time(NULL);\n  unsigned int i;\n\n  /*\n   * If the earliest expiration timestamp in the jar is in the future we can\n   * skip scanning the whole jar and instead exit early as there will not be\n   * any cookies to evict. If we need to evict however, reset the\n   * next_expiration counter in order to track the next one. In case the\n   * recorded first expiration is the max offset, then perform the safe\n   * fallback of checking all cookies.\n   */\n  if(now < ci->next_expiration &&\n     ci->next_expiration != CURL_OFF_T_MAX)\n    return;\n  else\n    ci->next_expiration = CURL_OFF_T_MAX;\n\n  for(i = 0; i < COOKIE_HASH_SIZE; i++) {\n    struct Curl_llist_node *n;\n    struct Curl_llist_node *e = NULL;\n\n    for(n = Curl_llist_head(&ci->cookielist[i]); n; n = e) {\n      co = Curl_node_elem(n);\n      e = Curl_node_next(n);\n      if(co->expires && co->expires < now) {\n        Curl_node_remove(n);\n        freecookie(co);\n        ci->numcookies--;\n      }\n      else {\n        /*\n         * If this cookie has an expiration timestamp earlier than what we\n         * have seen so far then record it for the next round of expirations.\n         */\n        if(co->expires && co->expires < ci->next_expiration)\n          ci->next_expiration = co->expires;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n#ifndef USE_LIBPSL\n/* Make sure domain contains a dot or is localhost. */\nstatic bool bad_domain(const char *domain, size_t len)\n{\n  if((len == 9) && strncasecompare(domain, \"localhost\", 9))\n    return FALSE;\n  else {\n    /* there must be a dot present, but that dot must not be a trailing dot */\n    char *dot = memchr(domain, '.', len);\n    if(dot) {\n      size_t i = dot - domain;\n      if((len - i) > 1)\n        /* the dot is not the last byte */\n        return FALSE;\n    }\n  }\n  return TRUE;\n}\n#endif\n\n/*\n  RFC 6265 section 4.1.1 says a server should accept this range:\n\n  cookie-octet    = %x21 / %x23-2B / %x2D-3A / %x3C-5B / %x5D-7E\n\n  But Firefox and Chrome as of June 2022 accept space, comma and double-quotes\n  fine. The prime reason for filtering out control bytes is that some HTTP\n  servers return 400 for requests that contain such.\n*/\nstatic bool invalid_octets(const char *p)\n{\n  /* Reject all bytes \\x01 - \\x1f (*except* \\x09, TAB) + \\x7f */\n  static const char badoctets[] = {\n    \"\\x01\\x02\\x03\\x04\\x05\\x06\\x07\\x08\\x0a\"\n    \"\\x0b\\x0c\\x0d\\x0e\\x0f\\x10\\x11\\x12\\x13\\x14\"\n    \"\\x15\\x16\\x17\\x18\\x19\\x1a\\x1b\\x1c\\x1d\\x1e\\x1f\\x7f\"\n  };\n  size_t len;\n  /* scan for all the octets that are *not* in cookie-octet */\n  len = strcspn(p, badoctets);\n  return p[len] != '\\0';\n}\n\n#define CERR_OK            0\n#define CERR_TOO_LONG      1 /* input line too long */\n#define CERR_TAB           2 /* in a wrong place */\n#define CERR_TOO_BIG       3 /* name/value too large */\n#define CERR_BAD           4 /* deemed incorrect */\n#define CERR_NO_SEP        5 /* semicolon problem */\n#define CERR_NO_NAME_VALUE 6 /* name or value problem */\n#define CERR_INVALID_OCTET 7 /* bad content */\n#define CERR_BAD_SECURE    8 /* secure in a bad place */\n#define CERR_OUT_OF_MEMORY 9\n#define CERR_NO_TAILMATCH  10\n#define CERR_COMMENT       11 /* a commented line */\n#define CERR_RANGE         12 /* expire range problem */\n#define CERR_FIELDS        13 /* incomplete netscape line */\n#define CERR_PSL           14 /* a public suffix */\n#define CERR_LIVE_WINS     15\n\n/* The maximum length we accept a date string for the 'expire' keyword. The\n   standard date formats are within the 30 bytes range. This adds an extra\n   margin just to make sure it realistically works with what is used out\n   there.\n*/\n#define MAX_DATE_LENGTH 80\n\nstatic int\nparse_cookie_header(struct Curl_easy *data,"
}

FIRSTSOCKET : -1;\n   839\t  int send_index = (send_recv & CURL_XFER_SEND) ? FIRSTSOCKET : -1;\n   840\t  DEBUGASSERT((recv_index >= 0) || (recv_size == -1));\n   841\t  xfer_setup(data, recv_index, recv_size, getheader, send_index, FALSE, FALSE);\n   842\t}\n   843\t\n   844\tvoid Curl_xfer_setup2(struct Curl_easy *data,\n   845\t                      int send_recv,\n   846\t                      curl_off_t recv_size,\n   847\t                      bool shutdown,\n   848\t                      bool shutdown_err_ignore)\n   849\t{\n   850\t  int recv_index = (send_recv & CURL_XFER_RECV) ? shutdown_err_ignore);\n   855\t}\n   856\t\n   857\tCURLcode Curl_xfer_write_resp(struct Curl_easy *data,\n   858\t                              const char *buf, size_t blen,\n   859\t                              bool is_eos)\n   860\t{\n   861\t  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n   862\t\n   863\t  if(data->conn->handler->write_resp) {\n   864\t    /* protocol handlers offering this function take full responsibility\n   865\t     * for writing all received download data to the client. */\n   866\t    result = data->conn->handler->write_resp(data, buf, blen, is_eos);\n   867\t  }\n   868\t  else {\n   869\t    /* No special handling by protocol handler, write all received data\n   870\t     * as BODY to the client. */\n   871\t    if(blen || is_eos) {\n   872\t      int cwtype = CLIENTWRITE_BODY;\n   873\t      if(is_eos)\n   874\t        cwtype |= CLIENTWRITE_EOS;\n   875\t      result = Curl_client_write(data, cwtype, buf, blen);\n   876\t    }\n   877\t  }\n   878\t\n   879\t  if(!result && is_eos) {\n   880\t    /* If we wrote the EOS, we are definitely done */\n   881\t    data->req.eos_written = TRUE;\n   882\t    data->req.download_done = TRUE;\n   883\t  }\n   884\t  CURL_TRC_WRITE(data, \"xfer_write_resp(len=%zu, eos=%d) -> %d\",\n   885\t                 blen, is_eos, result);\n   886\t  return result;\n   887\t}\n   888\t\n   889\tCURLcode Curl_xfer_write_resp_hd(struct Curl_easy *data,\n   890\t                                 const char *hd0, size_t hdlen, bool is_eos)\n   891\t{\n   892\t  if(data->conn->handler->write_resp_hd) {\n   893\t    /* protocol handlers offering this function take full responsibility\n   894\t     * for writing all received download data to the client. */\n   895\t    return data->conn->handler->write_resp_hd(data, hd0, hdlen, is_eos);\n   896\t  }\n   897\t  /* No special handling by protocol handler, write as response bytes */\n   898\t  return return Curl_cw_out_done(data);\n   905\t}\n   906\t\n   907\tbool Curl_xfer_needs_flush(struct Curl_easy *data)\n   908\t{\n   909\t  int sockindex;\n   910\t  sockindex = ((data->conn->writesockfd != 930\t  DEBUGASSERT(data);\n   931\t  DEBUGASSERT(data->conn);\n   932\t\n   933\t  sockindex = ((data->conn->writesockfd != CURL_SOCKET_BAD) &&\n   934\t               (data->conn->writesockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]));\n   935\t  result = Curl_conn_send(data, sockindex, buf, blen, eos, pnwritten);\n   936\t  if(result == CURLE_AGAIN) {\n   937\t    result = CURLE_OK;\n   938\t    *pnwritten = 0;\n   939\t  }\n   940\t  else if(!result && *pnwritten)\n   941\t    data->info.request_size += *pnwritten;\n   942\t\n   943\t  DEBUGF(infof(data, \"Curl_xfer_send(len=%zu, eos=%d) -> %d, %zu\",\n   944\t               blen, eos, result, *pnwritten));\n   945\t  return result;\n   946\t}\n   947\t\n   948\tCURLcode Curl_xfer_recv(struct Curl_easy *data,\n   949\t                        char *buf, size_t blen,\n   950\t                        ssize_t *pnrcvd)\n   951\t{\n   952\t  int sockindex;\n   953\t\n   954\t  DEBUGASSERT(data);\n   955\t  DEBUGASSERT(data->conn);\n   956\t  DEBUGASSERT(data->set.buffer_size > 0);\n   957\t\n   958\t  sockindex = ((data->conn->sockfd != CURL_SOCKET_BAD) &&\n   959\t               (data->conn->sockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]));\n   960\t  if((size_t)data->set.buffer_size < blen)\n   961\t    blen = (size_t)data->set.buffer_size;\n   962\t  return Curl_conn_recv(data, sockindex, buf, blen, pnrcvd);\n   963\t}\n   964\t\n   965\tCURLcode Curl_xfer_send_close(struct Curl_easy *data)\n   966\t{\n   967\t  Curl_conn_ev_data_done_send(data);\n   968\t  return CURLE_OK;\n   969\t}\n   970\t\n   971\tbool Curl_xfer_is_blocked(struct Curl_easy *data)\n   972\t{\n   973\t  bool want_send = ((data)->req.keepon & KEEP_SEND);\n   974\t  bool want_recv = ((data)->req.keepon & KEEP_RECV);\n   975\t  if(!want_send)\n   976\t    return want_recv && Curl_cwriter_is_paused(data);\n   977\t  else if(!want_recv)\n   978\t    return want_send && Curl_creader_is_paused(data);\n   979\t  else\n   980\t    return Curl_creader_is_paused(data) && Curl_cwriter_is_paused(data);\n   981\t}"

74. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_commit_search @ -
Input
{
  "after": "2024-12-30",
  "before": "2025-06-01",
  "messageTerms": [
    "security",
    "CVE",
    "vulnerability",
    "heap",
    "buffer",
