  ],
  "repos": [
    "github.com/curl/curl"
  ]
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"commits\":[{\"author\":\"Harry Sintonen <sintonen@iki.fi>\",\"commit\":\"f1662ae\",\"date\":\"2025-03-06\",\"message\":\"doh: improve HTTPS RR svcparams parsing\\n\\nFixed a heap read overflow when parsing the HTTP RR svcparams. Also the\\ncode failed to enforce the requirements of SvcParamKey order specified\\nin section 2.2 of the RFC 9460.\\n\\nCloses #16598\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"doh: improve HTTPS RR svcparams parsing\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/f1662ae97b37c509dbd3ad46d2ac7cc64806e250\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"7e32f65\",\"date\":\"2025-01-13\",\"message\":\"mprintf: fix integer handling in float precision\\n\\nIn the double output function when an extremely large width and\\nprecision is set that reaches the libcurl maximum (325), the handling of\\nthe precision part would do wrong which could lead to bad output.\\n\\nAlso: work-around for single-byte buffer snprintf overflow with mingw.\\n\\nExtend test 557 to verify.\\n\\nCoverity CID 1638751.\\n\\nCloses #15988\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"mprintf: fix integer handling in float precision\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/7e32f6568772f6830b42c855b686454c20664843\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"37523c9\",\"date\":\"2025-02-25\",\"message\":\"GHA/linux: build out-of-tree, make autotools `tidy` target support it\\n\\nTo sync with the rest of core workflows.\\n\\nAlso fix the `tidy` (clang-tidy) target in autotools to support\\nout-of-tree builds:\\n```\\nclang-tidy slist_wc.c terminal.c tool_bname.c [...] var.c tool_hugehelp.c tool_ca_embed.c\\n  -quiet --warnings-as-errors=* -checks=-clang-analyzer-security.insecureAPI.strcpy,-clang-analyzer-optin.performance.Padding,-clang-analyzer-security.insecureAPI.DeprecatedOrUnsafeBufferHandling,-clang-analyzer-valist.Uninitialized --\\n  -I../../include -I../lib -I../src -I../../lib -I../../src\\n  -DBUILDING_... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"GHA/linux: build out-of-tree, make autotools `tidy` target support it\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/37523c91bc418fc734ee54f329677dc7123eb465\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"155af7a\",\"date\":\"2025-05-20\",\"message\":\"ftplistparser: add two overflow preventions\\n\\nThese two conditions probably cannot actually happen, but these two\\nchecks make that certain and should please the static code analyzers.\\n\\nPointed out by Coverity\\n\\nCloses #17397\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"ftplistparser: add two overflow preventions\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/155af7ac777e3f39e7122b9d14cc031357316564\"},{\"author\":\"Jay Satiro <raysatiro@yahoo.com>\",\"commit\":\"8ab468c\",\"date\":\"2025-01-14\",\"message\":\"mprintf: terminate snprintf output on windows\\n\\n- Null terminate the end of the snprintf output buffer on Windows.\\n\\nOld versions of the Windows CRT (which are often found on later versions\\nof Windows) do not terminate the snprintf output buffer if the output\\nreaches the max size.\\n\\nThis is a follow-up to parent 7e32f656 which made the same change but\\nlimited it to mingw, however it is a CRT version issue irrespective of\\ncompiler.\\n\\nRef: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/c-runtime-library/reference/snprintf-snprintf-snprintf-l-snwprintf-snwprintf-l?view=msvc-170#remarks\\n\\nCloses https://github.... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"mprintf: terminate snprintf output on windows\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/8ab468c8aa2e3b7b2354f0e9545859ca631f4457\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"d5b4030\",\"date\":\"2025-03-15\",\"message\":\"memdebug: set gcc 11+ deallocator attribute, extend alloc attributes to clang\\n\\nTo make `-Wfree-nonheap-object` and `-Wmismatched-dealloc` work in\\n`CURLDEBUG` builds.\\n\\nAlso extend `ALLOC_FUNC` and `ALLOC_SIZE` attribute support\\nto llvm/clang.\\n\\nllvm/clang is missing the deallocator attribute, tracked here:\\nhttps://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/129068\\n\\nRef: https://gcc.gnu.org/onlinedocs/gcc-11.1.0/gcc/Common-Function-Attributes.html#Common-Function-Attributes\\nRef: https://www.gnu.org/software/gcc/gcc-11/changes.html\\nRef: 6b143d9cc13fcd208480f678dfd06bf97bde4998 #16734\\n\\nCloses #16737\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"memdebug: set gcc 11+ deallocator attribute, extend alloc attributes to clang\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/d5b403074ed2b28e41e0c17dcd436252d38adfa3\"},{\"author\":\"Jay Satiro <raysatiro@yahoo.com>\",\"commit\":\"4a70b98\",\"date\":\"2025-04-28\",\"message\":\"progress: fix integer overflow check\\n\\n- Fix logic typo.\\n\\nPrior to this change the overflow check was reversed, meaning it did\\nnot stop an overflow condition and also if there wasn't an overflow it\\nerroneously set the total expected transfer size to the maximum value.\\n\\nFollow-up to 69ce9a7f from earlier today.\\n\\nCloses https://github.com/curl/curl/pull/17213\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"progress: fix integer overflow check\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/4a70b9823baa78af953aa89d077681293efe8b22\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"edfa537\",\"date\":\"2025-02-24\",\"message\":\"cmake: restrict static CRT builds to static curl exe, test in CI\\n\\nStatic CRT crashes MSVCR* MSVC builds (in VS2008, VS2010, VS2012,\\nVS2013) according to CI and local tests. The reproducible crash happens\\nin `curl_mfprintf() -> fputc(s, stderr)` when trying to display the\\nwarning message in `curl -V`. `stderr` is non-NULL and resolves to `2`.\\nThis reproducer needs a debug-enabled build, but it's unrelated to debug\\nfeatures or curl's memory tracker. It happens regardless of unity build,\\nCPU architecture or `DllMain()` use. Example from VS2013:\\n\\n```\\n+ _bld/src/Debug/curl.exe --disable --version\\n.... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cmake: restrict static CRT builds to static curl exe, test in CI\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/edfa537100d6b5d2ac1b829c66757880afe59407\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"9f57c2e\",\"date\":\"2025-04-29\",\"message\":\"VULN-DISCLOSURE-POLICY: use of weak algos\\n\\nNot necessarily security problems.\\n\\nCloses #17220\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"VULN-DISCLOSURE-POLICY: use of weak algos\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/9f57c2ea95c70cd8985335e7c39e383499963474\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"14f26f5\",\"date\":\"2025-02-05\",\"message\":\"smb: silence `-Warray-bounds` with gcc 13+\\n\\nThe code look correct. The compiler gets confused by the `byte[1]`\\nstruct member mapped into a memory buffer with a variable-sized\\npayload starting at this member. Perhaps there is a cleaner way\\nto silence this by changing the code.\\n\\nFirst seen with gcc 13.2.0 in curl-for-win builds. Then with 13.2.1 and\\nthe latest 14.2.0.\\n\\n```\\ncurl/lib/smb.c: In function 'smb_connection_state':\\ncurl/lib/smb.c:895:5: warning: 'memcpy' offset [74, 80] from the object at 'buf' is out of the bounds of referenced subobject 'bytes' with type 'char[1]' at offset 73 [-Warra... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"smb: silence `-Warray-bounds` with gcc 13+\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/14f26f5ee78204c15bf906f3cf7480308e2feb28\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"b696fc1\",\"date\":\"2025-02-13\",\"message\":\"lib: use Curl_str_number() for parsing decimal numbers\\n\\nInstead of strtoul() and strtol() calls.\\n\\nEasier API with better integer overflow detection and built-in max check\\nthat now comes automatic everywhere this is used.\\n\\nCloses #16319\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: use Curl_str_number() for parsing decimal numbers\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/b696fc129bf318eb97404855a47ae08e9a297d39\"},{\"author\":\"Cole Helbling <cole.e.helbling@outlook.com>\",\"commit\":\"2404a11\",\"date\":\"2025-04-12\",\"message\":\"curl_get_line: handle lines ending on the buffer boundary\\n\\nVery similar to 9f8bdd0eae5c1d441d9d901a7cf917a8ee215c7f, but affects\\ne.g. netrc file parsing.\\n\\nSuggested-by: Graham Christensen <graham@grahamc.com>\\n\\nAdd test 744 to verify\\n\\nCloses #17036\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"curl_get_line: handle lines ending on the buffer boundary\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/2404a11d85ba8876092a1e7cf5285235156d66cd\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"116f490\",\"date\":\"2025-03-14\",\"message\":\"rustls: cap maximum allowed CRL file size to 8MB\\n\\nAllowing 4GB on a 32-bit system is just asking for problems and could in\\ntheory cause integer overflow in the dynbuf code.\\n\\nThe dynbuf now has an assert to catch code trying to set a max larger\\nthan half SIZE_T_MAX.\\n\\nReported-by: Rinku Das\\nCloses #16716\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"rustls: cap maximum allowed CRL file size to 8MB\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/116f490c81908f09432d45824c9ae62c26a13bd9\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"9f8bdd0\",\"date\":\"2025-04-11\",\"message\":\"tool_parsecfg: make get_line handle lines ending on the buffer boundary\\n\\nAdd test 743 to verify.\\n\\nFixes #17030\\nReported-by: Marius Kleidl\\nCloses #17031\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tool_parsecfg: make get_line handle lines ending on the buffer boundary\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/9f8bdd0eae5c1d441d9d901a7cf917a8ee215c7f\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"5b73a7b\",\"date\":\"2025-01-27\",\"message\":\"http2: fix data_pending check\\n\\nThe h2 filter mistakenly also checked `sendbuf` when asked\\nabout pending data. The call is only meant to account for\\nbuffered data that still needs to be received.\\n\\nAlso, remove obsolete recvbuf in stream as we write received\\nheaders and data directly.\\n\\nFixes #16084\\nCloses #16098\\nReported-by: Deniz S\u00f6kmen\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"http2: fix data_pending check\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/5b73a7b6376d933eaa4a57646cb3acecb57fc106\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"6b143d9\",\"date\":\"2025-03-15\",\"message\":\"asyn-thread: drop `free()` on non-heap address\\n\\nseen with mingw-w64 gcc 14.2.0 while playing with other modifications:\\n```\\nlib/asyn-thread.c: In function 'init_resolve_thread':\\nlib/asyn-thread.c:447:5: warning: 'free' called on pointer 'data' with nonzero offset 3264 [-Wfree-nonheap-object]\\n  447 |     free(td);\\n      |     ^~~~~~~~\\n```\\n\\nWhere `td` is:\\n```c\\n  struct thread_data *td = &data->state.async.thdata;\\n```\\n\\nFollow-up to d9fc64d3ab289a84548e952183d7eba79ccc846e #16241\\nCloses #16734\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"asyn-thread: drop `free()` on non-heap address\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/6b143d9cc13fcd208480f678dfd06bf97bde4998\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"7e282e1\",\"date\":\"2025-03-08\",\"message\":\"lib2302: fix crash due to stack overflow on MSVC and clang Windows\\n\\nIt fixes test 2302, 2303, 2307 with MSVC and clang on Windows.\\nGCC Windows builds were not affected.\\n\\nFailure was caused by stack overflow due to a 1MB+ sized test struct on\\nstack. Replace it with dynamic allocation.\\n\\nAlso unignore affected tests in GHA/windows.\\n\\nAs seen under WINE with llvm-mingw:\\n```\\n$ wine64 libtests.exe lib2302 ws://127.0.0.1:59964/2302 > stdout2302 2> stderr2302\\nTest: lib2302\\nURL: ws://127.0.0.1:59964/2302\\nwine: Unhandled stack overflow at address 000000014007486A (thread 0024), starting debugger...\\nUnhan... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib2302: fix crash due to stack overflow on MSVC and clang Windows\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/7e282e18a5c8f76e21de70533f8dce1198dd8133\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"5426cd5\",\"date\":\"2025-01-24\",\"message\":\"content_encoding: put the decomp buffers into the writer structs\\n\\n- no more malloc/free per chunk\\n- removes the extra malloc entirely\\n- make the buffer (much) smaller (10MB => 16KB!)\\n- rename 'decomp' to 'buffer' to clarify purpose\\n\\nCloses #16079\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"content_encoding: put the decomp buffers into the writer structs\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/5426cd594853aafbcf8e8efe5199f4c62de861e7\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"69ce9a7\",\"date\":\"2025-04-28\",\"message\":\"progress: avoid integer overflow when gathering total transfer size\\n\\nReported by OSS-fuzz\\n\\nCloses #17207\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"progress: avoid integer overflow when gathering total transfer size\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/69ce9a7feb0ef854940daec75fc91d13bcd68400\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"cb4cd36\",\"date\":\"2025-01-25\",\"message\":\"VULN-DISCLOSURE-POLICY: on legacy dependencies\\n\\nProblems that only trigger using *legacy* dependencies are not\\nconsidered security problems.\\n\\nCloses #16086\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"VULN-DISCLOSURE-POLICY: on legacy dependencies\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/cb4cd36fe7183b2f289b40be6e38c1442925ab18\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"834836d\",\"date\":\"2025-04-17\",\"message\":\"doh: reduce the DNS request buffer size\\n\\nNo point in having it larger than the largest allowed request size.\\n\\nAlso removed a no longer used struct.\\n\\nCloses #17087\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"doh: reduce the DNS request buffer size\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/834836dc14f7779f4ec6736f8d4b60745bc7747a\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"cfc657a\",\"date\":\"2025-02-22\",\"message\":\"multi: event based rework\\n\\nRework the event based handling of transfers and connections to\\nbe \\\"localized\\\" into a single source file with clearer dependencies.\\n\\n- add multi_ev.c and multi_ev.h\\n- add docs/internal/MULTI-EV.md to explain the overall workings\\n- only do event handling book keeping when the socket callback\\n  is set\\n- add handling for \\\"connection only\\\" event tracking, when internal\\n  easy handles are used that are not really tied to a connection.\\n  Used in connection pool.\\n- remove transfer member \\\"last_poll\\\" and connections \\\"shutdown_poll\\\"\\n  and keep all that internal to multi_ev.c\\n... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"multi: event based rework\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/cfc657a48dbafb4194676d4c9d841388b3a22210\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"8c8186e\",\"date\":\"2025-05-21\",\"message\":\"http_aws_sigv4: avoid risk of overflowed constant\\n\\n- Simplify canon_query() a bit. Avoid unconditionally using length -1\\n  where length risks being zero at times. Pointed out by Coverity.\\n- Fix indent errors\\n- narrow some variable scopes\\n- fix keywords in tests\\n\\nCloses #17402\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"http_aws_sigv4: avoid risk of overflowed constant\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/8c8186eadcb56ad1f3b51579e13f0ff416bdb58d\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"2a292c3\",\"date\":\"2025-01-10\",\"message\":\"build: add Windows CE / CeGCC support, with CI jobs\\n\\nMake it possible to build curl for Windows CE using the CeGCC toolchain.\\nWith both CMake and autotools, including tests and examples, also in CI.\\nThe build configuration is the default one with Schannel enabled. No\\n3rd-party dependencies have been tested.\\n\\nAlso revive old code to make Schannel build with Windows CE, including\\ncertificate verification.\\n\\nBuilds have been throughougly tested. But, I've made no functional tests\\nfor this PR. Some parts (esp. file operations, like truncate and seek)\\nare stubbed out and likely broken as a result. T... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: add Windows CE / CeGCC support, with CI jobs\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/2a292c39846107228201674d686be5b3ed96674d\"},{\"author\":\"Neil Horman <nhorman@openssl.org>\",\"commit\":\"957eb24\",\"date\":\"2025-01-03\",\"message\":\"osslq: use SSL_poll to determine writeability of QUIC streams\\n\\nThis discussion:\\nhttps://github.com/openssl/openssl/discussions/23339#discussion-6094341\\n\\nSpecifically item number 2 (Send Blocking) was raised by the curl team,\\nnoting that SSL_want_write returning false was not a good indicator of\\nwhen a stream is writeable. The suggestion in that discussion was to use\\nSSL_poll with an SSL_POLL_EVENT_W flag instead, as that is a proper\\nindication of when an SSL_object will allow writing without blocking.\\n\\nWhile ssl_want_write updates its state based on the last error\\nencountered (implying a need ... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"osslq: use SSL_poll to determine writeability of QUIC streams\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/957eb240ed7115f89a60c55f441a52ec7886327c\"},{\"author\":\"Max Eliaser <eliaserm@elementaltechnologies.com>\",\"commit\":\"7b375b8\",\"date\":\"2025-04-19\",\"message\":\"CURLOPT_ERRORBUFFER.md: the buffer is read only after curl takes ownership\\n\\nClarifies #17100\\nCloses #17105\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"CURLOPT_ERRORBUFFER.md: the buffer is read only after curl takes ownership\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/7b375b8b91ea3c84d71e9f2111b6d73f2c2d1d24\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"771c15b\",\"date\":\"2025-04-28\",\"message\":\"smb: avoid integer overflow on weird input date\\n\\nFound by OSS-fuzz\\n\\nCloses #17206\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"smb: avoid integer overflow on weird input date\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/771c15b6036a28a188ec69f324fdc589092a38e2\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"4db64a6\",\"date\":\"2025-04-25\",\"message\":\"tool_paramhlp: avoid integer overflow in secs2ms()\\n\\nThe previous approach was wrong and could lead to wrong timeout values\\ngetting used.\\n\\nReported-by: bsr13 on hackerone\\nCloses #17184\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tool_paramhlp: avoid integer overflow in secs2ms()\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/4db64a6437dc9133a8a5b127da8affcaa739c894\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"ad700a0\",\"date\":\"2025-02-17\",\"message\":\"strparse: speed up the hex parser somewhat\\n\\nAround 2.3x speed-up parsing many large hexadecimal numbers. The decimal and\\noctal parser get marginally faster.\\n\\nStill very readable, compact and easy to follow code.\\n\\nTweaks\\n\\n- combine the max and the overflow check, gains 3ns/num (use a separate\\n  check outside of the loop instead for max < base)\\n- one less indirection in the pointer, gains 3ns/num\\n- using the table lookup for hex nums, gains 5ns/num\\n- unfold the num_digit() macro, gains 3s/num\\n- use the hexasciitable unconditionally, gains 2ns/num\\n- use post-increment pointer in the table lookup,... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"strparse: speed up the hex parser somewhat\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/ad700a091788612017a4760ea4f81cbe8fb413ce\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"b984081\",\"date\":\"2025-01-10\",\"message\":\"tidy-up: `curl_setup.h`, `curl_setup_once.h`, `config-win32ce.h`\\n\\n- drop unused `LIBIDN_REQUIRED_VERSION` macro.\\n  Unused since 9c91ec778104ae3b744b39444d544e82d5ee9ece\\n\\n- drop compatibility error for `CURL_WANTS_CA_BUNDLE_ENV`.\\n  This macro was once set by `Makefile.mk` and Watcom makefiles.\\n  They are no longer supported, making the compatibility message moot.\\n\\n  Follow-up to 7d7346519d8bff513f9ed9f0c5e709782b159e5a #1542 (2017)\\n  Follow-up to c2aeb1b3ba09af8b2e555eafa94dd9c534426bfb #1538 (2017)\\n\\n- document last MSVC supporting the `!_MT` condition\\n  Ref: https://stackoverflow.com/questions... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tidy-up: `curl_setup.h`, `curl_setup_once.h`, `config-win32ce.h`\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/b9840814adbb01606377b02c65608db0f30461a6\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"02edae5\",\"date\":\"2025-01-02\",\"message\":\"websocket: fix message send corruption\\n\\n- Fix a bug in EAGAIN handling when sending frames that led to a\\n  corrupted last byte of the frame sent.\\n\\n- Restore sanity to curl_ws_send() behaviour:\\n\\n  - Partial writes are reported as OK with the actual number of\\n    payload bytes sent.\\n\\n  - CURLE_AGAIN is only returned when none of the payload bytes\\n    (or for 0-length frames, not all of the frame header bytes)\\n    could be sent.\\n\\n  - curl_ws_send() now behaves like a common send() call.\\n\\n- Change 'ws-data' test client to allow concurrent send/recv\\n  operations and vary frame sizes and repeat coun... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"websocket: fix message send corruption\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/02edae54e8b3953b8aeb0ff91be86d5db2169670\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"c2aa504\",\"date\":\"2025-02-07\",\"message\":\"wakeup_write: make sure the eventfd write sends eight bytes\\n\\nThe eventfd manpage says:\\n\\n  A write(2) fails with the error EINVAL if the size of the supplied\\n  buffer is less than 8 bytes\\n\\nWhen doing x32 on a 64-bit system, pointers are still four bytes so this\\ncode must not use the size of a pointer but the size of a 64-bit type.\\n\\nFixes #16237\\nReported-by: Jan Engelhardt\\nCloses #16239\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"wakeup_write: make sure the eventfd write sends eight bytes\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c2aa504ab9148b5c284b090c5043d9f0c3fbd903\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"554e4c1\",\"date\":\"2025-03-20\",\"message\":\"windows: fix issues detected by clang-tidy, and some more\\n\\n- digest_sspi: memory leak.\\n- digest_sspi: free buffers on `calloc()` fail.\\n  (not detected by clang-tidy)\\n- schannel_verify: avoid a `NULL` `alt_name_info`.\\n- schannel: fix potential `NULL` deref for `backend->cred`.\\n- schannel: fix uninitialized result value.\\n  Follow-up to 7f4c358541fdadcf29ba20bcdff71c5554e5f69c #3197\\n- schannel: drop unused assigment.\\n- tool_doswin: drop unused assigment.\\n- testutil: fix memory leak on error.\\n- testutil: fix memory leak on non-error.\\n  (not detected by clang-tidy)\\n\\nCherry-picked from #16764\\nCloses... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"windows: fix issues detected by clang-tidy, and some more\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/554e4c14bee82a604e08d51767f49fe11580e700\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"f787008\",\"date\":\"2025-02-10\",\"message\":\"client writer: handle pause before deocding\\n\\nAdds a \\\"cw-pause\\\" client writer in the PROTOCOL phase that buffers\\noutput when the client paused the transfer. This prevents content\\ndecoding from blowing the buffer in the \\\"cw-out\\\" writer.\\n\\nAdded test_02_35 that downloads 2 100MB gzip bombs in parallel and\\npauses after 1MB of decoded 0's.\\n\\nThis is a solution to issue #16280, with some limitations:\\n- cw-out still needs buffering of its own, since it can be paused\\n  \\\"in the middle\\\" of a write that started with some KB of gzipped\\n  zeros and exploded into several MB of calls to cw-out.\\n- cw-pause will... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"client writer: handle pause before deocding\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/f78700814d702c523a7a2c3766a1ca60f3db7edc\"}],\"limitHit\":false,\"query\":\"type:commit (message:security OR message:CVE OR message:vulnerability OR message:heap OR message:buffer OR message:overflow) repo:github.com/curl/curl after:2024-12-30 before:2025-06-01 count:100\",\"totalCount\":34}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "commits": [
      {
        "author": "Harry Sintonen <sintonen@iki.fi>",
        "commit": "f1662ae",
        "date": "2025-03-06",
        "message": "doh: improve HTTPS RR svcparams parsing\n\nFixed a heap read overflow when parsing the HTTP RR svcparams. Also the\ncode failed to enforce the requirements of SvcParamKey order specified\nin section 2.2 of the RFC 9460.\n\nCloses #16598",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "doh: improve HTTPS RR svcparams parsing",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/f1662ae97b37c509dbd3ad46d2ac7cc64806e250"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "7e32f65",
        "date": "2025-01-13",
        "message": "mprintf: fix integer handling in float precision\n\nIn the double output function when an extremely large width and\nprecision is set that reaches the libcurl maximum (325), the handling of\nthe precision part would do wrong which could lead to bad output.\n\nAlso: work-around for single-byte buffer snprintf overflow with mingw.\n\nExtend test 557 to verify.\n\nCoverity CID 1638751.\n\nCloses #15988",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "mprintf: fix integer handling in float precision",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/7e32f6568772f6830b42c855b686454c20664843"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "37523c9",
        "date": "2025-02-25",
        "message": "GHA/linux: build out-of-tree, make autotools `tidy` target support it\n\nTo sync with the rest of core workflows.\n\nAlso fix the `tidy` (clang-tidy) target in autotools to support\nout-of-tree builds:\n```\nclang-tidy slist_wc.c terminal.c tool_bname.c [...] var.c tool_hugehelp.c tool_ca_embed.c\n  -quiet --warnings-as-errors=* -checks=-clang-analyzer-security.insecureAPI.strcpy,-clang-analyzer-optin.performance.Padding,-clang-analyzer-security.insecureAPI.DeprecatedOrUnsafeBufferHandling,-clang-analyzer-valist.Uninitialized --\n  -I../../include -I../lib -I../src -I../../lib -I../../src\n  -DBUILDING_... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "GHA/linux: build out-of-tree, make autotools `tidy` target support it",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/37523c91bc418fc734ee54f329677dc7123eb465"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "155af7a",
        "date": "2025-05-20",
        "message": "ftplistparser: add two overflow preventions\n\nThese two conditions probably cannot actually happen, but these two\nchecks make that certain and should please the static code analyzers.\n\nPointed out by Coverity\n\nCloses #17397",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "ftplistparser: add two overflow preventions",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/155af7ac777e3f39e7122b9d14cc031357316564"
      },
      {
        "author": "Jay Satiro <raysatiro@yahoo.com>",
        "commit": "8ab468c",
        "date": "2025-01-14",
        "message": "mprintf: terminate snprintf output on windows\n\n- Null terminate the end of the snprintf output buffer on Windows.\n\nOld versions of the Windows CRT (which are often found on later versions\nof Windows) do not terminate the snprintf output buffer if the output\nreaches the max size.\n\nThis is a follow-up to parent 7e32f656 which made the same change but\nlimited it to mingw, however it is a CRT version issue irrespective of\ncompiler.\n\nRef: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/c-runtime-library/reference/snprintf-snprintf-snprintf-l-snwprintf-snwprintf-l?view=msvc-170#remarks\n\nCloses https://github.... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "mprintf: terminate snprintf output on windows",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/8ab468c8aa2e3b7b2354f0e9545859ca631f4457"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "d5b4030",
        "date": "2025-03-15",
        "message": "memdebug: set gcc 11+ deallocator attribute, extend alloc attributes to clang\n\nTo make `-Wfree-nonheap-object` and `-Wmismatched-dealloc` work in\n`CURLDEBUG` builds.\n\nAlso extend `ALLOC_FUNC` and `ALLOC_SIZE` attribute support\nto llvm/clang.\n\nllvm/clang is missing the deallocator attribute, tracked here:\nhttps://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/129068\n\nRef: https://gcc.gnu.org/onlinedocs/gcc-11.1.0/gcc/Common-Function-Attributes.html#Common-Function-Attributes\nRef: https://www.gnu.org/software/gcc/gcc-11/changes.html\nRef: 6b143d9cc13fcd208480f678dfd06bf97bde4998 #16734\n\nCloses #16737",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "memdebug: set gcc 11+ deallocator attribute, extend alloc attributes to clang",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/d5b403074ed2b28e41e0c17dcd436252d38adfa3"
      },
      {
        "author": "Jay Satiro <raysatiro@yahoo.com>",
        "commit": "4a70b98",
        "date": "2025-04-28",
        "message": "progress: fix integer overflow check\n\n- Fix logic typo.\n\nPrior to this change the overflow check was reversed, meaning it did\nnot stop an overflow condition and also if there wasn't an overflow it\nerroneously set the total expected transfer size to the maximum value.\n\nFollow-up to 69ce9a7f from earlier today.\n\nCloses https://github.com/curl/curl/pull/17213",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "progress: fix integer overflow check",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/4a70b9823baa78af953aa89d077681293efe8b22"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "edfa537",
        "date": "2025-02-24",
        "message": "cmake: restrict static CRT builds to static curl exe, test in CI\n\nStatic CRT crashes MSVCR* MSVC builds (in VS2008, VS2010, VS2012,\nVS2013) according to CI and local tests. The reproducible crash happens\nin `curl_mfprintf() -> fputc(s, stderr)` when trying to display the\nwarning message in `curl -V`. `stderr` is non-NULL and resolves to `2`.\nThis reproducer needs a debug-enabled build, but it's unrelated to debug\nfeatures or curl's memory tracker. It happens regardless of unity build,\nCPU architecture or `DllMain()` use. Example from VS2013:\n\n```\n+ _bld/src/Debug/curl.exe --disable --version\n.... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cmake: restrict static CRT builds to static curl exe, test in CI",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/edfa537100d6b5d2ac1b829c66757880afe59407"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "9f57c2e",
        "date": "2025-04-29",
        "message": "VULN-DISCLOSURE-POLICY: use of weak algos\n\nNot necessarily security problems.\n\nCloses #17220",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "VULN-DISCLOSURE-POLICY: use of weak algos",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/9f57c2ea95c70cd8985335e7c39e383499963474"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "14f26f5",
        "date": "2025-02-05",
        "message": "smb: silence `-Warray-bounds` with gcc 13+\n\nThe code look correct. The compiler gets confused by the `byte[1]`\nstruct member mapped into a memory buffer with a variable-sized\npayload starting at this member. Perhaps there is a cleaner way\nto silence this by changing the code.\n\nFirst seen with gcc 13.2.0 in curl-for-win builds. Then with 13.2.1 and\nthe latest 14.2.0.\n\n```\ncurl/lib/smb.c: In function 'smb_connection_state':\ncurl/lib/smb.c:895:5: warning: 'memcpy' offset [74, 80] from the object at 'buf' is out of the bounds of referenced subobject 'bytes' with type 'char[1]' at offset 73 [-Warra... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "smb: silence `-Warray-bounds` with gcc 13+",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/14f26f5ee78204c15bf906f3cf7480308e2feb28"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "b696fc1",
        "date": "2025-02-13",
        "message": "lib: use Curl_str_number() for parsing decimal numbers\n\nInstead of strtoul() and strtol() calls.\n\nEasier API with better integer overflow detection and built-in max check\nthat now comes automatic everywhere this is used.\n\nCloses #16319",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: use Curl_str_number() for parsing decimal numbers",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/b696fc129bf318eb97404855a47ae08e9a297d39"
      },
      {
        "author": "Cole Helbling <cole.e.helbling@outlook.com>",
        "commit": "2404a11",
        "date": "2025-04-12",
        "message": "curl_get_line: handle lines ending on the buffer boundary\n\nVery similar to 9f8bdd0eae5c1d441d9d901a7cf917a8ee215c7f, but affects\ne.g. netrc file parsing.\n\nSuggested-by: Graham Christensen <graham@grahamc.com>\n\nAdd test 744 to verify\n\nCloses #17036",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "curl_get_line: handle lines ending on the buffer boundary",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/2404a11d85ba8876092a1e7cf5285235156d66cd"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "116f490",
        "date": "2025-03-14",
        "message": "rustls: cap maximum allowed CRL file size to 8MB\n\nAllowing 4GB on a 32-bit system is just asking for problems and could in\ntheory cause integer overflow in the dynbuf code.\n\nThe dynbuf now has an assert to catch code trying to set a max larger\nthan half SIZE_T_MAX.\n\nReported-by: Rinku Das\nCloses #16716",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "rustls: cap maximum allowed CRL file size to 8MB",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/116f490c81908f09432d45824c9ae62c26a13bd9"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "9f8bdd0",
        "date": "2025-04-11",
        "message": "tool_parsecfg: make get_line handle lines ending on the buffer boundary\n\nAdd test 743 to verify.\n\nFixes #17030\nReported-by: Marius Kleidl\nCloses #17031",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tool_parsecfg: make get_line handle lines ending on the buffer boundary",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/9f8bdd0eae5c1d441d9d901a7cf917a8ee215c7f"
      },
      {
        "author": "Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>",
        "commit": "5b73a7b",
        "date": "2025-01-27",
        "message": "http2: fix data_pending check\n\nThe h2 filter mistakenly also checked `sendbuf` when asked\nabout pending data. The call is only meant to account for\nbuffered data that still needs to be received.\n\nAlso, remove obsolete recvbuf in stream as we write received\nheaders and data directly.\n\nFixes #16084\nCloses #16098\nReported-by: Deniz S\u00f6kmen",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "http2: fix data_pending check",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/5b73a7b6376d933eaa4a57646cb3acecb57fc106"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "6b143d9",
        "date": "2025-03-15",
        "message": "asyn-thread: drop `free()` on non-heap address\n\nseen with mingw-w64 gcc 14.2.0 while playing with other modifications:\n```\nlib/asyn-thread.c: In function 'init_resolve_thread':\nlib/asyn-thread.c:447:5: warning: 'free' called on pointer 'data' with nonzero offset 3264 [-Wfree-nonheap-object]\n  447 |     free(td);\n      |     ^~~~~~~~\n```\n\nWhere `td` is:\n```c\n  struct thread_data *td = &data->state.async.thdata;\n```\n\nFollow-up to d9fc64d3ab289a84548e952183d7eba79ccc846e #16241\nCloses #16734",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "asyn-thread: drop `free()` on non-heap address",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/6b143d9cc13fcd208480f678dfd06bf97bde4998"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "7e282e1",
        "date": "2025-03-08",
        "message": "lib2302: fix crash due to stack overflow on MSVC and clang Windows\n\nIt fixes test 2302, 2303, 2307 with MSVC and clang on Windows.\nGCC Windows builds were not affected.\n\nFailure was caused by stack overflow due to a 1MB+ sized test struct on\nstack. Replace it with dynamic allocation.\n\nAlso unignore affected tests in GHA/windows.\n\nAs seen under WINE with llvm-mingw:\n```\n$ wine64 libtests.exe lib2302 ws://127.0.0.1:59964/2302 > stdout2302 2> stderr2302\nTest: lib2302\nURL: ws://127.0.0.1:59964/2302\nwine: Unhandled stack overflow at address 000000014007486A (thread 0024), starting debugger...\nUnhan... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib2302: fix crash due to stack overflow on MSVC and clang Windows",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/7e282e18a5c8f76e21de70533f8dce1198dd8133"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "5426cd5",
        "date": "2025-01-24",
        "message": "content_encoding: put the decomp buffers into the writer structs\n\n- no more malloc/free per chunk\n- removes the extra malloc entirely\n- make the buffer (much) smaller (10MB => 16KB!)\n- rename 'decomp' to 'buffer' to clarify purpose\n\nCloses #16079",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "content_encoding: put the decomp buffers into the writer structs",

        "message": "doh: reduce the DNS request buffer size\n\nNo point in having it larger than the largest allowed request size.\n\nAlso removed a no longer used struct.\n\nCloses #17087",

        "title": "doh: reduce the DNS request buffer size",

      {
        "author": "Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>",
        "commit": "cfc657a",
        "date": "2025-02-22",
        "message": "multi: event based rework\n\nRework the event based handling of transfers and connections to\nbe \"localized\" into a single source file with clearer dependencies.\n\n- add multi_ev.c and multi_ev.h\n- add docs/internal/MULTI-EV.md to explain the overall workings\n- only do event handling book keeping when the socket callback\n  is set\n- add handling for \"connection only\" event tracking, when internal\n  easy handles are used that are not really tied to a connection.\n  Used in connection pool.\n- remove transfer member \"last_poll\" and connections \"shutdown_poll\"\n  and keep all that internal to multi_ev.c\n... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",

        "commit": "8c8186e",
        "date": "2025-05-21",
        "message": "http_aws_sigv4: avoid risk of overflowed constant\n\n- Simplify canon_query() a bit. Avoid unconditionally using length -1\n  where length risks being zero at times. Pointed out by Coverity.\n- Fix indent errors\n- narrow some variable scopes\n- fix keywords in tests\n\nCloses #17402",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",

      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "2a292c3",
        "date": "2025-01-10",
        "message": "build: add Windows CE / CeGCC support, with CI jobs\n\nMake it possible to build curl for Windows CE using the CeGCC toolchain.\nWith both CMake and autotools, including tests and examples, also in CI.\nThe build configuration is the default one with Schannel enabled. No\n3rd-party dependencies have been tested.\n\nAlso revive old code to make Schannel build with Windows CE, including\ncertificate verification.\n\nBuilds have been throughougly tested. But, I've made no functional tests\nfor this PR. Some parts (esp. file operations, like truncate and seek)\nare stubbed out and likely broken as a result. T... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: add Windows CE / CeGCC support, with CI jobs",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/2a292c39846107228201674d686be5b3ed96674d"
      },
      {
        "author": "Neil Horman <nhorman@openssl.org>",
        "commit": "957eb24",
        "date": "2025-01-03",
        "message": "osslq: use SSL_poll to determine writeability of QUIC streams\n\nThis discussion:\nhttps://github.com/openssl/openssl/discussions/23339#discussion-6094341\n\nSpecifically item number 2 (Send Blocking) was raised by the curl team,\nnoting that SSL_want_write returning false was not a good indicator of\nwhen a stream is writeable. The suggestion in that discussion was to use\nSSL_poll with an SSL_POLL_EVENT_W flag instead, as that is a proper\nindication of when an SSL_object will allow writing without blocking.\n\nWhile ssl_want_write updates its state based on the last error\nencountered (implying a need ... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "osslq: use SSL_poll to determine writeability of QUIC streams",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/957eb240ed7115f89a60c55f441a52ec7886327c"
      },

        "commit": "7b375b8",

        "commit": "4db64a6",
        "date": "2025-04-25",
        "message": "tool_paramhlp: avoid integer overflow in secs2ms()\n\nThe previous approach was wrong and could lead to wrong timeout values\ngetting used.\n\nReported-by: bsr13 on hackerone\nCloses #17184",

        "title": "tool_paramhlp: avoid integer overflow in secs2ms()",

      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "ad700a0",
        "date": "2025-02-17",
        "message": "strparse: speed up the hex parser somewhat\n\nAround 2.3x speed-up parsing many large hexadecimal numbers. The decimal and\noctal parser get marginally faster.\n\nStill very readable, compact and easy to follow code.\n\nTweaks\n\n- combine the max and the overflow check, gains 3ns/num (use a separate\n  check outside of the loop instead for max < base)\n- one less indirection in the pointer, gains 3ns/num\n- using the table lookup for hex nums, gains 5ns/num\n- unfold the num_digit() macro, gains 3s/num\n- use the hexasciitable unconditionally, gains 2ns/num\n- use post-increment pointer in the table lookup,... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "strparse: speed up the hex parser somewhat",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/ad700a091788612017a4760ea4f81cbe8fb413ce"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "b984081",
        "date": "2025-01-10",
        "message": "tidy-up: `curl_setup.h`, `curl_setup_once.h`, `config-win32ce.h`\n\n- drop unused `LIBIDN_REQUIRED_VERSION` macro.\n  Unused since 9c91ec778104ae3b744b39444d544e82d5ee9ece\n\n- drop compatibility error for `CURL_WANTS_CA_BUNDLE_ENV`.\n  This macro was once set by `Makefile.mk` and Watcom makefiles.\n  They are no longer supported, making the compatibility message moot.\n\n  Follow-up to 7d7346519d8bff513f9ed9f0c5e709782b159e5a #1542 (2017)\n  Follow-up to c2aeb1b3ba09af8b2e555eafa94dd9c534426bfb #1538 (2017)\n\n- document last MSVC supporting the `!_MT` condition\n  Ref: https://stackoverflow.com/questions... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tidy-up: `curl_setup.h`, `curl_setup_once.h`, `config-win32ce.h`",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/b9840814adbb01606377b02c65608db0f30461a6"
      },
      {
        "author": "Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>",
        "commit": "02edae5",
        "date": "2025-01-02",
        "message": "websocket: fix message send corruption\n\n- Fix a bug in EAGAIN handling when sending frames that led to a\n  corrupted last byte of the frame sent.\n\n- Restore sanity to curl_ws_send() behaviour:\n\n  - Partial writes are reported as OK with the actual number of\n    payload bytes sent.\n\n  - CURLE_AGAIN is only returned when none of the payload bytes\n    (or for 0-length frames, not all of the frame header bytes)\n    could be sent.\n\n  - curl_ws_send() now behaves like a common send() call.\n\n- Change 'ws-data' test client to allow concurrent send/recv\n  operations and vary frame sizes and repeat coun... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "websocket: fix message send corruption",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/02edae54e8b3953b8aeb0ff91be86d5db2169670"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "c2aa504",
        "date": "2025-02-07",
        "message": "wakeup_write: make sure the eventfd write sends eight bytes\n\nThe eventfd manpage says:\n\n  A write(2) fails with the error EINVAL if the size of the supplied\n  buffer is less than 8 bytes\n\nWhen doing x32 on a 64-bit system, pointers are still four bytes so this\ncode must not use the size of a pointer but the size of a 64-bit type.\n\nFixes #16237\nReported-by: Jan Engelhardt\nCloses #16239",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "wakeup_write: make sure the eventfd write sends eight bytes",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c2aa504ab9148b5c284b090c5043d9f0c3fbd903"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "554e4c1",
        "date": "2025-03-20",
        "message": "windows: fix issues detected by clang-tidy, and some more\n\n- digest_sspi: memory leak.\n- digest_sspi: free buffers on `calloc()` fail.\n  (not detected by clang-tidy)\n- schannel_verify: avoid a `NULL` `alt_name_info`.\n- schannel: fix potential `NULL` deref for `backend->cred`.\n- schannel: fix uninitialized result value.\n  Follow-up to 7f4c358541fdadcf29ba20bcdff71c5554e5f69c #3197\n- schannel: drop unused assigment.\n- tool_doswin: drop unused assigment.\n- testutil: fix memory leak on error.\n- testutil: fix memory leak on non-error.\n  (not detected by clang-tidy)\n\nCherry-picked from #16764\nCloses... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "windows: fix issues detected by clang-tidy, and some more",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/554e4c14bee82a604e08d51767f49fe11580e700"
      },
      {
        "author": "Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>",
        "commit": "f787008",
        "date": "2025-02-10",
        "message": "client writer: handle pause before deocding\n\nAdds a \"cw-pause\" client writer in the PROTOCOL phase that buffers\noutput when the client paused the transfer. This prevents content\ndecoding from blowing the buffer in the \"cw-out\" writer.\n\nAdded test_02_35 that downloads 2 100MB gzip bombs in parallel and\npauses after 1MB of decoded 0's.\n\nThis is a solution to issue #16280, with some limitations:\n- cw-out still needs buffering of its own, since it can be paused\n  \"in the middle\" of a write that started with some KB of gzipped\n  zeros and exploded into several MB of calls to cw-out.\n- cw-pause will... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "client writer: handle pause before deocding",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/f78700814d702c523a7a2c3766a1ca60f3db7edc"
      }
    ],
    "limitHit": false,

  "stdout": "4497dbd9a clang-tidy: fixes and improvements\n5fa5cb382 build: fix `-Wunused-macros` warnings, and related tidy-ups\n633ec719d curl_ntlm_core: merge two `#if` blocks\ndc08922a6 openssl: disable local keylog feature if built-in upstream\nf1c9d5e48 easy: reset errorbuf on eyeballing success\n36c2c7626 libcurl-security.md: Fix typos and add a point about URLs\n970e59a82 GHA/windows: mark 3023/3024 flaky\n8fb87f5a4 lib: delete unused local includes\ndaa6b27b4 include: avoid recursive macros\n1eec8b8d0 mprintf: rename internal enum to avoid collision with AmigaOS symbol\ndead29362 GHA/windows: mark test 3000 flaky too\na55f160b1 src: simplify declaring `curl_ca_embed`\n298f73f95 cmake: enable binutils ld workaround for all toolchains at build-time (revert)\n9e1e5ea67 curl_setup.h: update/expand `OPENSSL_SUPPRESS_DEPRECATED` comment\n849401219 examples/usercertinmem: use modern OpenSSL API, drop mentions of RSA\nd445f2d93 lib: skip compiling code with features disabled\n0649eb082 GHA/windows: bump cygwin/cygwin-install-action to v6.1\ncf9e445e0 cmake: normalize uppercase hex winver (for display)\n20ae14a32 cmake: warn for invalid `CURL_TARGET_WINDOWS_VERSION` values\nfb5541c28 GHA/windows: replace Cygwin distro server\n64d9f3543 fixup free the send token on errors\nfe276ff15 fixup merge two lines\ncd7cd5462 fixup white space\n45f2ed955 fixup return code\n207ab758f ws/tftp: include header file even when protocol disabled\n36dc50b7e smb: include arpa/inet.h for NonStop\n614abe111 fixup 'etbuf_size' may be used uninitialized\n57d5935f6 fixup oopses\n38a5098f8 socks_sspi: split Curl_SOCKS5_gssapi_negotiate into sub functions\n10bb489b2 schannel: refactor: reduce variable scopes, fix comment, fix indent\ndf6f3ae60 multi: split multi_runsingle into sub functions\n3ce596a95 msvc: VS2026: unlock picky warning in cmake, test in CI\naece530e2 GHA: update google/boringssl to v0.20260211.0\n5edd79aa9 cmake: add `CURL_PATCHSTAMP` configuration variable, test in CI\n516a0cd38 cmake: add `BORINGSSL_VERSION` configuration variable, test in CI\n61df5f466 tidy-up: miscellaneous\n2a92c39a2 windows: tidy up `wincrypt.h` / BoringSSL/AWS-LC coexist workaround\n208b2e897 RELEASE-NOTES: synced\n85a895c59 cli_hx_download: add Schannel support\n325d0e0a6 cmake: fix `CURL_DROP_UNUSED` accidental left always-enabled\nc220674ac windows: tidy up includes, macros, document dependencies\n982ab7b53 build: check `MSG_NOSIGNAL` directly, drop detection and interim macro\nc05cd2a10 curl_setup.h: merge back single-use internal header `curl_setup_once.h`\n6d2e9926d docs/libcurl: unify WARNING use\n434e5312f tool_operate: split post_per_transfer into sub functions\n69f67f5e1 GHA/linux: update pizlonator/fil-c to v0.678\ne269e3daf pytest: bump pip cryptography to 46.0.5\n64ea22558 GHA: update awslabs/aws-lc to v1.67.0\nee3a4dff1 http_aws_sigv4: fix query normalization of %2b\n5c250e242 gss: exclude verbose error logic from non-verbose builds\nb844c1a07 vtls: use ALPN http/1.0 & http/1.1 for HTTP/1.0 requests\n0291f751c RELEASE-NOTES: synced\n22010e418 test1980: verify sigv4 normalization query with + and spaces\n15a8a777b VULN-DISCLOSURE-POLICY.md: mention GitHub quirks\n950c7bb17 digest: escape double quotes and backslashes in realm and nonce\nafe9fdd1e tests: convert base64 data to %b64[]\ncdfc8dc7a build: tidy up and simplify `setmode()` detection and use\n2c0019b08 curl_setup.h: drop extra header guard for internal include\nbb56f325a curl_get_line: drop single-use macro\naa0be708b url.c: restore mistaken comment change"
}

}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "5fa5cb382 build: fix `-Wunused-macros` warnings, and related tidy-ups\ndc08922a6 openssl: disable local keylog feature if built-in upstream\n61df5f466 tidy-up: miscellaneous\n2a92c39a2 windows: tidy up `wincrypt.h` / BoringSSL/AWS-LC coexist workaround\n85de99520 tidy-up: move literals to right-side of `if` expressions (where missing)\n31a4f415a build: tidy up and dedupe `strdup` functions\n9c3fcb8a3 vtls: dedupe common on-session-reuse logic\n1b50dcc2a tls: remove checks for DEFAULT\n065b149df OpenSSL: check reuse of sessions for verify status\nb81341e8f tidy-up: Markdown, clang-format nits\n61093e2a8 build: fully omit verbose strings and code when disabled\n814b54d83 tidy-up: miscellaneous\nd7bbc7836 tls: add new SSLSUPP flags for several options\n6aaac9dd3 vquic: drop support for OpenSSL-QUIC\n85c841cb4 msvc: drop exception, make `BIT()` a bitfield with Visual Studio\n57ff2d6c9 dns: flatten the include tree\n59a5fd851 build: drop redundant unused variable suppressions\n60f9d3dd7 build: omit forward declarations\n7ad830bb5 openssl: stop checking for `OPENSSL_NO_SHA*` macros\n0d0ac29cd openssl: stop checking for `OPENSSL_NO_TLSEXT` macro"
}

Output
{
  "content": "{\"commits\":[{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"8fb87f5\",\"date\":\"2026-02-16\",\"message\":\"lib: delete unused local includes\\n\\nFiltered from `clang-tidy` `misc-include-cleaner` hits.\\n\\nAlso:\\n- pingping: scope includes.\\n- doh: say the reason for an include.\\n\\nCloses #20607\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: delete unused local includes\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/8fb87f5a4acecc44eab84fcac4ae5d73983915b7\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"31a4f41\",\"date\":\"2026-02-02\",\"message\":\"build: tidy up and dedupe `strdup` functions\\n\\n- de-dupe lib/src strdup/memdup functions into curlx.\\n- introduce `CURLX_STRDUP_LOW()` for mapping `strdup()`, and to do it at\\n  one place within the code, in `curl_setup.h`.\\n- tests/server: use `curlx_strdup()`. (Also to fix building without\\n  a system `strdup()`.)\\n- curlx/curlx.h: shorten and tidy up.\\n- adjust Windows build path to not need `HAVE_STRDUP`.\\n- build: stop detecting `HAVE_STRDUP` on Windows.\\n\\nCloses #20497\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: tidy up and dedupe `strdup` functions\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/31a4f415af112351ad54e59b2d6b03df4f4cb187\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"3003c32\",\"date\":\"2026-01-23\",\"message\":\"tidy-up: miscellaneous\\n\\n- INSTALL-CMAKE.md: add missing periods, text fixes.\\n- md4, md5: sync variables names.\\n- curl_trc: sync an argument type.\\n- docs/examples: sync debug/trace function copies, constify, tidy-ups.\\n- replace commented code with `#if 0`.\\n- drop redundant parenthesis (macro values, `return`, around single\\n  variables, function calls).\\n- fix indentation, apply clang-format in places.\\n\\nCloses #20481\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tidy-up: miscellaneous\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/3003c32cb29347478b8e2917ca5b3e2b37b4ae33\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"0e2507a\",\"date\":\"2026-01-25\",\"message\":\"build: constify `memchr()`/`strchr()`/etc result variables\\n\\nAnd a few variables around.\\n\\nThere remain cases where the accepted pointer is const, yet the returned\\npointer is written to.\\n\\nPartly addressing (glibc 2.43):\\n```\\n* For ISO C23, the functions bsearch, memchr, strchr, strpbrk, strrchr,\\n  strstr, wcschr, wcspbrk, wcsrchr, wcsstr and wmemchr that return\\n  pointers into their input arrays now have definitions as macros that\\n  return a pointer to a const-qualified type when the input argument is\\n  a pointer to a const-qualified type.\\n```\\nRef: https://lists.gnu.org/archive/html/info-gnu/2026... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: constify `memchr()`/`strchr()`/etc result variables\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/0e2507a3c65376d6bda860ff20bd94ada9bbb9fd\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"8edc033\",\"date\":\"2026-01-18\",\"message\":\"lib: separate scheme info from protocol implementation\\n\\nThis allows builds know about all schemes - but only have the protocol\\nimplementations for those actually built-in.\\n\\nIt further allows multiple protocols to reuse the same protocol setup\\nand functions for both TLS and non-TLS implementations instead of\\nneeding two (or more) structs.\\n\\nThe scheme information is now in 'struct Curl_scheme' and all the\\nfunction pointers for each scheme/protocol implementation are in struct\\nCurl_protocol.\\n\\nThe URL API now always work with all known protocols.\\n\\nCloses #20351\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: separate scheme info from protocol implementation\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/8edc0338f30f458f812f9ea355de1240771fa343\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"85c841c\",\"date\":\"2026-01-01\",\"message\":\"msvc: drop exception, make `BIT()` a bitfield with Visual Studio\\n\\nAdd casts to `bool`, or use `bit` type in local variables, where\\nneccessary to avoid MSVC compiler warnings C4242.\\n\\nNote: There may remain places needing the above updates, where not\\ntested in CI, and missed in manual review.\\n\\nAlso:\\n- urldata: convert struct field `connect_only` to bitfield to match its\\n  counterpart in another struct.\\n- rename curl-specific `bit` type to `curl_bit`.\\n\\nCloses #20142\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"msvc: drop exception, make `BIT()` a bitfield with Visual Studio\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/85c841cb45e5fe99ed3420adfa9c556fb397aefb\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"7032982\",\"date\":\"2025-12-20\",\"message\":\"tidy-up: miscellaneous\\n\\n- asyn-thrdd.c: scope an include.\\n- apply more clang-format suggestions.\\n- tidy-up PP guard comments.\\n- delete empty line from the top of headers.\\n- add empty line after `curl_setup.h` include where missing.\\n- fix indent.\\n- CODE_STYLE.md: add `strcpy`.\\n  Follow-up to 8636ad55dfbdbcc2809a61e65c3511baf5e7b0e8 #20088\\n- lib1901.c: drop unnecessary line.\\n  Follow-up to 436e67f65b9dee1e80aa063c39450f0d1df6ac72 #20076\\n\\nCloses #20070\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tidy-up: miscellaneous\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/70329828961a7de194c4c258bd5825afb1075888\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"18af4e4\",\"date\":\"2025-12-25\",\"message\":\"cookie: flush better\\n\\nThe cookie flushing (saving to a cookie jar) should only be done if a\\ntransfer has been started. This is now done by checking the\\ncookies->running field, which is not reset in curl_easy_reset() so the\\nsaving works correctly even after a call to that.\\n\\nFollow-up to fd6eb8d6e77d95e71c\\n\\nVerified by test 1920\\n\\nReported-by: Alexander Batischev\\nFixes #20090\\nCloses #20094\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: flush better\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/18af4e4e109f3825d1891c32aacb064fdd7e9dc4\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"e8415a8\",\"date\":\"2025-12-21\",\"message\":\"lib: drop, or replace `sendf.h` with `curl_trc.h` where possible\\n\\n- replace `sendf.h` with `curl_trc.h` where it was included just for it.\\n- drop unused `curl_trc.h` includes.\\n- easy: delete obsolete comment about `send.h` include reason.\\n\\nAlso:\\n- move out `curl_trc.h` include from `sendf.h` and include it directly\\n  in users, where not done already. To flatten the include tree and\\n  to less rely on indirect includes.\\n- stop including `sendf.h` from other headers, replace it with forward\\n  declaration of `Curl_easy`, as done already elsewhere.\\n\\nVerified with an all non-unity CI run.\\n\\nCloses #2... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: drop, or replace `sendf.h` with `curl_trc.h` where possible\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/e8415a8296f76c0f030b09f79eb75ebf8d71b8b8\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"6d0ee7b\",\"date\":\"2025-12-20\",\"message\":\"curlx: add `curlx_rename()`, fix to support long filenames on Windows\\n\\nMove existing `Curl_rename()` `rename()` wrapper from lib to\\ncurlx/fopen, and make it a curlx macro/function. To allow using\\nthe local worker function to fixup long filenames on Windows.\\n\\nThen fix the Windows-specific rename implementation to support long\\nfilenames. This operation may happen when using a cookie jar, HSTS cache\\nor alt-svc cache, via libcurl or the curl tool.\\n\\nBefore this patch, when passing a long filename to the above options,\\na `<random>.tmp` file was left on the disk without renaming it to the\\nfilename pa... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"curlx: add `curlx_rename()`, fix to support long filenames on Windows\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/6d0ee7b17b0c37c034a78314eda4148104187689\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"222fce0\",\"date\":\"2025-12-16\",\"message\":\"cookie. return proper error on OOM\\n\\nFollow-up to a78a07d3a9dc808a51\\n\\nCloses #19992\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie. return proper error on OOM\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/222fce0a50cf296c8633ec0330b074b133e163e1\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"a78a07d\",\"date\":\"2025-12-07\",\"message\":\"cookie: cleanups and improvements\\n\\n- Stricter cookie validation with earlier rejection of empty/invalid\\n  cookie names\\n\\n- secure and httponly attributes no longer accept = with empty values\\n  (only bare keywords)\\n\\n- Validation checks (length, TAB, prefixes) moved into the first\\n  name/value pair block for better code organization\\n\\n- Deferred time(NULL) calls for better performance when expires/max-age\\n  aren't used\\n\\n- Simplified loop control flow by removing done flag\\n\\n- The cookie size restriction now only applies to name + value, not other\\n  parts of the header line.\\n\\n- Fixed a gcc 4.8.1 qui... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: cleanups and improvements\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a78a07d3a9dc808a51f464d0dd297939e544e2a4\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"a093c93\",\"date\":\"2025-12-07\",\"message\":\"cookie: only keep and use the canonical cleaned up path\\n\\nInstead of keeping both versions around.\\n\\nCloses #19864\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: only keep and use the canonical cleaned up path\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a093c93994d2afed3beed94809844c961d3f4a64\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"524936f\",\"date\":\"2025-12-07\",\"message\":\"cookie: when parsing a cookie header, delay all allocations until okay\\n\\nTo avoid wasting time allocating data for incoming cookies that are\\ndiscarded for one reason or another, delay allocations until after\\nverifications are done.\\n\\nCloses #19864\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: when parsing a cookie header, delay all allocations until okay\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/524936fbebf2a37f5d02c97da2842fa6655a25bd\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"c6e5dfa\",\"date\":\"2025-12-07\",\"message\":\"cookie: allocate the main struct once cookie is fine\\n\\nThis delays the allocating of the cookie struct until after all the\\nchecks have been done, as many cookies are received and discarded\\ninstead of accepted and this then saves one allocation for every\\ndiscarded cookie.\\n\\nCloses #19864\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: allocate the main struct once cookie is fine\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c6e5dfa2c67570f98225959148a6e78c1e2180f2\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats With fixes to part 1.\\n\\npart 1: 47a1ab2ebecb21485c0e955316d90511e80a3c43 #19764\\n\\nCloses #19800\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: fix formatting nits (part 2)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/86b346443b68cde7ef33e1ab770e6c8ab641d2b1\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"47a1ab2\",\"date\":\"2025-11-29\",\"message\":\"lib: fix formatting nits (part 1)\\n\\nFrom `lib/a*` to `lib/cs*`.\\n\\nCloses #19764\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: fix formatting nits (part 1)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/47a1ab2ebecb21485c0e955316d90511e80a3c43\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"193cb00\",\"date\":\"2025-10-08\",\"message\":\"build: stop overriding standard memory allocation functions\\n\\nBefore this patch curl used the C preprocessor to override standard\\nmemory allocation symbols: malloc, calloc, strdup, realloc, free.\\nThe goal of these is to replace them with curl's debug wrappers in\\n`CURLDEBUG` builds, another was to replace them with the wrappers\\ncalling user-defined allocators in libcurl. This solution needed a bunch\\nof workarounds to avoid breaking external headers: it relied on include\\norder to do the overriding last. For \\\"unity\\\" builds it needed to reset\\noverrides before external includes. Also in test apps, w... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: stop overriding standard memory allocation functions\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/193cb00ce9b47e75d42157c650cc3de3fd96d35d\"}],\"limitHit\":true,\"query\":\"type:commit file:lib/cookie.c repo:github.com/curl/curl after:2024-12-01 count:20\",\"totalCount\":20}",

        "commit": "8edc033",
        "date": "2026-01-18",
        "message": "lib: separate scheme info from protocol implementation\n\nThis allows builds know about all schemes - but only have the protocol\nimplementations for those actually built-in.\n\nIt further allows multiple protocols to reuse the same protocol setup\nand functions for both TLS and non-TLS implementations instead of\nneeding two (or more) structs.\n\nThe scheme information is now in 'struct Curl_scheme' and all the\nfunction pointers for each scheme/protocol implementation are in struct\nCurl_protocol.\n\nThe URL API now always work with all known protocols.\n\nCloses #20351",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: separate scheme info from protocol implementation",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/8edc0338f30f458f812f9ea355de1240771fa343"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "85c841c",
        "date": "2026-01-01",
        "message": "msvc: drop exception, make `BIT()` a bitfield with Visual Studio\n\nAdd casts to `bool`, or use `bit` type in local variables, where\nneccessary to avoid MSVC compiler warnings C4242.\n\nNote: There may remain places needing the above updates, where not\ntested in CI, and missed in manual review.\n\nAlso:\n- urldata: convert struct field `connect_only` to bitfield to match its\n  counterpart in another struct.\n- rename curl-specific `bit` type to `curl_bit`.\n\nCloses #20142",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "msvc: drop exception, make `BIT()` a bitfield with Visual Studio",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/85c841cb45e5fe99ed3420adfa9c556fb397aefb"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "7032982",
        "date": "2025-12-20",
        "message": "tidy-up: miscellaneous\n\n- asyn-thrdd.c: scope an include.\n- apply more clang-format suggestions.\n- tidy-up PP guard comments.\n- delete empty line from the top of headers.\n- add empty line after `curl_setup.h` include where missing.\n- fix indent.\n- CODE_STYLE.md: add `strcpy`.\n  Follow-up to 8636ad55dfbdbcc2809a61e65c3511baf5e7b0e8 #20088\n- lib1901.c: drop unnecessary line.\n  Follow-up to 436e67f65b9dee1e80aa063c39450f0d1df6ac72 #20076\n\nCloses #20070",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tidy-up: miscellaneous",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/70329828961a7de194c4c258bd5825afb1075888"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "18af4e4",
        "date": "2025-12-25",
        "message": "cookie: flush better\n\nThe cookie flushing (saving to a cookie jar) should only be done if a\ntransfer has been started. This is now done by checking the\ncookies->running field, which is not reset in curl_easy_reset() so the\nsaving works correctly even after a call to that.\n\nFollow-up to fd6eb8d6e77d95e71c\n\nVerified by test 1920\n\nReported-by: Alexander Batischev\nFixes #20090\nCloses #20094",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: flush better",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/18af4e4e109f3825d1891c32aacb064fdd7e9dc4"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "e8415a8",
        "date": "2025-12-21",
        "message": "lib: drop, or replace `sendf.h` with `curl_trc.h` where possible\n\n- replace `sendf.h` with `curl_trc.h` where it was included just for it.\n- drop unused `curl_trc.h` includes.\n- easy: delete obsolete comment about `send.h` include reason.\n\nAlso:\n- move out `curl_trc.h` include from `sendf.h` and include it directly\n  in users, where not done already. To flatten the include tree and\n  to less rely on indirect includes.\n- stop including `sendf.h` from other headers, replace it with forward\n  declaration of `Curl_easy`, as done already elsewhere.\n\nVerified with an all non-unity CI run.\n\nCloses #2... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: drop, or replace `sendf.h` with `curl_trc.h` where possible",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/e8415a8296f76c0f030b09f79eb75ebf8d71b8b8"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "6d0ee7b",
        "date": "2025-12-20",
        "message": "curlx: add `curlx_rename()`, fix to support long filenames on Windows\n\nMove existing `Curl_rename()` `rename()` wrapper from lib to\ncurlx/fopen, and make it a curlx macro/function. To allow using\nthe local worker function to fixup long filenames on Windows.\n\nThen fix the Windows-specific rename implementation to support long\nfilenames. This operation may happen when using a cookie jar, HSTS cache\nor alt-svc cache, via libcurl or the curl tool.\n\nBefore this patch, when passing a long filename to the above options,\na `<random>.tmp` file was left on the disk without renaming it to the\nfilename pa... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "curlx: add `curlx_rename()`, fix to support long filenames on Windows",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/6d0ee7b17b0c37c034a78314eda4148104187689"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "222fce0",
        "date": "2025-12-16",
        "message": "cookie. return proper error on OOM\n\nFollow-up to a78a07d3a9dc808a51\n\nCloses #19992",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie. return proper error on OOM",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/222fce0a50cf296c8633ec0330b074b133e163e1"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "a78a07d",
        "date": "2025-12-07",
        "message": "cookie: cleanups and improvements\n\n- Stricter cookie validation with earlier rejection of empty/invalid\n  cookie names\n\n- secure and httponly attributes no longer accept = with empty values\n  (only bare keywords)\n\n- Validation checks (length, TAB, prefixes) moved into the first\n  name/value pair block for better code organization\n\n- Deferred time(NULL) calls for better performance when expires/max-age\n  aren't used\n\n- Simplified loop control flow by removing done flag\n\n- The cookie size restriction now only applies to name + value, not other\n  parts of the header line.\n\n- Fixed a gcc 4.8.1 qui... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: cleanups and improvements",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a78a07d3a9dc808a51f464d0dd297939e544e2a4"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "a093c93",
        "date": "2025-12-07",
        "message": "cookie: only keep and use the canonical cleaned up path\n\nInstead of keeping both versions around.\n\nCloses #19864",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: only keep and use the canonical cleaned up path",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a093c93994d2afed3beed94809844c961d3f4a64"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "524936f",
        "date": "2025-12-07",
        "message": "cookie: when parsing a cookie header, delay all allocations until okay\n\nTo avoid wasting time allocating data for incoming cookies that are\ndiscarded for one reason or another, delay allocations until after\nverifications are done.\n\nCloses #19864",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: when parsing a cookie header, delay all allocations until okay",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/524936fbebf2a37f5d02c97da2842fa6655a25bd"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "c6e5dfa",
        "date": "2025-12-07",
        "message": "cookie: allocate the main struct once cookie is fine\n\nThis delays the allocating of the cookie struct until after all the\nchecks have been done, as many cookies are received and discarded\ninstead of accepted and this then saves one allocation for every\ndiscarded cookie.\n\nCloses #19864",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "cookie: allocate the main struct once cookie is fine",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c6e5dfa2c67570f98225959148a6e78c1e2180f2"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "0476e4f",
        "date": "2025-11-29",
        "message": "tidy-up: one more round of formatting nits\n\nCloses #19835",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tidy-up: one more round of formatting nits",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/0476e4fc65a67c5d7e6d0229c4073ce2b8fd9325"
      },
      {
        "author": "Patrick Monnerat <patrick@monnerat.net>",
        "commit": "36542b7",
        "date": "2025-12-03",
        "message": "lib: turn state.url into a struct bufref\n\nCloses #19827",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: turn state.url into a struct bufref",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/36542b734966fce2ce5689d689a0e4922a103e50"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "86b3464",
        "date": "2025-11-27",
        "message": "lib: fix formatting nits (part 2)\n\nFrom `lib/curl*` to `lib/g*`. With fixes to part 1.\n\npart 1: 47a1ab2ebecb21485c0e955316d90511e80a3c43 #19764\n\nCloses #19800",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: fix formatting nits (part 2)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/86b346443b68cde7ef33e1ab770e6c8ab641d2b1"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",

        "date": "2025-11-29",
        "message": "lib: fix formatting nits (part 1)\n\nFrom `lib/a*` to `lib/cs*`.\n\nCloses #19764",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: fix formatting nits (part 1)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/47a1ab2ebecb21485c0e955316d90511e80a3c43"
