      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "193cb00",
        "date": "2025-10-08",
        "message": "build: stop overriding standard memory allocation functions\n\nBefore this patch curl used the C preprocessor to override standard\nmemory allocation symbols: malloc, calloc, strdup, realloc, free.\nThe goal of these is to replace them with curl's debug wrappers in\n`CURLDEBUG` builds, another was to replace them with the wrappers\ncalling user-defined allocators in libcurl. This solution needed a bunch\nof workarounds to avoid breaking external headers: it relied on include\norder to do the overriding last. For \"unity\" builds it needed to reset\noverrides before external includes. Also in test apps, w... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: stop overriding standard memory allocation functions",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/193cb00ce9b47e75d42157c650cc3de3fd96d35d"
      }
    ],
    "limitHit": true,
    "query": "type:commit file:lib/cookie.c repo:github.com/curl/curl after:2024-12-01 count:20",
    "totalCount": 20
  }
}

This solution needed a bunch\\nof workarounds to avoid breaking external headers: it relied on include\\norder to do the overriding last. For \\\"unity\\\" builds it needed to reset\\noverrides before external includes. Also in test apps, w... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: stop overriding standard memory allocation functions\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/193cb00ce9b47e75d42157c650cc3de3fd96d35d\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"bbb9291\",\"date\":\"2025-11-27\",\"message\":\"curlx_base64_encode: use uint8_t* for input\\n\\nChange `inputbuff` parameter from `const char *` to `const uint8_t *` to\\nreflect the binary nature of the input bytes. Half the code was casting\\nunsigned char to signed already in calling.\\n\\nCloses #19722\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"curlx_base64_encode: use uint8_t* for input\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/bbb929112b13643842eb9dbfa060bd83a5b5cf03\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"c5de083\",\"date\":\"2025-10-30\",\"message\":\"base64: make base64_encode() error on too long input\\n\\nThe maximum size is set to 16MB.\\n\\nIt should not possible to call this function with this large input, but\\nthis is a precaution to catch mistakes and replaces the earlier check on\\narchitectures with small size_t.\\n\\nCloses #19280\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"base64: make base64_encode() error on too long input\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c5de083bcc92e8edecbb4214b0ca5107496a80f6\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"5e2d4d7\",\"date\":\"2025-09-19\",\"message\":\"base64: accept zero length argument to base64_encode\\n\\nWe used to treat 0 as \\\"call strlen() to get the length\\\" for\\ncurlx_base64_encode, but it turns out this is rather fragile as we\\neasily do the mistake of passing in zero when the data is actually not\\nthere and then calling strlen() is wrong.\\n\\nForce the caller to pass in the correct size. A zero length input string\\nnow returns a zero length output and a NULL pointer.\\n\\nCloses #18617\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"base64: accept zero length argument to base64_encode\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/5e2d4d790575d4ad0381c4862f5e435a3b6532d1\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"c37e06c\",\"date\":\"2025-07-18\",\"message\":\"build: fix build errors/warnings in rare configurations\\n\\n- vtls: fix unused variable and symbols.\\n- ftp: fix unused variable.\\n- http: fix unused variables.\\n- smtp: fix unsued variable.\\n- wolfssl: fix unused variable with !proxy.\\n- libssh: fix unused argument.\\n- curl_trc: sync guards between declaration and definition.\\n- curl_trc: add missing guard for `Curl_trc_ssls` when !verbose.\\n- curl_trc: fix errors with !http + http3.\\n- curl_trc: fix missing function with !http + nghttp2.\\n- cf-h2-proxy: disable when !http + nghttp2, to avoid calling undeclared\\n  functions.\\n- sha256: fix missing declarati... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: fix build errors/warnings in rare configurations\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c37e06c642066b6cbf6b3c58278017ad40820bb3\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"fe81a80\",\"date\":\"2025-05-30\",\"message\":\"spelling: call it null-terminate consistently\\n\\nWith a dash, using two Ls. Also for different forms of the word.\\n\\nUse NULL in all uppercase if it means a zero pointer.\\n\\nFollow-up to 307b7543ea1e73ab04e062bdbe4b\\n\\nCloses #17489\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"spelling: call it null-terminate consistently\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/fe81a80ae72caedd1403769fc4c350e2f1afec43\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"6748363\",\"date\":\"2025-05-10\",\"message\":\"mime: reuse the base64 string from the base64 code\\n\\nAvoids duplicating an identical string here.\\n\\nCloses #17309\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"mime: reuse the base64 string from the base64 code\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/674836399fc90315abc3d8c82a2ea6a6a9dc87cd\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"ff84228\",\"date\":\"2025-05-10\",\"message\":\"base64: pass in the padding byte separately\\n\\nThe makes the string with base64 octets \\\"pure\\\" to be used elsewhere as\\nwell.\\n\\nCloses #17309\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"base64: pass in the padding byte separately\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/ff84228a5378ed7b866e8b72cceb6a78d7491958\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"255aac5\",\"date\":\"2025-05-07\",\"message\":\"curlx: move into to curlx/\\n\\nMove curlx_ functions into its own subdir.\\n\\nThe idea is to use the curlx_ prefix proper on these functions, and use\\nthese same function names both in tool, lib and test suite source code.\\nStop the previous special #define setup for curlx_ names.\\n\\nThe printf defines are now done for the library alone. Tests no longer\\nuse the printf defines. The tool code sets its own defines. The printf\\nfunctions are not curlx, they are publicly available.\\n\\nThe strcase defines are not curlx_ functions and should not be used by\\ntool or server code.\\n\\ndynbuf, warnless, base64, strparse,... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"curlx: move into to curlx/\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/255aac56f9773d7c138816bfe0390293f7da33a5\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"c0a70c5\",\"date\":\"2025-03-12\",\"message\":\"base64: drop `BUILDING_CURL` macro, always include in tests/server\\n\\nBefore this patch, building tests/server (or curl with winbuild) was\\nbroken in rare builds when many features were explicitly disabled.\\n\\nFix it by enabling base64 functions unconditionally when building\\nfor anything other than libcurl.\\n\\nCloses #16691\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"base64: drop `BUILDING_CURL` macro, always include in tests/server\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c0a70c564d5d60fca5fda093b1fb45063aff2386\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"f4e2395\",\"date\":\"2025-01-31\",\"message\":\"build: enable `-Wcast-qual`, fix or silence compiler warnings\\n\\nThe issues found fell into these categories, with the applied fixes:\\n\\n- const was accidentally stripped.\\n  Adjust code to not cast or cast with const.\\n\\n- const/volatile missing from arguments, local variables.\\n  Constify arguments or variables, adjust/delete casts. Small code\\n  changes in a few places.\\n\\n- const must be stripped because an API dependency requires it.\\n  Strip `const` with `CURL_UNCONST()` macro to silence the warning out\\n  of our control. These happen at API boundaries. Sometimes they depend\\n  on dependency version, ... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: enable `-Wcast-qual`, fix or silence compiler warnings\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/f4e23950c7b1c389cf0dde8b91353d85b8361b64\"}],\"limitHit\":false,\"query\":\"type:commit file:lib/base64.c|lib/curlx/base64.c repo:github.com/curl/curl after:2024-12-01 count:20\",\"totalCount\":16}",

      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "884b5ea",
        "date": "2025-12-20",
        "message": "lib: include `curlx/warnless.h` from `curl_setup.h`\n\nTo make it available for all files. Drop includes from individual\nsources. This header was already included from most sources and not\nspecific to any internal subsystem.\n\nAlso to ensure that two system symbol redefines on Windows (`read()` and\n`write()`) get applied to all sources. Move them to `curl_setup.h`.\n\nCloses #20056",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: include `curlx/warnless.h` from `curl_setup.h`",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/884b5ea921f2e6b4e9c6e5b6d47de25006f8c6e8"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "d11b859",
        "date": "2025-12-19",
        "message": "build: drop duplicate include `curl/curl.h` and others\n\n- curl_range: replace `sendf.h` with direct header dependency\n  `curl_trc.h`.\n- drop `curl/curl.h` includes from internal sourcees in favor of the\n  include made from `curl_setup.h`. Replace it with the latter where\n  it's the only include.\n- include `curl_setup.h` before using macros, where missing.\n- drop redundant `stdlib.h`, `string.h` includes, in favor of\n  `curl_setup_once.h` including them.\n- drop redundant `limits.h` in favor of `curl_setup.h` including it.\n- fake_addrinfo.h: fix typo in comment.\n- curl_setup_once.h: drop `stdio.... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: drop duplicate include `curl/curl.h` and others",

      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "af5def0",
        "date": "2025-12-04",
        "message": "tidy-up: avoid `(())`, clang-format fixes and more\n\n- drop redundant parentheses from macro definitions.\n- apply clang-format in some places missed earlier.\n- wolfssl: fix a macro guard comment.\n- curl_setup.h: drop empty lines\n- FAQ: fix C formatting.\n\nCloses #19854",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tidy-up: avoid `(())`, clang-format fixes and more",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/af5def0738dd53db84a23221be9bf352d8708917"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "2253bc3",
        "date": "2025-11-29",
        "message": "lib/subdirs: fix formatting nits\n\nCloses #19757",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib/subdirs: fix formatting nits",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/2253bc330f56315faaa287c29d7612f5a34d8058"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "193cb00",
        "date": "2025-10-08",
        "message": "build: stop overriding standard memory allocation functions\n\nBefore this patch curl used the C preprocessor to override standard\nmemory allocation symbols: malloc, calloc, strdup, realloc, free.\nThe goal of these is to replace them with curl's debug wrappers in\n`CURLDEBUG` builds, another was to replace them with the wrappers\ncalling user-defined allocators in libcurl. This solution needed a bunch\nof workarounds to avoid breaking external headers: it relied on include\norder to do the overriding last. For \"unity\" builds it needed to reset\noverrides before external includes. Also in test apps, w... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: stop overriding standard memory allocation functions",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/193cb00ce9b47e75d42157c650cc3de3fd96d35d"
      },
      {
        "author": "Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>",
        "commit": "bbb9291",
        "date": "2025-11-27",
        "message": "curlx_base64_encode: use uint8_t* for input\n\nChange `inputbuff` parameter from `const char *` to `const uint8_t *` to\nreflect the binary nature of the input bytes. Half the code was casting\nunsigned char to signed already in calling.\n\nCloses #19722",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "curlx_base64_encode: use uint8_t* for input",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/bbb929112b13643842eb9dbfa060bd83a5b5cf03"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "c5de083",
        "date": "2025-10-30",
        "message": "base64: make base64_encode() error on too long input\n\nThe maximum size is set to 16MB.\n\nIt should not possible to call this function with this large input, but\nthis is a precaution to catch mistakes and replaces the earlier check on\narchitectures with small size_t.\n\nCloses #19280",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "base64: make base64_encode() error on too long input",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c5de083bcc92e8edecbb4214b0ca5107496a80f6"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "5e2d4d7",
        "date": "2025-09-19",
        "message": "base64: accept zero length argument to base64_encode\n\nWe used to treat 0 as \"call strlen() to get the length\" for\ncurlx_base64_encode, but it turns out this is rather fragile as we\neasily do the mistake of passing in zero when the data is actually not\nthere and then calling strlen() is wrong.\n\nForce the caller to pass in the correct size. A zero length input string\nnow returns a zero length output and a NULL pointer.\n\nCloses #18617",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "base64: accept zero length argument to base64_encode",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/5e2d4d790575d4ad0381c4862f5e435a3b6532d1"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "c37e06c",
        "date": "2025-07-18",
        "message": "build: fix build errors/warnings in rare configurations\n\n- vtls: fix unused variable and symbols.\n- ftp: fix unused variable.\n- http: fix unused variables.\n- smtp: fix unsued variable.\n- wolfssl: fix unused variable with !proxy.\n- libssh: fix unused argument.\n- curl_trc: sync guards between declaration and definition.\n- curl_trc: add missing guard for `Curl_trc_ssls` when !verbose.\n- curl_trc: fix errors with !http + http3.\n- curl_trc: fix missing function with !http + nghttp2.\n- cf-h2-proxy: disable when !http + nghttp2, to avoid calling undeclared\n  functions.\n- sha256: fix missing declarati... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: fix build errors/warnings in rare configurations",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c37e06c642066b6cbf6b3c58278017ad40820bb3"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "fe81a80",
        "date": "2025-05-30",
        "message": "spelling: call it null-terminate consistently\n\nWith a dash, using two Ls. Also for different forms of the word.\n\nUse NULL in all uppercase if it means a zero pointer.\n\nFollow-up to 307b7543ea1e73ab04e062bdbe4b\n\nCloses #17489",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "spelling: call it null-terminate consistently",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/fe81a80ae72caedd1403769fc4c350e2f1afec43"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "6748363",
        "date": "2025-05-10",
        "message": "mime: reuse the base64 string from the base64 code\n\nAvoids duplicating an identical string here.\n\nCloses #17309",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "mime: reuse the base64 string from the base64 code",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/674836399fc90315abc3d8c82a2ea6a6a9dc87cd"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "ff84228",
        "date": "2025-05-10",
        "message": "base64: pass in the padding byte separately\n\nThe makes the string with base64 octets \"pure\" to be used elsewhere as\nwell.\n\nCloses #17309",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "base64: pass in the padding byte separately",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/ff84228a5378ed7b866e8b72cceb6a78d7491958"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "255aac5",
        "date": "2025-05-07",
        "message": "curlx: move into to curlx/\n\nMove curlx_ functions into its own subdir.\n\nThe idea is to use the curlx_ prefix proper on these functions, and use\nthese same function names both in tool, lib and test suite source code.\nStop the previous special #define setup for curlx_ names.\n\nThe printf defines are now done for the library alone. Tests no longer\nuse the printf defines. The tool code sets its own defines. The printf\nfunctions are not curlx, they are publicly available.\n\nThe strcase defines are not curlx_ functions and should not be used by\ntool or server code.\n\ndynbuf, warnless, base64, strparse,... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "curlx: move into to curlx/",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/255aac56f9773d7c138816bfe0390293f7da33a5"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "c0a70c5",
        "date": "2025-03-12",
        "message": "base64: drop `BUILDING_CURL` macro, always include in tests/server\n\nBefore this patch, building tests/server (or curl with winbuild) was\nbroken in rare builds when many features were explicitly disabled.\n\nFix it by enabling base64 functions unconditionally when building\nfor anything other than libcurl.\n\nCloses #16691",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "base64: drop `BUILDING_CURL` macro, always include in tests/server",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c0a70c564d5d60fca5fda093b1fb45063aff2386"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "f4e2395",
        "date": "2025-01-31",
        "message": "build: enable `-Wcast-qual`, fix or silence compiler warnings\n\nThe issues found fell into these categories, with the applied fixes:\n\n- const was accidentally stripped.\n  Adjust code to not cast or cast with const.\n\n- const/volatile missing from arguments, local variables.\n  Constify arguments or variables, adjust/delete casts. Small code\n  changes in a few places.\n\n- const must be stripped because an API dependency requires it.\n  Strip `const` with `CURL_UNCONST()` macro to silence the warning out\n  of our control. These happen at API boundaries. Sometimes they depend\n  on dependency version, ... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: enable `-Wcast-qual`, fix or silence compiler warnings",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/f4e23950c7b1c389cf0dde8b91353d85b8361b64"
      }
    ],

curl_trc: sync an argument type.\\n- docs/examples: sync debug/trace function copies, constify, tidy-ups.\\n- replace commented code with `#if 0`.\\n- drop redundant parenthesis (macro values, `return`, around single\\n  variables, function calls).\\n- fix indentation, apply clang-format in places.\\n\\nCloses #20481\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tidy-up: miscellaneous\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/3003c32cb29347478b8e2917ca5b3e2b37b4ae33\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"8ce16e7\",\"date\":\"2026-01-19\",\"message\":\"timeout handling: auto-detect effective timeout\\n\\nWhen checking a transfer for being expired via `Curl_timeleft_ms()`,\\neleminate the `bool connecting` parameter and have the function check\\nthe `mstate` of the transfer instead.\\n\\nAdvantages:\\n* eleminate the caller needing awareness if the transfer is\\n  connecting or in a later state\\n* fix pingpong timeout handling to check the correct timeout\\n  during \\\"proto_connect\\\" phases\\n* avoid using \\\"connecting\\\" timeouts during establishing a secondary\\n  connection (e.g. FTP) since this would use the timestamp from\\n  the original, primary connect and thus be... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"timeout handling: auto-detect effective timeout\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/8ce16e7bf29925eb89b602861623695efc7a4af6\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"8edc033\",\"date\":\"2026-01-18\",\"message\":\"lib: separate scheme info from protocol implementation\\n\\nThis allows builds know about all schemes - but only have the protocol\\nimplementations for those actually built-in.\\n\\nIt further allows multiple protocols to reuse the same protocol setup\\nand functions for both TLS and non-TLS implementations instead of\\nneeding two (or more) structs.\\n\\nThe scheme information is now in 'struct Curl_scheme' and all the\\nfunction pointers for each scheme/protocol implementation are in struct\\nCurl_protocol.\\n\\nThe URL API now always work with all known protocols.\\n\\nCloses #20351\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: separate scheme info from protocol implementation\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/8edc0338f30f458f812f9ea355de1240771fa343\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats Replace it with the latter where\\n  it's the only include.\\n- include `curl_setup.h` before using macros, where missing.\\n- drop redundant `stdlib.h`, `string.h` includes, in favor of\\n  `curl_setup_once.h` including them.\\n- drop redundant `limits.h` in favor of `curl_setup.h` including it.\\n- fake_addrinfo.h: fix typo in comment.\\n- curl_setup_once.h: drop `stdio.... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: drop duplicate include `curl/curl.h` and others\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/d11b8593a29442a890a8d910efad25f1fcc80391\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"b4be1f2\",\"date\":\"2025-12-18\",\"message\":\"time-keeping: keep timestamp in multi, always update\\n\\nAlways use curlx_now() when calling Curl_pgrs_now(data). Tests with the\\n\\\"manual\\\" updates to now proved differ more then 100ms in parallel testing.\\n\\nAdd `curlx_nowp()` to set current time into a struct curltime.\\nAdd `curlx_ptimediff_ms() and friends, passing pointers.\\n\\nUpdate documentation.\\n\\nCloses #19998\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"time-keeping: keep timestamp in multi, always update\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/b4be1f271e8e2010be89b1cc243fc516cc00ac80\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"2de22a0\",\"date\":\"2025-12-15\",\"message\":\"lib: keep timestamp in easy handle\\n\\nUse `data->progress.now` as the timestamp of proecssing a transfer.\\nUpdate it on significant events and refrain from calling `curlx_now()`\\nin many places.\\n\\nThe problem this addresses is\\na) calling curlx_now() has costs, depending on platform. Calling it\\n   every time results in 25% increase `./runtest` duration on macOS.\\nb) we used to pass a `struct curltime *` around to save on calls, but\\n   when some method directly use `curx_now()` and some use the passed\\n   pointer, the transfer experienes non-linear time. This results in\\n   timeline checks to report eve... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: keep timestamp in easy handle\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/2de22a00c7adb81b4e5cbc90785e29b4b083c1ed\"},{\"author\":\"Christian Schmitz <support@monkeybreadsoftware.de>\",\"commit\":\"624d98e\",\"date\":\"2025-12-11\",\"message\":\"transfer: remove @param for err for xfer_recv_resp function.\\n\\nThe err parameter in xfer_recv_resp doesn't exist anymore. Removed in\\ncb2bcb681fc9044d2a7adaaa621.\\n\\nCloses #19937\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"transfer: remove @param for err for xfer_recv_resp function.\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/624d98e79ccde1304f40967509b56c32feb2da48\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"d517efe\",\"date\":\"2025-12-04\",\"message\":\"bufref: add Curl_bufref_dup that returns a strdup()ed version\\n\\nCleans up a common pattern somewhat. Implemented as a macro.\\n\\nCloses #19834\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"bufref: add Curl_bufref_dup that returns a strdup()ed version\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/d517efe5bd1dbcc6faafe6b0c123521f96d955b0\"},{\"author\":\"Patrick Monnerat <patrick@monnerat.net>\",\"commit\":\"36542b7\",\"date\":\"2025-12-03\",\"message\":\"lib: turn state.url into a struct bufref\\n\\nCloses #19827\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: turn state.url into a struct bufref\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/36542b734966fce2ce5689d689a0e4922a103e50\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"c3b030b\",\"date\":\"2025-12-02\",\"message\":\"lib: fix formatting nits (part 3)\\n\\nFrom `lib/h` to `lib/w`.\\n\\npart 1: 47a1ab2ebecb21485c0e955316d90511e80a3c43 #19764\\npart 2: 86b346443b68cde7ef33e1ab770e6c8ab641d2b1 #19800\\n\\nCloses #19811\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: fix formatting nits (part 3)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c3b030b860a2c1b2a74e032bb82b981d2f806249\"}],\"limitHit\":true,\"query\":\"type:commit file:lib/transfer.c repo:github.com/curl/curl after:2024-12-01 count:20\",\"totalCount\":20}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "commits": [
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "8fb87f5",
        "date": "2026-02-16",
        "message": "lib: delete unused local includes\n\nFiltered from `clang-tidy` `misc-include-cleaner` hits.\n\nAlso:\n- pingping: scope includes.\n- doh: say the reason for an include.\n\nCloses #20607",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: delete unused local includes",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/8fb87f5a4acecc44eab84fcac4ae5d73983915b7"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "61df5f4",
        "date": "2026-02-01",
        "message": "tidy-up: miscellaneous\n\n- curl_ntlm_core, smtp, schannel: fix comments.\n- curl_setup.h: fix to undef before define.\n- tool_doswin, server/sockfilt: reduce variables scopes.\n- tool_doswin: drop an interim variable.\n- windows: replace `SOCKET_ERROR` with `0` to align with rest of code.\n- libssh2: rename variable to align with rest of code.\n- gtls, unit1398: use `#if 0`.\n- curl_trc.h, curlx/inet_ntop.h: add missing parentheses in macro\n  expressions.\n- ldap.c: set empty macro to `do {} while(0)`.\n- examples/crawler: rename a non-CURLcode `result` variable.\n- CURLINFO_TLS_SESSION: drop stray colon... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tidy-up: miscellaneous",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/61df5f466ca9a3f6b731e7be65c6c955a63f9028"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "982ab7b",
        "date": "2026-02-11",
        "message": "build: check `MSG_NOSIGNAL` directly, drop detection and interim macro\n\nDrop detecting it at configure time, along with the interim macro\n`HAVE_MSG_NOSIGNAL`. There is no longer a reason for this workaround,\nand allows to save the work at configure time and simplify.\n\nAlso say in a comment that `sys/socket.h` is defining this macro.\n\nFollow-up to 77b3bc239daf75d9fb7702ee34c8e5871c47d387\n\nCloses #20559",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: check `MSG_NOSIGNAL` directly, drop detection and interim macro",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/982ab7b53fdcef9db636e2f02b137579787e0837"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "3003c32",
        "date": "2026-01-23",
        "message": "tidy-up: miscellaneous\n\n- INSTALL-CMAKE.md: add missing periods, text fixes.\n- md4, md5: sync variables names.\n- curl_trc: sync an argument type.\n- docs/examples: sync debug/trace function copies, constify, tidy-ups.\n- replace commented code with `#if 0`.\n- drop redundant parenthesis (macro values, `return`, around single\n  variables, function calls).\n- fix indentation, apply clang-format in places.\n\nCloses #20481",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tidy-up: miscellaneous",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/3003c32cb29347478b8e2917ca5b3e2b37b4ae33"
      },
      {
        "author": "Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>",
        "commit": "8ce16e7",
        "date": "2026-01-19",
        "message": "timeout handling: auto-detect effective timeout\n\nWhen checking a transfer for being expired via `Curl_timeleft_ms()`,\neleminate the `bool connecting` parameter and have the function check\nthe `mstate` of the transfer instead.\n\nAdvantages:\n* eleminate the caller needing awareness if the transfer is\n  connecting or in a later state\n* fix pingpong timeout handling to check the correct timeout\n  during \"proto_connect\" phases\n* avoid using \"connecting\" timeouts during establishing a secondary\n  connection (e.g. FTP) since this would use the timestamp from\n  the original, primary connect and thus be... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "timeout handling: auto-detect effective timeout",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/8ce16e7bf29925eb89b602861623695efc7a4af6"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "8edc033",
        "date": "2026-01-18",
        "message": "lib: separate scheme info from protocol implementation\n\nThis allows builds know about all schemes - but only have the protocol\nimplementations for those actually built-in.\n\nIt further allows multiple protocols to reuse the same protocol setup\nand functions for both TLS and non-TLS implementations instead of\nneeding two (or more) structs.\n\nThe scheme information is now in 'struct Curl_scheme' and all the\nfunction pointers for each scheme/protocol implementation are in struct\nCurl_protocol.\n\nThe URL API now always work with all known protocols.\n\nCloses #20351",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: separate scheme info from protocol implementation",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/8edc0338f30f458f812f9ea355de1240771fa343"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "7032982",
        "date": "2025-12-20",
        "message": "tidy-up: miscellaneous\n\n- asyn-thrdd.c: scope an include.\n- apply more clang-format suggestions.\n- tidy-up PP guard comments.\n- delete empty line from the top of headers.\n- add empty line after `curl_setup.h` include where missing.\n- fix indent.\n- CODE_STYLE.md: add `strcpy`.\n  Follow-up to 8636ad55dfbdbcc2809a61e65c3511baf5e7b0e8 #20088\n- lib1901.c: drop unnecessary line.\n  Follow-up to 436e67f65b9dee1e80aa063c39450f0d1df6ac72 #20076\n\nCloses #20070",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tidy-up: miscellaneous",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/70329828961a7de194c4c258bd5825afb1075888"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "e78a466",
        "date": "2025-12-24",
        "message": "lib: drop unused protocol headers\n\n- drop unused `http.h` includes.\n- drop unused `http1.h` include.\n- drop unused `http2.h` includes.\n- vssh/ssh.h: drop unused `vssh.h` include.\n- urldata.h: drop unused protocol includes.\n- url: include `smtp.h` directly.\n- rtsp.h: include directly where used.\n- imap, smtp: drop redundant include, move another from .h to .c.\n\nVerified with an all non-unity CI run.\n\nCloses #20093",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: drop unused protocol headers",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/e78a466ebddd35d25b14487ef4a03081e13773fe"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "e8415a8",
        "date": "2025-12-21",
        "message": "lib: drop, or replace `sendf.h` with `curl_trc.h` where possible\n\n- replace `sendf.h` with `curl_trc.h` where it was included just for it.\n- drop unused `curl_trc.h` includes.\n- easy: delete obsolete comment about `send.h` include reason.\n\nAlso:\n- move out `curl_trc.h` include from `sendf.h` and include it directly\n  in users, where not done already. To flatten the include tree and\n  to less rely on indirect includes.\n- stop including `sendf.h` from other headers, replace it with forward\n  declaration of `Curl_easy`, as done already elsewhere.\n\nVerified with an all non-unity CI run.\n\nCloses #2... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: drop, or replace `sendf.h` with `curl_trc.h` where possible",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/e8415a8296f76c0f030b09f79eb75ebf8d71b8b8"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "884b5ea",
        "date": "2025-12-20",
        "message": "lib: include `curlx/warnless.h` from `curl_setup.h`\n\nTo make it available for all files. Drop includes from individual\nsources. This header was already included from most sources and not\nspecific to any internal subsystem.\n\nAlso to ensure that two system symbol redefines on Windows (`read()` and\n`write()`) get applied to all sources. Move them to `curl_setup.h`.\n\nCloses #20056",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: include `curlx/warnless.h` from `curl_setup.h`",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/884b5ea921f2e6b4e9c6e5b6d47de25006f8c6e8"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "a3f866a",
        "date": "2025-12-21",
        "message": "transfer: drop unused `vquic/vquic.h` include\n\nCloses #20059",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "transfer: drop unused `vquic/vquic.h` include",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a3f866ac76fab51b3ec76d6bcc92629c5aaa1f30"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "fdb5152",
        "date": "2025-12-21",
        "message": "lib: drop unused `vtls/vtls.h` includes\n\nCloses #20057",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: drop unused `vtls/vtls.h` includes",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/fdb5152091ce6d1366bec6745fb86019de3d4d5c"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "a354cc8",
        "date": "2025-12-20",
        "message": "lib: drop includes unused or duplicate\n\nCloses #20051",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: drop includes unused or duplicate",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a354cc866472400958a28b75e333579980261f28"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "d11b859",
        "date": "2025-12-19",
        "message": "build: drop duplicate include `curl/curl.h` and others\n\n- curl_range: replace `sendf.h` with direct header dependency\n  `curl_trc.h`.\n- drop `curl/curl.h` includes from internal sourcees in favor of the\n  include made from `curl_setup.h`. Replace it with the latter where\n  it's the only include.\n- include `curl_setup.h` before using macros, where missing.\n- drop redundant `stdlib.h`, `string.h` includes, in favor of\n  `curl_setup_once.h` including them.\n- drop redundant `limits.h` in favor of `curl_setup.h` including it.\n- fake_addrinfo.h: fix typo in comment.\n- curl_setup_once.h: drop `stdio.... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: drop duplicate include `curl/curl.h` and others",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/d11b8593a29442a890a8d910efad25f1fcc80391"
      },
      {
        "author": "Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>",
        "commit": "b4be1f2",
        "date": "2025-12-18",
        "message": "time-keeping: keep timestamp in multi, always update\n\nAlways use curlx_now() when calling Curl_pgrs_now(data). Tests with the\n\"manual\" updates to now proved differ more then 100ms in parallel testing.\n\nAdd `curlx_nowp()` to set current time into a struct curltime.\nAdd `curlx_ptimediff_ms() and friends, passing pointers.\n\nUpdate documentation.\n\nCloses #19998",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "time-keeping: keep timestamp in multi, always update",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/b4be1f271e8e2010be89b1cc243fc516cc00ac80"
      },
      {
        "author": "Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>",
        "commit": "2de22a0",
        "date": "2025-12-15",
        "message": "lib: keep timestamp in easy handle\n\nUse `data->progress.now` as the timestamp of proecssing a transfer.\nUpdate it on significant events and refrain from calling `curlx_now()`\nin many places.\n\nThe problem this addresses is\na) calling curlx_now() has costs, depending on platform. Calling it\n   every time results in 25% increase `./runtest` duration on macOS.\nb) we used to pass a `struct curltime *` around to save on calls, but\n   when some method directly use `curx_now()` and some use the passed\n   pointer, the transfer experienes non-linear time. This results in\n   timeline checks to report eve... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: keep timestamp in easy handle",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/2de22a00c7adb81b4e5cbc90785e29b4b083c1ed"
      },
      {
        "author": "Christian Schmitz <support@monkeybreadsoftware.de>",
        "commit": "624d98e",
        "date": "2025-12-11",
        "message": "transfer: remove @param for err for xfer_recv_resp function.\n\nThe err parameter in xfer_recv_resp doesn't exist anymore. Removed in\ncb2bcb681fc9044d2a7adaaa621.\n\nCloses #19937",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "transfer: remove @param for err for xfer_recv_resp function.",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/624d98e79ccde1304f40967509b56c32feb2da48"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "d517efe",
        "date": "2025-12-04",
        "message": "bufref: add Curl_bufref_dup that returns a strdup()ed version\n\nCleans up a common pattern somewhat. Implemented as a macro.\n\nCloses #19834",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "bufref: add Curl_bufref_dup that returns a strdup()ed version",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/d517efe5bd1dbcc6faafe6b0c123521f96d955b0"
      },
      {
        "author": "Patrick Monnerat <patrick@monnerat.net>",
        "commit": "36542b7",
        "date": "2025-12-03",
        "message": "lib: turn state.url into a struct bufref\n\nCloses #19827",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: turn state.url into a struct bufref",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/36542b734966fce2ce5689d689a0e4922a103e50"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "c3b030b",
        "date": "2025-12-02",
        "message": "lib: fix formatting nits (part 3)\n\nFrom `lib/h` to `lib/w`.\n\npart 1: 47a1ab2ebecb21485c0e955316d90511e80a3c43 #19764\npart 2: 86b346443b68cde7ef33e1ab770e6c8ab641d2b1 #19800\n\nCloses #19811",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "lib: fix formatting nits (part 3)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c3b030b860a2c1b2a74e032bb82b981d2f806249"
      }
    ],
    "limitHit": true,
    "query": "type:commit file:lib/transfer.c repo:github.com/curl/curl after:2024-12-01 count:20",
    "totalCount": 20
  }
}

  "content": "{\"base\":\"c5de083~1\",\"endCursor\":\"\",\"files\":[{\"added\":4,\"deleted\":4,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\"   if(!insize)\\n     return CURLE_OK;\\n \\n-#if SIZEOF_SIZE_T == 4\\n-  if(insize > UINT_MAX/4)\\n-    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\\n-#endif\\n+  /* safety precaution */\\n+  DEBUGASSERT(insize <= CURL_MAX_BASE64_INPUT);\\n+  if(insize > CURL_MAX_BASE64_INPUT)\\n+    return CURLE_TOO_LARGE;\\n \\n   base64data = output = malloc((insize + 2) / 3 * 4 + 1);\\n   if(!output)\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":10,\"startLine\":184},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":10,\"startLine\":184},\"section\":\"static CURLcode base64_encode(const char *table64,\"}],\"path\":\"lib/curlx/base64.c\"},{\"added\":4,\"deleted\":0,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" \\n extern const char Curl_base64encdec[];\\n \\n+/* maximum input length acceptable to base64 encode, here to catch and prevent\\n+   mistakes */\\n+#define CURL_MAX_BASE64_INPUT 16000000\\n+\\n #endif /* HEADER_CURL_BASE64_H */\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":33},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":4,\"startLine\":33},\"section\":\"CURLcode curlx_base64_decode(const char *src,\"}],\"path\":\"lib/curlx/base64.h\"}],\"hasNextPage\":false,\"head\":\"c5de083\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"totalAdded\":8,\"totalDeleted\":4,\"totalModified\":2}",

81. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_commit_search @ -
Input
{
  "after": "2024-12-01",
  "count": 20,
  "files": [
    "lib/vtls/openssl.c"
  ],
  "repos": [
    "github.com/curl/curl"
  ]
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"commits\":[{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"5fa5cb3\",\"date\":\"2026-02-13\",\"message\":\"build: fix `-Wunused-macros` warnings, and related tidy-ups\\n\\n- fix internal macro `AN_APPLE_OS` reused between sources without\\n  resetting it. It may potentially have left the system sha256\\n  function unused.\\n- fix to define `WOLFSSL_OPTIONS_IGNORE_SYS` so that it always applies\\n  to wolfSSL headers, also during feature detection.\\n- md4, md5, sha256: simplify fallback logic.\\n- delete 20+ unused macros.\\n- scope or move macros to avoid `-Wunused-macros` warnings.\\n- examples: delete unused code.\\n\\nThe warning detects macros defined but not used within the same C\\nsource. It does not warn for macros... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: fix `-Wunused-macros` warnings, and related tidy-ups\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/5fa5cb382560316a55f0954f1e8cebdbd6568cfb\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"dc08922\",\"date\":\"2026-02-17\",\"message\":\"openssl: disable local keylog feature if built-in upstream\\n\\nReported-by: Paul Howarth\\nFixes #20611\\n\\nCloses #20614\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"openssl: disable local keylog feature if built-in upstream\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/dc08922a61efe546b318daf964514ffbf4158325\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"61df5f4\",\"date\":\"2026-02-01\",\"message\":\"tidy-up: miscellaneous\\n\\n- curl_ntlm_core, smtp, schannel: fix comments.\\n- curl_setup.h: fix to undef before define.\\n- tool_doswin, server/sockfilt: reduce variables scopes.\\n- tool_doswin: drop an interim variable.\\n- windows: replace `SOCKET_ERROR` with `0` to align with rest of code.\\n- libssh2: rename variable to align with rest of code.\\n- gtls, unit1398: use `#if 0`.\\n- curl_trc.h, curlx/inet_ntop.h: add missing parentheses in macro\\n  expressions.\\n- ldap.c: set empty macro to `do {} while(0)`.\\n- examples/crawler: rename a non-CURLcode `result` variable.\\n- CURLINFO_TLS_SESSION: drop stray colon... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tidy-up: miscellaneous\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/61df5f466ca9a3f6b731e7be65c6c955a63f9028\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"2a92c39\",\"date\":\"2026-02-11\",\"message\":\"windows: tidy up `wincrypt.h` / BoringSSL/AWS-LC coexist workaround\\n\\n- openssl: move and expand explanatory comment.\\n\\n- openssl: drop duplicate workaround.\\n\\n- schannel: drop workaround. Unnecessary, because OpenSSL headers are\\n  not included in or after schannel code.\\n\\n- schannel: drop explicit `wincrypt.h` include. It's indirectly\\n  included by system `<schannel.h>`.\\n\\n- ldap: drop explicit `wincrypt.h` include.\\n  It isn't used there, and also not required for the workaround.\\n  `winldap.h` keeps including it indirectly.\\n\\nTested with BoringSSL and AWS-LC (MultiSSL with Schannel), also LDAP\\nenab... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"windows: tidy up `wincrypt.h` / BoringSSL/AWS-LC coexist workaround\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/2a92c39a218713635768364f801fa13831a019e0\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"85de995\",\"date\":\"2026-02-07\",\"message\":\"tidy-up: move literals to right-side of `if` expressions (where missing)\\n\\nCloses #20535\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tidy-up: move literals to right-side of `if` expressions (where missing)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/85de99520880e7627b867d56a04f992a40cc99b5\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"31a4f41\",\"date\":\"2026-02-02\",\"message\":\"build: tidy up and dedupe `strdup` functions\\n\\n- de-dupe lib/src strdup/memdup functions into curlx.\\n- introduce `CURLX_STRDUP_LOW()` for mapping `strdup()`, and to do it at\\n  one place within the code, in `curl_setup.h`.\\n- tests/server: use `curlx_strdup()`. (Also to fix building without\\n  a system `strdup()`.)\\n- curlx/curlx.h: shorten and tidy up.\\n- adjust Windows build path to not need `HAVE_STRDUP`.\\n- build: stop detecting `HAVE_STRDUP` on Windows.\\n\\nCloses #20497\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: tidy up and dedupe `strdup` functions\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/31a4f415af112351ad54e59b2d6b03df4f4cb187\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"9c3fcb8\",\"date\":\"2026-01-29\",\"message\":\"vtls: dedupe common on-session-reuse logic\\n\\nCloses #20475\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"vtls: dedupe common on-session-reuse logic\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/9c3fcb8a321882232409f9b05b07098821378219\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"1b50dcc\",\"date\":\"2026-01-27\",\"message\":\"tls: remove checks for DEFAULT\\n\\nSince 9d8998c99408e1adf, the setopt code changes input DEFAULT to an\\nactual more specific TLS version (1.2) for the backends to use and check\\nfor.\\n\\nThis means that the default value (0L) cannot and should not actually be\\nused when the TLS backends run. This change adds asserts to verify that\\nand removes code that accepts the DEFAULT value as a valid version with\\nthe TLS version functions' logic.\\n\\nApplications can still set a specific lower version if they want (1, 1.0\\nor 1.1).\\n\\nCloses #20453\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tls: remove checks for DEFAULT\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/1b50dcc2abd40d60d16c7c9b2fcbfc093c0419d3\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"065b149\",\"date\":\"2026-01-27\",\"message\":\"OpenSSL: check reuse of sessions for verify status\\n\\nOpenSSL records its peer verification status inside its SSL_SESSION\\nobjects. When a session is later reused, the SSL connection inherits\\nthis verify status.\\n\\nSession keys prevent reuse of sessions between connections that verify\\nthe peer and those who do not. However, when Apple SecTrust is used\\nto verify a connection, this does not update the Sessions verify\\nstatus (and there is no setter). On session reuse, OpenSSL fails\\nthe verification and Apple SecTrust cannot verify either since the\\ncertificate peer chain is not available.\\n\\nFix this by ... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"OpenSSL: check reuse of sessions for verify status\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/065b149df07256174891dfcde307c39c98908d5f\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"b81341e\",\"date\":\"2026-01-20\",\"message\":\"tidy-up: Markdown, clang-format nits\\n\\n- drop leading indent from Markdown.\\n- switch to Markdown section markers where missing.\\n- move `&&` and `||` to the end of the line (C, Perl).\\n- openssl: add parenthesis to an if sub-expression.\\n- misc clang-format nits.\\n- unfold Markdown links.\\n- SSL-PROBLEMS.md: drop stray half code-fence.\\n\\nCloses #20402\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tidy-up: Markdown, clang-format nits\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/b81341e8f557a719ea9c9c28ffd4385ac3e36f64\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"61093e2\",\"date\":\"2026-01-19\",\"message\":\"build: fully omit verbose strings and code when disabled\\n\\nWhen the compiler supports C99.\\n\\n- map logging functions to macro stubs when verbose logging is disabled\\n  and the compiler is C99. Make sure these stubs silence unused variable\\n  warnings for non-variadic arguments.\\n  Before this patch they mapped to function stubs, the same codepath\\n  used for C89 compiler in this configuration.\\n\\n- introduce new macros to tell the compiler which code to include\\n  when verbose code is active, or inactive:\\n\\n  - `CURLVERBOSE`: defined when verbose code is active.\\n    To enclose blocks of code only used f... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: fully omit verbose strings and code when disabled\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/61093e2a819d26b7ddf309baef264b9e50c6c56f\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"814b54d\",\"date\":\"2026-01-16\",\"message\":\"tidy-up: miscellaneous\\n\\n- whitespace, indent, comments, clang-format.\\n- openssl: move feature guards within function blocks.\\n- tunit: drop redundant blocks.\\n\\nCloses #20361\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tidy-up: miscellaneous\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/814b54d83edbc808de0aa0d5bc7f264c816f6853\"},{\"author\":\"Frank Buss <fb@frank-buss.de>\",\"commit\":\"d7bbc78\",\"date\":\"2026-01-19\",\"message\":\"tls: add new SSLSUPP flags for several options\\n\\nSo that curl_easy_setopt() correctly returns error for those not\\nsupported by the backend.\\n\\nCloses #20364\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tls: add new SSLSUPP flags for several options\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/d7bbc78360152a448e47fc1d202ab0b6a315df6d\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"6aaac9d\",\"date\":\"2026-01-17\",\"message\":\"vquic: drop support for OpenSSL-QUIC\\n\\n- It is slower and uses more memory than the alternatives and is only\\n  experimental in curl.\\n- We disable a few tests for OpenSSL-QUIC because of flakiness\\n- It gets little attention from OpenSSL and we have no expectation of the\\n  major flaws getting corrected anytime soon.\\n- No one has spoken up for keeping it\\n- curl users building with vanilla OpenSSL can still use QUIC through the\\n  means of ngtcp2\\n\\nCloses #20226\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"vquic: drop support for OpenSSL-QUIC\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/6aaac9dd388a64d0f511544496608693e1105d13\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"85c841c\",\"date\":\"2026-01-01\",\"message\":\"msvc: drop exception, make `BIT()` a bitfield with Visual Studio\\n\\nAdd casts to `bool`, or use `bit` type in local variables, where\\nneccessary to avoid MSVC compiler warnings C4242.\\n\\nNote: There may remain places needing the above updates, where not\\ntested in CI, and missed in manual review.\\n\\nAlso:\\n- urldata: convert struct field `connect_only` to bitfield to match its\\n  counterpart in another struct.\\n- rename curl-specific `bit` type to `curl_bit`.\\n\\nCloses #20142\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"msvc: drop exception, make `BIT()` a bitfield with Visual Studio\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/85c841cb45e5fe99ed3420adfa9c556fb397aefb\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"57ff2d6\",\"date\":\"2025-12-28\",\"message\":\"dns: flatten the include tree\\n\\nCloses #20106\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"dns: flatten the include tree\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/57ff2d6c918d0bb444e5a8a53405217aec116b1b\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"59a5fd8\",\"date\":\"2026-01-14\",\"message\":\"build: drop redundant unused variable suppressions\\n\\nAlso:\\n- digest_sspi: merge some `ifdef`s.\\n\\nCloses #20310\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: drop redundant unused variable suppressions\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/59a5fd8518f6136494d04f694e16cd8879f03730\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"60f9d3d\",\"date\":\"2026-01-13\",\"message\":\"build: omit forward declarations\\n\\n- drop redundant forward declarations.\\n- reorder local functions to not need forward declarations.\\n- tftpd: merge two `ifdef` blocks.\\n\\nCloses #20297\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"build: omit forward declarations\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/60f9d3dd7b52ec6eed890e74856de767de6b6a34\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"7ad830b\",\"date\":\"2025-12-31\",\"message\":\"openssl: stop checking for `OPENSSL_NO_SHA*` macros\\n\\nMacros have been deleted upstream and never defined in OpenSSL 1.1.0+:\\nhttps://github.com/openssl/openssl/commit/474e469bbd056aebcf7e7d3207ef820f2faed4ce\\n\\nBoringSSL deleted the last internals uses in 2014:\\nhttps://github.com/google/boringssl/commit/457112e1973251a721ae419128d7108844d3c1a3\\n\\nLibreSSL refers to them internally and in two public headers, but never\\nset them via `openssl/opensslfeatures.h` / `openssl/opensslconf.h`.\\n\\nFollow-up to 69c89bf3d3137fcbb2b8bc57233182adcf1e2817 #18330\\n\\nCloses #20130\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"openssl: stop checking for `OPENSSL_NO_SHA*` macros\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/7ad830bb5812b0a246125c780f2723fefd9b66d8\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"0d0ac29\",\"date\":\"2025-12-31\",\"message\":\"openssl: stop checking for `OPENSSL_NO_TLSEXT` macro\\n\\nThe macro has been deleted upstream and never defined in OpenSSL 1.1.0+:\\nhttps://github.com/openssl/openssl/commit/e481f9b90b164fd1053015d1c4e0a0d92076d7a8\\n\\nBoringSSL and LibreSSL deleted the last uses in 2014:\\nhttps://github.com/google/boringssl/commit/6dbd73db5d58ec44304266012d23ff8d297eca55\\nhttps://github.com/libressl/openbsd/commit/7b2f3298f7eb7ce5cfd1c3eb55b1ecc89118f52c\\n\\nAlso:\\n- drop internal guard `HAS_ALPN_OPENSSL`. It's always set.\\n\\nFollow-up to 69c89bf3d3137fcbb2b8bc57233182adcf1e2817 #18330\\n\\nCloses #20129\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"openssl: stop checking for `OPENSSL_NO_TLSEXT` macro\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/0d0ac29cdd8b64781a3d771a7454acc0f0ddf005\"}],\"limitHit\":true,\"query\":\"type:commit file:lib/vtls/openssl.c repo:github.com/curl/curl after:2024-12-01 count:20\",\"totalCount\":20}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "commits": [
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "5fa5cb3",
        "date": "2026-02-13",
        "message": "build: fix `-Wunused-macros` warnings, and related tidy-ups\n\n- fix internal macro `AN_APPLE_OS` reused between sources without\n  resetting it. It may potentially have left the system sha256\n  function unused.\n- fix to define `WOLFSSL_OPTIONS_IGNORE_SYS` so that it always applies\n  to wolfSSL headers, also during feature detection.\n- md4, md5, sha256: simplify fallback logic.\n- delete 20+ unused macros.\n- scope or move macros to avoid `-Wunused-macros` warnings.\n- examples: delete unused code.\n\nThe warning detects macros defined but not used within the same C\nsource. It does not warn for macros... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: fix `-Wunused-macros` warnings, and related tidy-ups",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/5fa5cb382560316a55f0954f1e8cebdbd6568cfb"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "dc08922",
        "date": "2026-02-17",
        "message": "openssl: disable local keylog feature if built-in upstream\n\nReported-by: Paul Howarth\nFixes #20611\n\nCloses #20614",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "openssl: disable local keylog feature if built-in upstream",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/dc08922a61efe546b318daf964514ffbf4158325"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "61df5f4",
        "date": "2026-02-01",
        "message": "tidy-up: miscellaneous\n\n- curl_ntlm_core, smtp, schannel: fix comments.\n- curl_setup.h: fix to undef before define.\n- tool_doswin, server/sockfilt: reduce variables scopes.\n- tool_doswin: drop an interim variable.\n- windows: replace `SOCKET_ERROR` with `0` to align with rest of code.\n- libssh2: rename variable to align with rest of code.\n- gtls, unit1398: use `#if 0`.\n- curl_trc.h, curlx/inet_ntop.h: add missing parentheses in macro\n  expressions.\n- ldap.c: set empty macro to `do {} while(0)`.\n- examples/crawler: rename a non-CURLcode `result` variable.\n- CURLINFO_TLS_SESSION: drop stray colon... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tidy-up: miscellaneous",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/61df5f466ca9a3f6b731e7be65c6c955a63f9028"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "2a92c39",
        "date": "2026-02-11",
        "message": "windows: tidy up `wincrypt.h` / BoringSSL/AWS-LC coexist workaround\n\n- openssl: move and expand explanatory comment.\n\n- openssl: drop duplicate workaround.\n\n- schannel: drop workaround. Unnecessary, because OpenSSL headers are\n  not included in or after schannel code.\n\n- schannel: drop explicit `wincrypt.h` include. It's indirectly\n  included by system `<schannel.h>`.\n\n- ldap: drop explicit `wincrypt.h` include.\n  It isn't used there, and also not required for the workaround.\n  `winldap.h` keeps including it indirectly.\n\nTested with BoringSSL and AWS-LC (MultiSSL with Schannel), also LDAP\nenab... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "windows: tidy up `wincrypt.h` / BoringSSL/AWS-LC coexist workaround",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/2a92c39a218713635768364f801fa13831a019e0"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "85de995",
        "date": "2026-02-07",
        "message": "tidy-up: move literals to right-side of `if` expressions (where missing)\n\nCloses #20535",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tidy-up: move literals to right-side of `if` expressions (where missing)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/85de99520880e7627b867d56a04f992a40cc99b5"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "31a4f41",
        "date": "2026-02-02",
        "message": "build: tidy up and dedupe `strdup` functions\n\n- de-dupe lib/src strdup/memdup functions into curlx.\n- introduce `CURLX_STRDUP_LOW()` for mapping `strdup()`, and to do it at\n  one place within the code, in `curl_setup.h`.\n- tests/server: use `curlx_strdup()`. (Also to fix building without\n  a system `strdup()`.)\n- curlx/curlx.h: shorten and tidy up.\n- adjust Windows build path to not need `HAVE_STRDUP`.\n- build: stop detecting `HAVE_STRDUP` on Windows.\n\nCloses #20497",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: tidy up and dedupe `strdup` functions",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/31a4f415af112351ad54e59b2d6b03df4f4cb187"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "9c3fcb8",
        "date": "2026-01-29",
        "message": "vtls: dedupe common on-session-reuse logic\n\nCloses #20475",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "vtls: dedupe common on-session-reuse logic",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/9c3fcb8a321882232409f9b05b07098821378219"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "1b50dcc",
        "date": "2026-01-27",
        "message": "tls: remove checks for DEFAULT\n\nSince 9d8998c99408e1adf, the setopt code changes input DEFAULT to an\nactual more specific TLS version (1.2) for the backends to use and check\nfor.\n\nThis means that the default value (0L) cannot and should not actually be\nused when the TLS backends run. This change adds asserts to verify that\nand removes code that accepts the DEFAULT value as a valid version with\nthe TLS version functions' logic.\n\nApplications can still set a specific lower version if they want (1, 1.0\nor 1.1).\n\nCloses #20453",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tls: remove checks for DEFAULT",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/1b50dcc2abd40d60d16c7c9b2fcbfc093c0419d3"
      },
      {
        "author": "Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>",
        "commit": "065b149",
        "date": "2026-01-27",
        "message": "OpenSSL: check reuse of sessions for verify status\n\nOpenSSL records its peer verification status inside its SSL_SESSION\nobjects. When a session is later reused, the SSL connection inherits\nthis verify status.\n\nSession keys prevent reuse of sessions between connections that verify\nthe peer and those who do not. However, when Apple SecTrust is used\nto verify a connection, this does not update the Sessions verify\nstatus (and there is no setter). On session reuse, OpenSSL fails\nthe verification and Apple SecTrust cannot verify either since the\ncertificate peer chain is not available.\n\nFix this by ... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "OpenSSL: check reuse of sessions for verify status",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/065b149df07256174891dfcde307c39c98908d5f"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "b81341e",
        "date": "2026-01-20",
        "message": "tidy-up: Markdown, clang-format nits\n\n- drop leading indent from Markdown.\n- switch to Markdown section markers where missing.\n- move `&&` and `||` to the end of the line (C, Perl).\n- openssl: add parenthesis to an if sub-expression.\n- misc clang-format nits.\n- unfold Markdown links.\n- SSL-PROBLEMS.md: drop stray half code-fence.\n\nCloses #20402",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tidy-up: Markdown, clang-format nits",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/b81341e8f557a719ea9c9c28ffd4385ac3e36f64"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "61093e2",
        "date": "2026-01-19",
        "message": "build: fully omit verbose strings and code when disabled\n\nWhen the compiler supports C99.\n\n- map logging functions to macro stubs when verbose logging is disabled\n  and the compiler is C99. Make sure these stubs silence unused variable\n  warnings for non-variadic arguments.\n  Before this patch they mapped to function stubs, the same codepath\n  used for C89 compiler in this configuration.\n\n- introduce new macros to tell the compiler which code to include\n  when verbose code is active, or inactive:\n\n  - `CURLVERBOSE`: defined when verbose code is active.\n    To enclose blocks of code only used f... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: fully omit verbose strings and code when disabled",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/61093e2a819d26b7ddf309baef264b9e50c6c56f"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "814b54d",
        "date": "2026-01-16",
        "message": "tidy-up: miscellaneous\n\n- whitespace, indent, comments, clang-format.\n- openssl: move feature guards within function blocks.\n- tunit: drop redundant blocks.\n\nCloses #20361",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tidy-up: miscellaneous",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/814b54d83edbc808de0aa0d5bc7f264c816f6853"
      },
      {
        "author": "Frank Buss <fb@frank-buss.de>",
        "commit": "d7bbc78",
        "date": "2026-01-19",
        "message": "tls: add new SSLSUPP flags for several options\n\nSo that curl_easy_setopt() correctly returns error for those not\nsupported by the backend.\n\nCloses #20364",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "tls: add new SSLSUPP flags for several options",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/d7bbc78360152a448e47fc1d202ab0b6a315df6d"
      },
      {
        "author": "Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>",
        "commit": "6aaac9d",
        "date": "2026-01-17",
        "message": "vquic: drop support for OpenSSL-QUIC\n\n- It is slower and uses more memory than the alternatives and is only\n  experimental in curl.\n- We disable a few tests for OpenSSL-QUIC because of flakiness\n- It gets little attention from OpenSSL and we have no expectation of the\n  major flaws getting corrected anytime soon.\n- No one has spoken up for keeping it\n- curl users building with vanilla OpenSSL can still use QUIC through the\n  means of ngtcp2\n\nCloses #20226",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "vquic: drop support for OpenSSL-QUIC",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/6aaac9dd388a64d0f511544496608693e1105d13"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "85c841c",
        "date": "2026-01-01",
        "message": "msvc: drop exception, make `BIT()` a bitfield with Visual Studio\n\nAdd casts to `bool`, or use `bit` type in local variables, where\nneccessary to avoid MSVC compiler warnings C4242.\n\nNote: There may remain places needing the above updates, where not\ntested in CI, and missed in manual review.\n\nAlso:\n- urldata: convert struct field `connect_only` to bitfield to match its\n  counterpart in another struct.\n- rename curl-specific `bit` type to `curl_bit`.\n\nCloses #20142",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "msvc: drop exception, make `BIT()` a bitfield with Visual Studio",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/85c841cb45e5fe99ed3420adfa9c556fb397aefb"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "57ff2d6",
        "date": "2025-12-28",
        "message": "dns: flatten the include tree\n\nCloses #20106",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "dns: flatten the include tree",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/57ff2d6c918d0bb444e5a8a53405217aec116b1b"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "59a5fd8",
        "date": "2026-01-14",
        "message": "build: drop redundant unused variable suppressions\n\nAlso:\n- digest_sspi: merge some `ifdef`s.\n\nCloses #20310",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: drop redundant unused variable suppressions",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/59a5fd8518f6136494d04f694e16cd8879f03730"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "60f9d3d",
        "date": "2026-01-13",
        "message": "build: omit forward declarations\n\n- drop redundant forward declarations.\n- reorder local functions to not need forward declarations.\n- tftpd: merge two `ifdef` blocks.\n\nCloses #20297",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "build: omit forward declarations",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/60f9d3dd7b52ec6eed890e74856de767de6b6a34"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "7ad830b",
        "date": "2025-12-31",
        "message": "openssl: stop checking for `OPENSSL_NO_SHA*` macros\n\nMacros have been deleted upstream and never defined in OpenSSL 1.1.0+:\nhttps://github.com/openssl/openssl/commit/474e469bbd056aebcf7e7d3207ef820f2faed4ce\n\nBoringSSL deleted the last internals uses in 2014:\nhttps://github.com/google/boringssl/commit/457112e1973251a721ae419128d7108844d3c1a3\n\nLibreSSL refers to them internally and in two public headers, but never\nset them via `openssl/opensslfeatures.h` / `openssl/opensslconf.h`.\n\nFollow-up to 69c89bf3d3137fcbb2b8bc57233182adcf1e2817 #18330\n\nCloses #20130",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "openssl: stop checking for `OPENSSL_NO_SHA*` macros",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/7ad830bb5812b0a246125c780f2723fefd9b66d8"
      },
      {
        "author": "Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>",
        "commit": "0d0ac29",
        "date": "2025-12-31",
        "message": "openssl: stop checking for `OPENSSL_NO_TLSEXT` macro\n\nThe macro has been deleted upstream and never defined in OpenSSL 1.1.0+:\nhttps://github.com/openssl/openssl/commit/e481f9b90b164fd1053015d1c4e0a0d92076d7a8\n\nBoringSSL and LibreSSL deleted the last uses in 2014:\nhttps://github.com/google/boringssl/commit/6dbd73db5d58ec44304266012d23ff8d297eca55\nhttps://github.com/libressl/openbsd/commit/7b2f3298f7eb7ce5cfd1c3eb55b1ecc89118f52c\n\nAlso:\n- drop internal guard `HAS_ALPN_OPENSSL`. It's always set.\n\nFollow-up to 69c89bf3d3137fcbb2b8bc57233182adcf1e2817 #18330\n\nCloses #20129",
        "repository": "github.com/curl/curl",
        "title": "openssl: stop checking for `OPENSSL_NO_TLSEXT` macro",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/0d0ac29cdd8b64781a3d771a7454acc0f0ddf005"
      }
    ],
    "limitHit": true,
    "query": "type:commit file:lib/vtls/openssl.c repo:github.com/curl/curl after:2024-12-01 count:20",
    "totalCount": 20
  }
}

          {
            "body": "  * encoded data. Size of encoded data is returned in variable pointed by\n  * outlen.\n  *\n- * Input length of 0 indicates input buffer holds a null-terminated string.\n- *\n  * Returns CURLE_OK on success, otherwise specific error code.             "body": "                                      origin */\n {\n   /* This line was read off an HTTP-header */\n-  time_t now;\n+  time_t now = 0;\n   size_t linelength = strlen(ptr);\n-  CURLcode result;\n-  struct Curl_str cookie[COOKIE_PIECES] = { 0 };\n+  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n+  struct Curl_str cookie[COOKIE_PIECES];\n   *okay = FALSE;\n   if(linelength > MAX_COOKIE_LINE)\n     /* discard overly long lines at once */\n     return CURLE_OK;\n \n-  now = time(NULL);\n+  /* memset instead of initializer because gcc 4.8.1 is silly */\n+  memset(cookie, 0, sizeof(cookie));\n   do {\n     struct Curl_str name;\n     struct Curl_str val;\n \n     /* we have a <name>=<value> pair or a stand-alone word here */\n     if(!curlx_str_cspn(&ptr, &name, \";\\t\\r\\n=\")) {\n-      bool done = FALSE;\n       bool sep = FALSE;\n       curlx_str_trimblanks(&name);\n \n       if(!curlx_str_single(&ptr, '=')) {\n         sep = TRUE; /* a '=' was used */\n-        if(!curlx_str_cspn(&ptr, &val, \";\\r\\n\")) {\n+        if(!curlx_str_cspn(&ptr, &val, \";\\r\\n\"))\n           curlx_str_trimblanks(&val);\n-\n-          /* Reject cookies with a TAB inside the value */\n-          if(memchr(curlx_str(&val), '\\t', curlx_strlen(&val))) {\n-            infof(data, \"cookie contains TAB, dropping\");\n-            return CURLE_OK;\n-          }\n-        }\n       }\n-      else {\n+      else\n         curlx_str_init(&val);\n-      }\n-\n-      /*\n-       * Check for too long individual name or contents, or too long\n-       * combination of name + contents.             "body": "           return CURLE_OK;\n         }\n       }\n-      else if(curlx_str_casecompare(&name, \"version\")) {\n-        /* just ignore */\n-      }\n       else if(curlx_str_casecompare(&name, \"max-age\") && curlx_strlen(&val)) {\n         /*\n          * Defined in RFC2109:\n",

          },

            "body": "         if(*maxage == '\\\"')\n           maxage++;\n         rc = curlx_str_number(&maxage, &co->expires, CURL_OFF_T_MAX);\n+        if(!now)\n+          now = time(NULL);\n         switch(rc) {\n         case STRE_OVERFLOW:\n           /* overflow, used max value */\n",

            "body": "         }\n         cap_expires(now, co);\n       }\n-      else if(curlx_str_casecompare(&name, \"expires\") && curlx_strlen(&val)) {\n-        if(!co->expires && (curlx_strlen(&val) < MAX_DATE_LENGTH)) {\n-          /*\n-           * Let max-age have priority.\n-           *\n-           * If the date cannot get parsed for whatever reason, the cookie\n-           * will be treated as a session cookie\n-           */\n-          char dbuf[MAX_DATE_LENGTH + 1];\n-          time_t date = 0;\n-          memcpy(dbuf, curlx_str(&val), curlx_strlen(&val));\n-          dbuf[curlx_strlen(&val)] = 0;\n-          if(!Curl_getdate_capped(dbuf, &date)) {\n-            if(!date)\n-              date++;\n-            co->expires = (curl_off_t)date;\n-          }\n-          else\n-            co->expires = 0;\n-          cap_expires(now, co);\n+      else if(curlx_str_casecompare(&name, \"expires\") && curlx_strlen(&val) &&\n+              !co->expires && (curlx_strlen(&val) < MAX_DATE_LENGTH)) {\n+        /*\n+         * Let max-age have priority.\n+         *\n+         * If the date cannot get parsed for whatever reason, the cookie\n+         * will be treated as a session cookie\n+         */\n+        char dbuf[MAX_DATE_LENGTH + 1];\n+        time_t date = 0;\n+        memcpy(dbuf, curlx_str(&val), curlx_strlen(&val));\n+        dbuf[curlx_strlen(&val)] = 0;\n+        if(!Curl_getdate_capped(dbuf, &date)) {\n+          if(!date)\n+            date++;\n+          co->expires = (curl_off_t)date;\n         }\n+        else\n+ about!\n-       */\n     }\n+  } while(!curlx_str_single(&ptr, ';'));\n \n-    if(curlx_str_single(&ptr, ';'))\n-      break;\n-  } while(1);\n-\n-  if(!curlx_strlen(&cookie[COOKIE_NAME]))\n-    /* If we did not get a cookie name, or a bad one, bail out. */\n-    return CURLE_OK;\n-\n-  /* the header was fine, now store the data */\n-  result = storecookie(co, &cookie[0], path, domain);\n-  if(!result)\n-    *okay = TRUE;\n+  if(curlx_strlen(&cookie[COOKIE_NAME])) {\n+    /* the header was fine, now store the data */\n+    result = storecookie(co, &cookie[0], path, domain);\n+    if(!result)\n+      *okay = TRUE;\n+  }\n   return result;\n }\n \n",

            "body": " Set-Cookie: withspaces=  yes  within and around    ;%CR\n Set-Cookie: withspaces2 =before equals;%CR\n Set-Cookie: prespace=  yes before;%CR\n-Set-Cookie: securewithspace=after    ; secure =%CR\n+Set-Cookie: securewithspace=after    ; secure %CR\n Set-Cookie: %hex[%c3%82%c2%b3%c3%83%5c%78%39%32%c3%83%5c%78%39%61%c3%83%5c%78%38%64%c3%83%5c%78%39%37]hex%=%96%A6g%9Ay%B0%A5g%A7tm%7C%95%9A\n %CR\n boo\n",
